That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Durango School District 9-R, the 25-26 Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the 25-26 Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School at Durango School District 9-R is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobTitle: 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School
Job.JobDescription:
Durango High School is looking for an Ice Hockey coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School at Durango High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Durango High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The 25/26 Ice Hockey Specialist Coach - Durango High School maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Durango High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Riverside Middle School is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 8th Grade Head Football Coach at Riverside Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the 8th Grade Head Football Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Riverside Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The 8th Grade Head Football Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the 8th Grade Head Football Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the 8th Grade Head Football Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Riverside Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobTitle: Football Assistant Coach (multiple positions Jr and Sr High)
Job.JobDescription:
Woodland Park School Dist RE-2 is looking for a Football Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS: * Has the ability to organize, assist and supervise a high school football program. * Has previous successful coaching experience in coaching football. * The assistant coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of football and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. * The assistant coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions. * Adheres to the defined rules and regulations as set forth by CHSAA. SALARY: * Stipend Position - depended upon experience REPORTS TO: * The head coach, athletic director and/or HS Principal, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the administrative team. SUPERVISES: * In several instances, the assistant coach must advise, coordinate and support a staff of high school assistant coaches and middle school coaches in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal. JOB GOAL: * To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. * At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. * To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. GENERAL: * The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community’s image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override all other objectives for a successful program. * The position includes other unusual aspect such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments. * It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: * Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Woodland Park RE-2 Schools’ Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. * Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. * Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. STAFF RESPONSIBILITIES: * Established the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of the overall program. * Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. * Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season’s end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. * Maintains discipline, adjusts grievances and works to increase morale and cooperation. * Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal. ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES: * Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. * Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates programs with maintenance and school employees. * Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. * Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. * Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES: * Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. * Gives constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. * By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. * Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. * Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. * Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office within 24 hours. * Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. * Determines discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contracts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. * Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. FINANCE AND EQUIPMENT: * Participates in the budgeting function with the athletic director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. * Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning the same. * Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. * Monitors equipment rooms and coaches’ offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. * Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. * Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. * Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. * Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. PUBLIC RELATIONS: * Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. * Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, little league programs, or in any other feasible manner. * Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. * Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster club, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. * Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results.
Job.Experience: Has previous successful coaching experience in coaching football.
Job.CompanyDescription: Woodland Park School Dist RE-2 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Estes Park High School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
The High School Assistant Track Coach role at Estes Park High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the High School Assistant Track Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Estes Park High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The High School Assistant Track Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the High School Assistant Track Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The High School Assistant Track Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Estes Park High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the High School Assistant Track Coach at Estes Park High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.
Job.Experience: Experience in coaching preferred but not required
Job.CompanyDescription: Estes Park School District R-3 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Trinidad High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Volleyball Coach at Trinidad High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Head Volleyball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Trinidad High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Head Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Head Volleyball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Trinidad High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Trinidad High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Head Volleyball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Head Volleyball Coach role at Trinidad High School.

Ignacio District 11-JT is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The MS Boys Basketball Asst. Coach at Ignacio District 11-JT is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the MS Boys Basketball Asst. Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Ignacio District 11-JT, the MS Boys Basketball Asst. Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The MS Boys Basketball Asst. Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Ignacio District 11-JT well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The MS Boys Basketball Asst. Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Position Summary
The Coach is responsible for developing and leading a competitive athletic program that emphasizes teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth. Job.JobDescription:
Position Summary The Coach is responsible for developing and leading a competitive athletic program that emphasizes teamwork, sportsmanship, and personal growth. The coach will instruct student-athletes in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical conditioning required to succeed in their sport while promoting academic achievement and positive character development.
Key Responsibilities Plan, organize, and conduct regular practices and training sessions. Teach fundamental skills, techniques, and strategies appropriate to the sport. Instill positive values such as teamwork, respect, discipline, and perseverance. Manage all aspects of game preparation, including strategy, player development, and scouting (as appropriate). Ensure the safety and well-being of all student-athletes by following proper supervision and risk management practices. Promote academic success by supporting eligibility requirements and encouraging student-athletes to balance athletics and academics. Communicate regularly with athletic directors, school administrators, parents, and athletes regarding schedules, expectations, and program goals. Oversee equipment, uniforms, and facility use, ensuring proper care and accountability. Adhere to all district, state, and governing athletic association rules and regulations.
Qualifications High school diploma or equivalent required Previous coaching and/or playing experience in the sport preferred. Knowledge of rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport. Strong leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to serve as a positive role model for student-athletes. First Aid/CPR certification (or ability to obtain). Must meet all district and state certification requirements for coaches.
Working Conditions Evening and weekend practices/games may be required. Travel for competitions and tournaments. Physical ability to demonstrate skills and participate in training activities.

Job.JobDescription:
Montgomery County is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Cheerleader Coach position at Montgomery Co Central Office is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Head Cheerleader Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Montgomery Co Central Office, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Head Cheerleader Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Head Cheerleader Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Head Cheerleader Coach role at Montgomery Co Central Office is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Park Crossing High School is looking for a Swimming and Diving Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Swim Coach at Park Crossing High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Swim Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Park Crossing High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Swim Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Swim Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Park Crossing High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Park Crossing High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Swim Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Swim Coach role at Park Crossing High School.

The Volleyball Head Coach - Williams Middle at Huntsville City Schools is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Volleyball Head Coach - Williams Middle manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Huntsville City Schools, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Volleyball Head Coach - Williams Middle demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Volleyball Head Coach - Williams Middle follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Volleyball Head Coach - Williams Middle at Huntsville City Schools allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobTitle: Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD
Job.JobDescription:
Elkmont High School is looking for a Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD position at Elkmont High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Elkmont High School, the Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Head Coach - Elkmont High School Location TBD at Elkmont High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Harding HS is looking for a Soccer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) position at Harding HS is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) at Harding HS sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Harding HS, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the ASSISTANT COACH - GIRLS SOCCER (Gr. 9-12) position at Harding HS is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Tennis Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) role at Bridgeport School Dist. is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Bridgeport School Dist. are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Bridgeport School Dist., the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the GIRLS ASSISTANT TENNIS COACH (VOLUNTEER) at Bridgeport School Dist., a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Volleyball Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position is responsible for assisting with the coaching of student athletes in game strategies and techniques to prepare them for athletic competition. Position motivates student athletes to develop an appreciation of the sport.
RESPONSIBILITIES: Assists with organizational meetings for team prospects and encourages potential athletes to participate in the sport. Assesses players' skills and assigns team positions. Assists with developing a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both individual and team development. Works with the head coach and/or athletic director in scheduling facilities for practices and competition. Assists coaching and instructing players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. Observes players, during competition and practice to determine the needs for individual or team improvement. Determines game strategy based on the team's capabilities. Establishes and maintains standards of behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times. Monitors the academic performance of team members to ensure that eligibility requirements are met. Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding the athletic performance of their children. Follows state, regional, and district regulations governing the athletic program. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. Acts as a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with the news media, booster clubs, service clubs, and other organizations. Follows established procedures for the proper care, maintenance, & requisitioning of equipment, supplies, and uniforms. Works with the head coach and/or athletic director to develop a policy for awards and submits a list of award winners at the end of the season. Participates in special activities to include parents' night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. Interacts in a positive manner with staff, students and parents Promotes good public relations by personal appearance, attitude and conversation
KNOWLEDGE/SKILLS: Experience working with teenaged athletes Knowledge and previous experience coaching Ability to communicate ideas and directives clearly and effectively, both orally and in writing Effective, active listening skills Ability to work effectively with others Organizational and problem-solving skills Basic knowledge of all other subject matter Basic computer skills Excellent reading comprehension Excellent math comprehension
QUALIFICATIONS: High school diploma or equivalent CT SDE coaching certification or Connecticut Teaching Certificate First Aid Certification CPR Certification Concussion Management Training Experience at high school or college level preferred; combination of head coach, assistant coach, player and/or education may be substituted Three years' high school coaching experience or a higher level preferred Must possess effective coaching techniques and skills Must possess knowledge of rules, regulations, strategies and techniques of the sport. Bilingual in Spanish, preferred
APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS: Letter of Intent Resume Photocopy of coaching permit or application for coaching permit Photocopy of valid First Aid card – SIGNED, front and back Photocopy of valid CPR card – SIGNED, front and back Certificate verifying concussion management training (Original Certificate will be required from candidates selected for interview)
Stipend is in accordance with the BEA Collective Bargaining Agreement. This job description is not, nor is it intended to be, a complete statement of all duties, functions, responsibilities and qualifications which comprise this position.
It is the policy of the Bridgeport Board of Education not to discriminate on the basis of race, color, sex, sexual orientation, marital status, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, or any other basis prohibited by law, in its educational programs, activities, and employment practices.

Job.Experience: Three years' high school coaching experience or a higher level preferred

Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Soccer coach to join our program. Apply now.
The GIRLS SOCCER COACH at Bridgeport School Dist. is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the GIRLS SOCCER COACH plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Bridgeport School Dist. reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The GIRLS SOCCER COACH expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the GIRLS SOCCER COACH supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the GIRLS SOCCER COACH may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Bridgeport School Dist.. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

New Haven School Dist. is looking for a Lacrosse Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Type: Athletics/Athletic Coach
Date Posted: 10/15/2025
Location: Athletics Department
Job Title: Athletic Coach
Location: New Haven Public Schools
Certification Requirements: Must possess and maintain the certification requirements as established by Connecticut State Statutes. State of CT Coaching Permit (Module 15) Concussion Management/CPR and First Aid
New Haven Public Schools Vision: We believe that all kids can learn, achieve, and rise to a bright future. Our purpose is to provide an outstanding education that extends beyond graduation and prepares our students to be the next generation of leaders, innovators and problem-solvers.
Engagement is the foundation of learning and growth, for students, adults, and the school district as a whole. We will be successful when all students actively engage in their own learning, when adults engage in their professional community, and when the school district is consistently learning and growing to respond to the needs of children and schools. We must all engage and take collective responsibility for ensuring student success and wellbeing so that every child has a chance to rise.
To create this engagement, our vision is to build a portfolio of great schools that empower students to achieve success in college, career and life through purposeful, supportive and meaningful learning experiences. In crafting those learning experiences, we must strive to engage the whole child – the academic learning, the social-emotional growth, and health that enable students and schools to rise.
As we work, we hold tight to the values of collaboration, growth and innovation, and equity.
An Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Employer, M/F/V/D

Job.JobTitle: Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Stamford School Dist. is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach at Stamford School Dist. is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Stamford School Dist., coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Westhill High School: Assistant Baseball Coach at Stamford School Dist. allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.SalaryRange: Assistant Baseball Coach: $4,699 - $5,760 based upon experience

Job.JobTitle: Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Stamford High School is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach at Stamford High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Stamford High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Stamford High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Stamford High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Stamford High School: Assistant Softball Coach role at Stamford High School.

Job.JobTitle: Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions)
Job.JobDescription:
Stamford School Dist. is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions) at Stamford School Dist. is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions) plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Stamford School Dist. reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions) expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions) supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Lacrosse Coach (2 positions) may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Stamford School Dist.. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobTitle: Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Stamford School Dist. is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach role at Stamford School Dist. is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Stamford School Dist., teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Stamford School Dist., the Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Westhill High School: Assistant Boys Lacrosse Coach at Stamford School Dist. is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobTitle: Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions)
Job.JobDescription:
Stamford School Dist. is looking for a Basketball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) role at Stamford School Dist. is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Stamford School Dist. are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Stamford School Dist., the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Westhill High School: Assistant Girls Basketball Coach (2 positions) at Stamford School Dist., a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Danbury High School is looking for a Soccer Athletics Coaches/Head Coach - Girls Soccer to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach - Girls Soccer at Danbury High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Head Coach - Girls Soccer are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Danbury High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Coach - Girls Soccer recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Coach - Girls Soccer collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Coach - Girls Soccer maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Danbury High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Danbury High School is looking for a Swimming and Diving Athletics Coaches/Head Coach - Girls Swimming to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach - Swimming - Girls role at Danbury High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Head Coach - Swimming - Girls coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Danbury High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.

The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Head Coach - Swimming - Girls monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Head Coach - Swimming - Girls must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Danbury High School.

Conard High School is looking for a Tennis Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
West Hartford Public Schools seeks to fill the JV Boys Tennis Coach position at Conard High School for the spring season.
Qualifications: 1. Valid Connecticut coaching certification or temporary emergency coaching permit. 2. Updated first aid/CPR training approved by Connecticut State Department of Education. 3. Has knowledge and background in assigned sport. Report to: The head coach, in conjunction with the athletic director.
Primary Function: To carry out the aims and objectives of the sport program as outlined by the head coach. To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success while at the same time helping the athlete to achieve an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship and an increased level of self-esteem.
Major Duties and Responsibilities: 1. Assist the head coach in carrying out his/her responsibilities. 2. Is accountable for and assists the head coach in the issuing, collection, and storage of all equipment. 3. Is responsible for knowing the existing state and league regulations and at all times abides by them. 4. Assist the head coach and athletic director in scheduling contests and practices. 5. Provides proper safeguards for maintaining and protection of assigned equipment sites. 6. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events and practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. 7. Makes recommendations to head coach for awards. 8. Recommends to the head coach budgetary items for next year in his/her area of the program. 9. Assist the head coach in explaining the team's training rules, "Code of Conduct", as stated in the student-athlete handbook, CIAC eligibility rules, and procedures for participation in athletics to all members of the team. 10. Has thorough knowledge and implements the policies and procedures in the student-athlete handbook. 11. Secure, maintain cleanliness and monitor athletes in all athletic work areas. 12. Understand the proper administrative line of command and refer all student and parent requests or grievances through proper channels. 13. Never criticizes, admonishes or argues with head coach or any staff member in the presence of players or parents. 14. Is responsible for the actions and conduct of the athletes whenever they are under his/her jurisdiction. 15. Is responsible for the general health and welfare of students in his/her sport and gives appropriate attention to athletes who are injured, ill or otherwise incapacitated. 16. Attends all staff meetings and carries out scouting assignments as outlined by the head coach. 17. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and use. 18. Assist the head coach in maintaining necessary paperwork, i.e. attendance records, inventory of uniforms and equipment, parent permission forms, etc...

Job.JobDescription:
Edison Middle School is looking for a Baseball coach to join our program. Apply now.
DESCRIPTION: Assist each participating student to achieve his/her highest level of skill in a particular sport, gain appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and develop an increased level of self-esteem.
QUALIFICATIONS: Applicants must meet the CT State Department of Education's athletic coach certification requirements, which include Standard First Aid with CPR. Applicants should be thoroughly acquainted with the sport and be able to teach the sport's fundamental skills. Preference will be given to applicants who have previous experience in coaching and baseball.
SALARY: Per 2025-2026 Salary Schedule for Middle School Athletic Coaches
APPLICATION PROCEDURE: Apply online
WE ARE AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER AND DO NOT DISCRIMINATE ON THE BASIS OF RACE, RELIGION, AGE, SEX, DISABILITY, NATIONAL ORIGIN, MARITAL, GENDER IDENTITY OR EXPRESSION, VETERAN OR OTHER PROTECTED CLASS STATUS UNDER APPLICABLE LAW IN ANY OF OUR EDUCATION PROGRAMS, ACTIVITIES OR EMPLOYMENT POLICIES.
