Job.JobDescription:
Lincoln Middle School is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH position at Lincoln Middle School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Lincoln Middle School, the MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH at Lincoln Middle School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Experience: Preference will be given to applicants who have previous experience in coaching and softball.

Job.JobDescription:
Platt High School is looking for a Track & Field Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The HEAD COACH GIRLS TRACK role at Platt High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the HEAD COACH GIRLS TRACK is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Platt High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The HEAD COACH GIRLS TRACK sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Platt High School, the HEAD COACH GIRLS TRACK is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the HEAD COACH GIRLS TRACK at Platt High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.SalaryRange: Per 2025-2026 Salary Schedule for Athletic Coaches

Job.JobDescription:
Platt High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL role at Platt High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Platt High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Platt High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the HEAD COACH BOYS VOLLEYBALL at Platt High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.
Job.SalaryRange: Per 2025-2026 Salary Schedule for Athletic Coaches

Job.JobDescription:
Meriden School District is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK at Meriden Public Schools is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Meriden Public Schools, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Meriden Public Schools operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Meriden Public Schools with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the ASSISTANT COACH BOYS TRACK role at Meriden Public Schools.
Job.SalaryRange: Per 2025-2026 Salary Schedule for Athletic Coaches

Job.JobDescription:
Lincoln Middle School is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH position at Lincoln Middle School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH at Lincoln Middle School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Lincoln Middle School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the MIDDLE SCHOOL SOFTBALL COACH position at Lincoln Middle School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Bristol Central High School is looking for a Volleyball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION: Girls Volleyball Head Coach -Bristol Central High School POSITION TITLE: Head Coach REPORTS TO: BUILDING PRINCIPAL / SUPERVISOR OF ATHLETICS SUMMARY: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success while at the same time helping the athlete to achieve an appreciation for the values of discipline, sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Other duties may be assigned. Has full responsibility for the overall supervision of their program, including the junior varsity and freshman teams. Is in charge of assignments and duties of assistant coaches working in their sport. Is responsible for the general upkeep, protection, and inventory of the equipment and uniforms used in their sport including the junior varsity and freshman teams. Is responsible for assuring that assistant coaches and themselves know the existing state and league regulations and abide by them at all times. Assist the athletic coordinator in providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Attend all league meetings regarding scheduling and rule updates specific to their sport. Responsible for developing their schedules for all levels of play. Communicates all schedule changes to the Director of Athletics or athletic coordinator. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment storage areas. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices, and adheres to scheduled facility times. Makes recommendations for awards and is responsible for clarification of the award policy to athletes. Participates in the budgeting process with the Director of Athletics by establishing requirements for the next season. Is responsible for explaining equipment guidelines as to type, color, style, or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within the established budget. Has a thorough knowledge of policies and procedures in the student-athlete handbook and CIAC handbook. Is responsible for explaining and reinforcing rules of training and code of conduct in the student-athlete handbook along with the CIAC eligibility rules to all members of the team. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by the staff. Follows and has a good understanding of the basic NCAA recruiting rules for their specific sport. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' office, i.e.: permits athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Never leave athletes unsupervised during scheduled practices. Examines locker rooms, equipment rooms, and playing safe areas before and after team use checking general cleanliness and safety. Secures all doors, lights, windows, gates, locks, and alarm when leaving the building or outside facility when custodians are not on duty. Will evaluate assistant coaches and programs and submit necessary postseason reports to the Director of Athletics at the conclusion of the season. Is responsible for the actions and conduct of their team whenever they are under their jurisdiction. Is responsible for the general health and welfare of student-athletes in their sport. Communicates instructions and commands using clear, simple, and positive language. Pays appropriate attention to athletes who are injured or ill or otherwise incapacitated. Is responsible for following the protocol set by the athletic department in regards to injured athletes. Organizes pre-season meetings for players and coaches. Is in charge of evaluating performance of student-athletes and providing suitable feedback, balancing critique with positive and motivating comments. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with media, parents, officials, opponents, and fans. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care, and use. Directs student managers, assistants, and statisticians. Maintains current Connecticut coaching certification with the State of Connecticut Department of Education. Performs other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the Director of Athletics and/or principal. NOTE: Coaches must meet certification requirements set by the State of Connecticut. ****Coaching Certificate Required.**** QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: Valid Connecticut coaching certification or temporary emergency coaching permit. Updated CPR/First Aid training approved by Connecticut State Department of Education. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. Has substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport. EXPECTATIONS: Staff assigned to these positions is expected to perform their duties with the following expectations in mind: All supervisory responsibilities must be fulfilled. In particular, students must be supervised at all times. Ability to read, interpret directions, write reports and communicate effectively with students, parents and other staff. Ability to calculate figures and perform any math functions associated with the position and provides budget input when requested. Ability to apply common sense and deal with problems in a practical and reasonable manner. Ability to apply basic computer knowledge to the activity at hand. Ability to perform duties with all district and Board of Education guidelines and policies in mind. Knowledge of and/or experience in the activity. Ability to meet the physical requirements of the position that may vary widely depending on the activity. START DATE: As soon as possible SALARY: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule. APPLICATION: Internal applicants can fill out an internal application by November 25, 2025. All others fill out an online application at www.applitrack.com/bristol/onlineapp.
Job.SalaryRange: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule.

START DATE: AS SOON AS POSSIBLE SALARY: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule. APPLICATION: If you are currently employed by the Bristol Board of Education, please complete an internal application online by 4:00pm December 11, 2025.

Cape Henlopen S.D. is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
GENERAL INFORMATION: Mariner Middle School Boys Lacrosse Assistant Coach
General Description: The Assistant Coach is responsible for supporting the head coach to ensure that the head coach can lead the team, organize and implement game plans, and foster a positive and competitive environment. The ideal candidate will work collaboratively with the head coach and demonstrate an understanding of the sport.
Minimum Qualification A minimum of a high school diploma or GED is required. Advanced education above high school is preferred.
Essential Responsibilities 1. Team Leadership and Development: * Develop and implement training programs to enhance player performance. * Provide mentorship and support to players, fostering their physical, mental, and emotional development. * Evaluate player performance and provide constructive feedback. 2. Game Strategy and Planning: * Analyze opponents and develop game plans and strategies to maximize team performance. * Make real-time decisions during games to adapt strategies as needed. * Lead meetings/practices when asked by the head coach to review performance and plan future games. 3. Team Management: * Collaborate with the head coach and other staff to ensure cohesive team operations. * Assist with the selection of players * Ensure all players adhere to team rules, policies, and standards. 4. Communication and Collaboration: * Maintain open lines of communication with players, coaching staff, and families. * Foster a positive and inclusive team culture. 5. Performance Monitoring: * Track and analyze team and player performance metrics. * Implement changes and improvements based on performance statistics. 6. Administrative Duties: * Assist in managing the team’s schedule, including practices, games, and travel. * Ensure compliance with DIAA rules and regulations.
Professional Responsibilities * Able to establish and maintain collaborative working relationships. * Willingness to accept guidance and suggestions. * Able to communicate effectively both orally and in writing. * Able to work independently and exercise initiative. * Able to maintain confidentiality. * Continue professional growth and development through opportunities provided by CHSD and DIAA. * Able to work with other cultures represented in the community.
Competencies: Success in this position requires the following knowledge, skills, and abilities 1. Knowledge * Must have knowledge of DIAA rules and regulations. * Must complete training regarding Title IX and Athletics. * Must complete Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and Concussion Training every two years. * Must be familiar with Cape Henlopen School District’s Policy 118 (Prohibited Actions of Employees) & Policy 551 (Electronic Communication & Social Media Policy). * Equity initiatives & resources that promote a culture of diversity and inclusion embracing differences as a strategic opportunity towards leading the District’s efforts to build a culture of equality and embodiment for all students, families, employees and the community. * Must be aware of relevant policies and procedures mandated by the State of DE for coaches and employees such as Criminal Background Checks, Child Protection Registry Forms & TB Tests. * Must have experience and a detailed knowledge of online systems such as Recruiting and Hiring and Google Docs. * Must possess online, digital skills to maintain records and to complete evaluations of coaching staff. 2. Skills * Active listening –Giving full attention to what others are saying and taking the time to understand what is being said. * Social perceptiveness – Being aware of others’ reactions and understanding why they react as they do. * Coordination & Flexibility – Adjusting actions in relation to others actions, and an openness to accepting new assignments and duties. * Communicating – Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person, and communicating with the public. * Planning and organization - Coordinating schedules in a timely and efficient manner. 3. Abilities * Interpersonal Relationships – Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with students and staff, and maintaining them over time. * Oral comprehension – Able to listen and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.
The foregoing statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this job and are not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and competencies that may be required.

Broward County Public Schools is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) at Broward County Public Schools is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Broward County Public Schools, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Broward County Public Schools operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Broward County Public Schools with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the SUPPLEMENTAL ONLY- BASEBALL (ASSISTANT COACH) role at Broward County Public Schools.

This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant Coach for Girls Soccer prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Choctawhatchee High School, the Assistant Coach for Girls Soccer works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant Coach for Girls Soccer encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant Coach for Girls Soccer at Choctawhatchee High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.CompanyDescription: Okaloosa County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Fort Walton Beach High School is looking for an Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The High School Boy's Assistant Coach position at Fort Walton Beach High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the High School Boy's Assistant Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The High School Boy's Assistant Coach at Fort Walton Beach High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Fort Walton Beach High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The High School Boy's Assistant Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The High School Boy's Assistant Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the High School Boy's Assistant Coach position at Fort Walton Beach High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.CompanyDescription: Okaloosa County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Bruner Middle School is looking for a Soccer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Soccer Coach at Bruner Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Boys Soccer Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Bruner Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Boys Soccer Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Boys Soccer Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Boys Soccer Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Bruner Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobDescription:
Fort Walton Beach High School is looking for a Soccer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girl's assistant soccer coach at Fort Walton Beach High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Girl's assistant soccer coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Fort Walton Beach High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Girl's assistant soccer coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Girl's assistant soccer coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Girl's assistant soccer coach at Fort Walton Beach High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobDescription:
Niceville High School is looking for a Wrestling Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Wrestling Coach at Niceville High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Boys Wrestling Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Niceville High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Boys Wrestling Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Boys Wrestling Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Boys Wrestling Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Niceville High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.CompanyDescription: Okaloosa County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Indian River County School District is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
SDIRC Steps to Employment as an Athletic Coach
Apply for Athletic Coaching Certification at www.fldoe.org/edcert/apply.asp. Apply for employment on the school district's website: www.indianriverschools.org/employment. Wait to be contacted by Human Resources to schedule orientation session. This only occurs once an offer of employment is extended and accepted. Orientation includes the following fees: Fingerprinting - $82.00 Workplace screening - $31.00 TOTAL: $113.00, made payable to SDIRC by check or money order only
Once all requirements are met, the coach's name will be submitted to the School Board for approval. The coach may not work with students until the approval process is completed.
Athletic Coaching Certification: All coaches who do not hold a valid Florida Educator's Certificate must hold either a three-year or five-year Florida Athletic Coaching Certificate.
Three-year Athletic Coaching Certificate: Only one three-year, nonrenewable certificate covering Athletic Coaching may be issued to an individual who: Submits an application and processing fee, which is currently $75.00 (www.fldoe.org/edcert/apply.asp ) Obtains employment and fingerprint results processed by FDLE and FBI. Fingerprints are processed after an offer of employment is made and accepted.
Five-year Athletic Coaching Certificate: A certificate valid for five years covering Athletic Coaching may be issued to an individual who: Submits an application and processing fee, which is currently $75.00 (www.fldoe.org/edcert/apply.asp ) Satisfies specialization for the Athletic Coaching Endorsement (Rule 6A-4.0282) which includes the following: 1. A bachelor's or higher degree from an accredited institution (official transcript required) 2. A valid cardiopulmonary resuscitation (CPR) certificate issued by the American Heart Association or the American Red Cross 3. Completion of a sports first aid course 4. Completion of a sports-specific course 5. Completion of a professional education course 6. Completion of a safety course. 7. Fingerprints are processed and retained by the Florida Department of Law Enforcement (FDLE) and the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) For more information regarding the Florida Athletic Coaching Certification, please visit: www.fldoe.org/edcert/acc.asp
Job.EmployerFullName: School District of Indian River County
Job.CompanyDescription: Indian River County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Indian River County School District is looking for a Baseball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coaching
Date Posted: 9/24/2025
Location: Storm Grove Middle
SDIRC Steps to Employment as an Athletic Coach
Submit your application for an Athletic Coaching Certification with the Florida Department of Education. Apply to become an Athletic Coach through the district's website. Wait to be contacted by Human Resources to schedule your orientation appointment. This only occurs once an offer of employment is extended and accepted. Orientation includes the following fee: Fingerprinting/Workplace screening fee: $118.00 Payable by credit or debit card only at time of service using Visa, Mastercard or American Express. Discover is not accepted. Please note: You may not begin employment until the orientation is complete and Human Resources has given final clearance.
Athletic Coaching Certification All coaches who do not hold a valid Florida Educator's Certificate are required to obtain either a Three-Year or Five-Year Florida Athletic Coaching Certificate.
Three-Year Athletic Coaching Certificate A one-time, nonrenewable three-year certificate in Athletic Coaching may be issued to an individual who: Submits an application and the current processing fee of $75.00 with the Florida Department of Education. Has been approved to volunteer and completes fingerprinting through the FDLE and FBI after being accepted for a coaching role.
Five-year Athletic Coaching Certificate A five-year certificate in Athletic Coaching may be issued to an individual who: Submits an application and the current processing fee of $75.00 with the Florida Department of Education. Meets the specialization requirements for the Athletic Coaching Endorsement (Rule 6A-4.0282), which include: o Three (3) semester hours in Care and Prevention of Athletic Injuries and the effects and dangers of drug use, including performance-enhancing drugs. o Three (3) semester hours in coaching theory. o Three (3) semester hours in theory and practice of coaching a specific sport. o A valid CPR certificate from the American Heart Association or American Red Cross. o First Aid certification, including training in the use of an Automated External Defibrillator (AED). Has been approved for employment and completed fingerprinting (FDLE/FBI), with results no older than one year.
Coursework Options The course requirements may be met through college coursework or the Human Kinetics Athletic Coaching Endorsement program, which is approved under the School District of Indian River County's Master Inservice Plan.
For additional information about coursework or certifications: Certification: (772) 564-3116 Professional Learning: (772) 564-3025 Please visit the Agency for Heath Care Administration for education and awareness on background screening.

Job.JobDescription:
ATLANTIC COAST HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School role at ATLANTIC COAST HIGH SCHOOL emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At ATLANTIC COAST HIGH SCHOOL, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent ATLANTIC COAST HIGH SCHOOL at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Athletic Coach - Varsity Head Beach Volleyball - Atlantic Coast High School must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at ATLANTIC COAST HIGH SCHOOL.

Job.JobTitle: Athletic Coach -Assistant Boys Lacrosse - Mandarin High School
Job.JobDescription:
MANDARIN HIGH is looking for an Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Coach -Assistant Boys Lacrosse - Mandarin High School position at MANDARIN HIGH is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Athletic Coach -Assistant Boys Lacrosse - Mandarin High School is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At MANDARIN HIGH, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Athletic Coach -Assistant Boys Lacrosse - Mandarin High School encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Job.CompanyDescription: Lee County PUblic Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Riverdale High is looking for a Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) at Riverdale High is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Riverdale High, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Riverdale High operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Riverdale High with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Football Assistant Coach, (Spring) role at Riverdale High.

Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Flag Football Assistant Coach role at South Fort Myers High is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.CompanyDescription: Lee County PUblic Schools coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
J W MITCHELL HIGH is looking for a Flag Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Coach- Flag Football position at J W MITCHELL HIGH provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant Coach- Flag Football prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At J W MITCHELL HIGH, the Assistant Coach- Flag Football works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant Coach- Flag Football encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant Coach- Flag Football at J W MITCHELL HIGH is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at CYPRESS CREEK HIGH.

Job.JobTitle: Horizon Area School_ Assistant Coach, Golf (Lithonia HS) 2025-2026
Job.JobDescription:
Lithonia High School is looking for a Golf Athletics/Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Lithonia High School is seeking a motivated Assistant Golf Coach, who has a passion for working with high school students. The ideal candidate should have excellent instructional delivery skills and the ability to communicate with various stakeholders.
The material contained herein is subject to change. All open positions offered by the DeKalb County School District are subject to specific job requirements. The DeKalb County School District is an equal opportunity employer, and all hiring decisions are subject to federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations.
Title: Assistant Coach
The successful candidate will work under the supervision of the Principal and the Head Coach in the sport that you express an interest. Candidate must be willing to assist the Head Coach in game techniques and strategies to prepare them for competition, and determine the need for individual or team improvement; candidate must be willing to accept directions from the Head Coach at all times. A Lay Coach will be required to assist with maintaining inventory of athletic equipment, maintain up to date knowledge of first aid techniques, and maintain locker room and playing facilities.
Community Coaches are temporary positions and must be renewed annually.
EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE: • High School Diploma or GED equivalent required. • Experience and knowledge coaching sports preferred.
CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, PERMITS: • Georgia High School Association Lay Coaching Certificate required. • Valid certificate in first aid required.

A Lay Coach will be required to assist with maintaining inventory of athletic equipment, maintain up to date knowledge of first aid techniques, and maintain locker room and playing facilities.
Community Coaches are temporary positions and must be renewed annually.

Job.JobDescription:
Mountain Vista High School is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Coach - Coed Track - HS position at Mountain Vista High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Assistant Coach - Coed Track - HS prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Mountain Vista High School, the Assistant Coach - Coed Track - HS works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Assistant Coach - Coed Track - HS encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Assistant Coach - Coed Track - HS at Mountain Vista High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Job.JobTitle: Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School
Job.JobDescription:
Wellington Middle/High School is looking for a Golf Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School at Wellington Middle/High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Wellington Middle/High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Coach-Head Boys Middle School Golf-Wellington Middle/High School at Wellington Middle/High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobTitle: Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School
Job.JobDescription:
Rocky Mountain High School is looking for a Swimming and Diving Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School position at Rocky Mountain High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School at Rocky Mountain High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Rocky Mountain High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Coach-Head Boys High School Swimming-Rocky Mountain High School position at Rocky Mountain High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Centennial Middle is looking for a Wrestling Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach-Wrestling-Coed at Centennial Middle is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Head Coach-Wrestling-Coed are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Centennial Middle use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Coach-Wrestling-Coed recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Coach-Wrestling-Coed collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Coach-Wrestling-Coed maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Centennial Middle. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobDescription:
Loveland High School is looking for a Golf Full time to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Girls Golf Coach role at Loveland High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Head Girls Golf Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Loveland High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Head Girls Golf Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Loveland High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Head Girls Golf Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Head Girls Golf Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Head Girls Golf Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Loveland High School.
