Job.JobTitle: 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School
Job.JobDescription:
Escalante Middle School is looking for a Wrestling Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School at Escalante Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Escalante Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Escalante Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Escalante Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the 25/26 Head Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School role at Escalante Middle School.

Ortega Middle School is looking for a Volleyball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS: Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. (Major sports) The assistant coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. The assistant coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her instructions and actions. REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who provides overall objectives and final evaluation in conjunction with the high school principals SUPERVISES: In several instances, the head coach must advise, coordinate and support a staff of high school assistant coaches and middle school coaches in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal. JOB GOAL: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. To upgrade his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation, etc. GENERAL: The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not override the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. The position includes other unusual aspect such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments. It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Plainwell Community Schools' Board of Education and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. Has knowledge of existing system, state and league regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. STAFF RESPONSIBILITIES: Established the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local clinic policy. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. Maintains discipline, adjusts grievances and works to increase morale and cooperation. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director/principal. ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES: Assists the athletic director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. STUDENT RESPONSIBILITIES: Serves as a mentor to each athlete to promote the development and demonstration in student athletes the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. By his/her presence at all practices games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant. Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered a participant. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office within 24 hours. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. Determines discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contracts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection. FINANCE AND EQUIPMENT: Participates in the budgeting function with the athletic director by establishing requirements for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning same. Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Examines locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. PUBLIC RELATIONS: Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program and promotes the sport outside the school through news media, little league programs, or in any other feasible manner. Responsible for the quality, effectiveness and validity of any oral or written release to local media. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with news media, booster club, parents, officials, volunteers and fans. Presents information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and results.

Dolores School District Re-4a is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The HS Boys Head Baseball Coach at Travis Rantz is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the HS Boys Head Baseball Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Travis Rantz use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The HS Boys Head Baseball Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the HS Boys Head Baseball Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The HS Boys Head Baseball Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Travis Rantz. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobDescription:
Harrisburg is looking for a Football coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 2026-27 MS Football Coach (open until filled) at Harrisburg SchoolDistrict is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the 2026-27 MS Football Coach (open until filled) is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Harrisburg SchoolDistrict, the 2026-27 MS Football Coach (open until filled) helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The 2026-27 MS Football Coach (open until filled) may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Harrisburg SchoolDistrict well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The 2026-27 MS Football Coach (open until filled) follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobDescription:
Harrisburg is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 2026-27 MS Volleyball Coach (open until filled) at Harrisburg SchoolDistrict is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the 2026-27 MS Volleyball Coach (open until filled) manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Harrisburg SchoolDistrict, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The 2026-27 MS Volleyball Coach (open until filled) demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The 2026-27 MS Volleyball Coach (open until filled) follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the 2026-27 MS Volleyball Coach (open until filled) at Harrisburg SchoolDistrict allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobDescription:
Huron Middle School is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY role at Huron Middle School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Huron Middle School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Huron Middle School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Middle School Track Coach 2026-2027 SY at Huron Middle School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School
Job.JobDescription:
Durango School District is looking for a Wrestling coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School role at Ryan Knorr is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Ryan Knorr, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Ryan Knorr, the 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the 25/26 Girls' Wrestling Specialist, Durango High School at Ryan Knorr is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobTitle: 25/26 Assistant Wrestling Coach, Escalante Middle School
Job.JobDescription: Escalante Middle School is looking for a Wrestling coach to join our program. Apply now.

Weld County District Re-5j is looking for an Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Roosevelt High School Athletic Statement of Purpose “Develop Champions for Life”: To encourage Roosevelt student athletes to become champions. To foster an environment where academic integrity, social responsibility, respect for others, and competitive athletic excellence are highly valued. To discover and nurture the total person who enriches the community and brings honor to Roosevelt High School throughout life. High School Assistant Coach
Terms: This position does not qualify for benefits. Salary is based on experience and per our Athletic Salary Schedule Column II ($2,350 - $3,350).
GENERAL STATEMENT OF RESPONSIBILITY
The assistant coach in each sport assigned is directly responsible to the head coach of that sport. He/she should have a working knowledge of the rules and regulations of the Re-5J Board of Education and the athletic standards of Roosevelt High School. He/she should also be familiar with the rules and regulations of the League and the Colorado High School Activities Association.
PROFESSIONAL QUALIFICATIONS
1. Teaching assignment at Roosevelt High School. If not staff, must have successfully completed certified coaching class and be certified by CHSAA. 2. Successful experience in his/her chosen coaching area. 3. Successful background check. 4. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as may be found appropriate and acceptable.
PERSONAL QUALIFICATIONS
1. Encourage the student body and spectators to be courteous and polite to the visiting team. 2. Always display good sportsmanship, winning or losing. 3. Maintain poise and control at all times, especially at the contests. 4. Teach the team to play fairly and within the rules. 5. Be a good host to the visiting team and their coaches. 6. Recommend discipline to the head coach for players who disregard good sportsmanship either on the field/floor or on the bench. 7. Respect the officials’ judgement and interpretation of the rules. If an appeal is necessary, follow appropriate procedures. 8. Coach the team and not officiate the game. 9. Publicity attempt to shake hands with officials and the opposing coach prior to and after the game.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SEASONAL
1. Assist the head coach in carrying out his/her responsibilities. 2. Assist the head coach is the issuance of equipment, registration of the players, and providing accurate information needed to compile eligibility lists and other reports. 3. Assume responsibility for care of equipment and facilities. 4. Assume control over athletes and teams assigned to him/her and assist in helping supervise all athletes in the program. 5. Be in regular attendance at practice, contests, and meetings. 6. Discipline, when necessary, in firm and positive manner. 7. Provide the head coach with information needed in making reports and/or other releases. 8. Emphasize safety and be aware of the best training and injury.
END OF SEASON
1. Assist in the return and inventory of school equipment. 2. Recommend athletes for letter awards. 3. Recommend equipment to be purchased.

Employees hired into a position are rarely qualified to receive the maximum of the pay range shown on the salary schedule. MCSDSalarySchedule (Interscholastic Athletic/Activities Compensation Schedule GCBC-E) Qualifications Requirements: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Language Skills: Ability to read and analyze and interpret general business periodicals, professional journals, technical procedures, or governmental regulations. Ability to write reports, business correspondence, and procedure manuals. Ability to effectively present information and respond to questions from groups of administrators, staff and the general public. Mathematical Skills: Ability to calculate figures and amounts such as discounts, interest, commissions, proportions, percentages, area, circumference, and volume. Ability to apply concepts of basic algebra and geometry. Reasoning Ability: Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists. Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form. Other Skills and Abilities: Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with students, peers, parents and community; ability to speak clear and concisely in written or oral communication. Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to sit, stand, walk and talk or hear. The employee is occasionally required to run; stoop and kneel. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 50lbs. The employee must occasionally push items of 75lbs. or more, such as moving/rearranging sports or field equipment. Specific vision abilities required include close vision, distance vision, and depth perception. Work Environment: The work environment characteristics described below are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. These employees spend most of their coaching time outside and they are exposed to a variety of weather conditions. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate to loud and is a standard acceptable level for this environment. Procedure for application: Applications and transfer request (for current employees) are available online at http://www.moffatsd.org. (Employment) Moffat County School District uses the applicant tracking system from Frontline Education to manage employment applications online. www.moffatsd.org
Job.Benefits: PERA retirement, and other optional benefit elections. Employees who are regularly scheduled to work 30 or more hours per week as defined by their position are also eligible for the district's health insurance.
Job.CompanyDescription: Moffat County S.D. RE-1 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Coronado High School is looking for a Swimming and Diving Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) role at Coronado High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Coronado High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Coronado High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Girls Swim Coach (Assistant) must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Coronado High School.

Big Sandy District 100j is looking for a Wrestling coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Junior High School Wrestling Coach at Big Sandy District is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Junior High School Wrestling Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Big Sandy District use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Junior High School Wrestling Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Junior High School Wrestling Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Junior High School Wrestling Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Big Sandy District. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Glenwood Springs High School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities to join our program. Apply now.
The Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach position at Glenwood Springs High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach at Glenwood Springs High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Glenwood Springs High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Track and Field Jumps Assistant Coach position at Glenwood Springs High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.Experience: Former experience with jumping events (coaching or competing)
Job.SalaryRange: Based on prior paid coaching experience in the specific sport

Job.JobTitle: Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025-2026
Job.JobDescription:
Dekalb County Schools is looking for a Wrestling coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025 position at Dekalb County Schools provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025 prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Dekalb County Schools, the Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025 works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025 encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Horizon Area School_Assistant Coach, Girls Wrestling (Lithonia HS) 2025 at Dekalb County Schools is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Brunswick High School is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Softball Coach - School Year 26/27 position at Brunswick High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Head Softball Coach - School Year 26/27 is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Brunswick High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Head Softball Coach - School Year 26/27 encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Head Softball Coach - School Year 26/27 also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Head Softball Coach - School Year 26/27 role at Brunswick High School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.JobTitle: Head Girls Volleyball Coach - School Year 26/27
Job.JobDescription:
Glynn Academy High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Serving as the Head Girls Volleyball Coach - School Year 26/27 at Glynn Academy High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobDescription:
Bartow County Schools is looking for a Softball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION TITLE: Coach - Head Softball for the 2026-2027 School Year LOCATION(S): Cass HS START DATE: July 27, 2026 JOB DESCRIPTION: Teacher: Establish a positive learning environment and respond to the individual needs of students in the classroom. Plan and implement effective lessons, using time, materials and resources effectively. Display a thorough knowledge of high school curriculum. Demonstrate awareness of the needs of students and provide for individual differences. Support the philosophy and mission of the Bartow County School System. The Bartow County School System does not discriminate on the basis of age, sex, race, color, national origin or disability in its educational programs, activities or employment practices. Job Requirements * Bachelor degree preferred. * Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Track & Field Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Job Posting: Throws Track & Field Coach - Empire High School, Tucson, AZ Location: Empire High School, Tucson, Arizona Job Type: Part-Time/Seasonal Application Deadline: Open Until Filled Season: Feb. 9th - May 2026 Apply via VSD Red Rover at: https://jobs.redroverk12.com/org/2111 (when available) Job Description: Empire High School is seeking a Throws Track & Field Coach who is responsible for coaching, developing, and mentoring student-athletes in throwing events, including shot put, discus, hammer, and javelin (as applicable). The coach will design and implement training programs that emphasize proper technique, strength development, safety, sportsmanship, and competitive excellence while supporting the overall goals of the track and field program. Key Responsibilities: Coach and instruct athletes in throwing events (shot put, discus, hammer, and/or javelin) Develop and implement effective training programs to enhance player performance and team success. Provide leadership and mentorship to student-athletes, emphasizing sportsmanship, discipline, and teamwork. Organize and supervise team practices, conditioning programs, and game strategies. Promote academic achievement and ensure players adhere to eligibility requirements. Represent Empire High School with professionalism in the community and in interscholastic competitions. Ensure compliance with Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) rules and school policies. Qualifications: Required: Prior coaching experience at the high school, club, or collegiate level. Strong knowledge of the sport's fundamentals, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to foster a positive and inclusive team environment. CPR/First Aid certification or willingness to obtain. Must pass a background check as required by the school district. Preferred: Previous experience as an assistant coach. Knowledge of strength and conditioning principles. Experience with student-athlete development programs. Benefits: Stipend-based compensation. Professional development opportunities. Access to school athletic facilities and resources. How to Apply: Interested candidates should complete the application process for the Vail School District's Employment platform. Applicants may also send their resumes to Empire's Athletic Director, James Sye, at syej@vailschooldistrict.org
Job.Experience: Prior coaching experience at the high school, club, or collegiate level.
Job.Benefits: Stipend-based compensation. Professional development opportunities. Access to school athletic facilities and resources.

From a coaching standpoint, the Head Beach Volleyball Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Demencio Solorzano, the Head Beach Volleyball Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Head Beach Volleyball Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Demencio Solorzano well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Head Beach Volleyball Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.
Job.Location: Cienega High School, 12775 E. Mary Ann Cleveland Way Vail, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
Marana Unified is looking for a Baseball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The S26-1183 Supplemental Baseball Coach (4 positions) at Marana Unified is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the S26-1183 Supplemental Baseball Coach (4 positions) are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Marana Unified use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The S26-1183 Supplemental Baseball Coach (4 positions) recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the S26-1183 Supplemental Baseball Coach (4 positions) collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The S26-1183 Supplemental Baseball Coach (4 positions) maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Marana Unified. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: S26-1180 Supplemental Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions)
Job.JobDescription:
Marana High School is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES: Demonstrates a high level of dependability, organization, respect, integrity, and sportsmanship Utilizes positive interpersonal relations and effective communication skills Exhibits effective problem solving skills and judgment Maintains inventory of all sporting equipment and supplies Works with administrators, purchasing staff, and vendors to procure needed athletic equipment and supplies Communicates expectations to team members and families at the beginning of each season. This communication includes information for practice schedules, athlete expectations, and playing time distribution. Organizes and implements an efficient and effective try-out systems, practice plans, and game plans. This includes the organization of additional coaching staff members. Surveys playing surfaces and environment to ensure player safety Complies with district and school procedures Applies knowledge of sport to develop players at all levels of ability Hold athletes to high behavioral and academic expectations Ensures sportsmanship is displayed by team members during practice, travel, and competition Works to increase knowledge of sport and player development Partners with parent groups to positively impact experience of players Follows all procurement and fundraising rules and procedures Encourages and supports skill development throughout the year Proactively portrays a positive image of sports team in the community Respects the integrity and judgment of sports officials. The head coach is responsible for all bench personnel by rule. Fulfills responsibilities to provide health services and an environment free of safety hazards. Administer basic first aid and CPR in the event of a medical emergency. Encourages the highest standards of conduct and scholastic achievement among all athletes. Performs other duties as assigned
QUALIFICATIONS Experience coaching assigned sport Exceptional communication skills with students and parents, and the ability to work with people who have a variety of skills, backgrounds, and temperaments Must possess a patient, positive and proactive attitude in dealing with students and parents Successful completion of the online National Federation of State High School Association’s (NFHS) Concussions in Sports Current CPR/First Aid Certification
Seasonal position - $1,000.00/Stipend
EQUAL EMPLOYMENT OPPORTUNITY Discrimination against an otherwise qualified individual with a disability or any individual by reason of race, color, religion, sex, age, or national origin, is prohibited. Efforts will be made in recruitment and employment to ensure equal opportunity in employment for all qualified persons.

Job.JobTitle: S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions)
Job.JobDescription:
Marana High School is looking for a Volleyball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions) role at Marana High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Marana High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Marana High School, the S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the S26-1179 Asst. Boys Volleyball Coach (2 positions) at Marana High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Tortolita Middle School is looking for a Wrestling Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The S26-1170 Head Wrestling Coach at Tortolita Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the S26-1170 Head Wrestling Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Tortolita Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The S26-1170 Head Wrestling Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the S26-1170 Head Wrestling Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the S26-1170 Head Wrestling Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Tortolita Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Principal for Athletics, Flagstaff High School (2026-2027)
Job.JobDescription:
Flagstaff High School is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Assistant Principal, Athletics Department/Section: Flagstaff High School Supervisor: Principal 12 months; Benefit eligible Placement on Salary Schedule based on experience and skills: Admin Salary Schedule EE - $89,586 - $99,441 Desired Start Date: July 01, 2026 Application Packet: Must include cover letter, three letters of recommendations dated within prior 12 months, and resume Summary of Function Responsible for assisting the Principal with leadership, direction, supervision, operations, and accountability at the assigned high school. The assistant principal will supervise the student athletic program, which includes hiring and overseeing all coaching staff. The assistant principal has shared responsibilities with the principal and serves as the principal in their absence. The assistant principal may assume responsibilities from within the principal's job description as directed and is supervised by the principal. Qualifications Qualificatlons include: Valid AZ Department of Education Administrative Certiflcate required Master's degree from an accredited college or university required Emphasis in supervision, educational administration and secondary curriculum preferred AZ Department of Education Certification required; Dual Certification preferred Structured English Immersion (SEl) endorsement, English as a Second Language (ESL) or Bilingual endorsement is required within one year of hire Valid Arizona Department of Public Safety IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card Three (3) years successful experience in teaching, administration and supervision of staff preferred Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities Understanding of district and school student-achievement data and AZ State Standards. Knowledge of Restorative Practice and coordination of discipline. Ability to manage a variety of student behaviors. Ability to communicate effectively in both written and verbal form. Essential Duties and Responsibilities The following statements of essential duties and responsibilities are intended to describe the general nature and level of work being performed by individuals assigned to this position. These statements are not intended to be an exhaustive list of all duties and responsibilities. Specific Duties 1. Position would focus/oversee district and school level programs housed at the High School with an emphasis in: A. Athletic programs and oversight B. Teacher observations and evaluations C. Staff observations and evaluations Illustrative Duties 1. Responsible for all matters involving interscholastic athletics at the high school level. 2. Represents their school at all meetings, contests, and other activities related to athletics. 3. Coordinates the arrangement of schedules for athletic activities and coordinates schedules and contracts for staff and students needed to support associated activities. 4. Coordinates transportation services for all athletic teams and other related groups directly concerned with the event inclusive of band. 5. Supports the Principal in the coordination of the athletic program budget and ensures compliance with expenditures and purchase order requirements associated with the athletic program. 6. Directs the duties for the staff assigned to support the athletic program including but not limited to administrative assistant support, coaches, game officials, student workers, and athletic trainers. 7. Evaluates staff within the athletic and physical education departments. 8. Coordinates the schedules for coverage and assists in the supervision of after-school activities. 9. Works collaboratively with all staff to strengthen the instructional program. 10. Maintains high standards of student conduct through a respectful guidance and development program, which focuses on prevention, communication with parents and high expectations of conduct. 11. Establishes effective communication and maintains positive and respectful relationships with students, staff, parents and the community 12. Participates in the recruitment, selection and supervision of all school building personnel. 13. Assists with principal in providing the staff with professional development that will enhance their teaching skills to meet the needs of a culturally diverse student populations and a variety of learning styles. 14. Participates in school disaster preparedness and safety information programs. 15. Assists in developing and implementing a school improvement plan 16. Serves with parent, faculty, and student groups as requested in advancing educational and related activities and objectives.
Job.Experience: Three (3) years successful experience in teaching, administration and supervision of staff preferred

This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Paradise Valley use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The 2025-26 Assistant Girls Track Coach - Shadow Mountain HS recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the 2025-26 Assistant Girls Track Coach - Shadow Mountain HS collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The 2025-26 Assistant Girls Track Coach - Shadow Mountain HS maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Paradise Valley. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: 2025-26 Assistant Girls Track Coach .5 - Shadow Mountain HS
Job.JobDescription:
Purpose Statement
The job of Assistant Coach .5 was established for the purpose/s of designing and implementing the assigned athletic program in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; providing supervision of students during all aspects of the program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program activities; serving as a positive role model to student athletes; and serving as a liaison between the school and community to promote a positive image of the program.
Essential Functions
Administers first aid and/or CPR for the purpose of meeting the immediate health care needs. Assesses student athletes for the purpose of providing feedback to students on his/her individual performance and/or determining team placement. Assists the head coach for the purpose of implementing and maintaining the athletic program activities. Attends a variety of meetings, trainings, workshops, etc. for the purpose of maintaining coaching knowledge and/or conveying and/or gathering information required to perform job functions. Communicates with head coach, student athletes, parents, and community for the purpose of providing information and/or direction regarding the athletic program. Develops team and individual practice regimes for student athletes (e.g. instructional activities, etc.) for the purpose of improving the student athletes' skills in the sport and teamwork. Develops game plans and scouting reports for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition. Implements practice schedules and team activities (e.g. fundraising, parent meetings, etc.) for the purpose of building a cohesive and effective team. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. game/student athlete statistics, insurance, signed permission forms, emergency information, schedules, equipment inventories, etc.) for the purpose of complying with district, athletic conference, and state requirements. Manages uniforms, equipment and school facilities for the purpose of maintaining items in a responsible and safe manner. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing appropriate intervention (e.g. counseling, tutoring, etc.) to students identified as needing assistance to maintain their athletic eligibility. Supervises student athletes during all areas of participation for the purpose of ensuring activities are conducted in an environment with minimal risk of injury to the participants. Teaches sports techniques, playing rules, strategies, and playing tactics for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition.
Other Functions Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the work unit.
Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to perform multiple, technical tasks with a need to occasionally upgrade skills in order to meet changing job conditions. Specific skill based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: adapting to changing work priorities; administering first aid; applying pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; organizing and communicating information and concepts. KNOWLEDGE is required to perform basic math, including calculations using fractions, percents, and/or ratios; review and interpret highly technical information, write technical materials, and/or speak persuasively to implement desired actions; and analyze situations to define issues and draw conclusions. Specific knowledge based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: equipment used in activity/program; injury prevention and appropriate treatment protocols; pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; participant eligibility, attendance and discipline guidelines; public relations protocols; relevant professional standards and practices; safety practices and procedures. ABILITY is required to schedule activities, meetings, and/or events; gather and/or collate data; and consider a wide variety of factors when using equipment. Flexibility is required to independently work with others in a wide variety of circumstances; work with data utilizing defined and similar processes; and operate equipment using defined methods. Ability is also required to work with a diversity of individuals and/or groups; work with data of varied types and/or purposes; and utilize a variety of types of job-related equipment. Problem solving is required to analyze issues and create action plans. Problem solving with data requires independent interpretation of guidelines; and problem solving with equipment is moderate to significant. Specific ability based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: communicating with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; setting priorities; working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends.
Responsibility Responsibilities include: working under limited supervision following standardized practices and/or methods; directing other persons within a small work unit; utilization of some resources from other work units is often required to perform the job's functions. There is a continual opportunity to impact the organization's services.
Work Environment The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: occasional lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, some climbing and balancing, some stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness.
Experience: Job related experience is required. Education: High school diploma or equivalent. Equivalency: Required Testing Certificates and Licenses Continuing Educ./Training Clearances: Criminal Background Clearance Valid Arizona DPS Level I IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card www.azdps.gov/services/public/fingerprint
FLSA Status - Non Exempt Paradise Valley Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex or disability, in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities. Any person having inquiries concerning the School's compliance with the regulations implementing Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI), Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (Section 504), Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), or Title II of the Genetic Information NonDiscrimination Act of 2008 (GINA) may contact the Assistant Superintendent of Human Resources. Position Type: .5 FTE Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching
Job.Experience: Job related experience is required.

SUMMARY: Coaches student athletic programs.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Coach student athletes in area of assigned responsibility. Organize and supervise a total sports program. Establish and maintain good rapport with students, employees, community members and vendors. Maintain a high level of ethical behavior and confidentiality. Other job related duties as assigned.
EDUCATION: Valid Arizona teacher certification, or Arizona coaching certification, or Level 1 Certification issued by National Federation of High School Associations (NFHS) (This requirement is only for Head/Varsity positions)
EXPERIENCE: Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport.
LICENSING/CERTIFICATIONS: Obtain and maintain a valid Arizona Identity Verified Prints (IVP) Fingerprint Clearance Card (AZ IVP FPCC). Obtain and maintain First Aid/CPR certification. Completion of NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching Course. Completion of NFHS Heat Illness Prevention Course. Completion of NFHS Concussion in Sports. Completion of NFHS Cheer & Dance Safety Certificate (this requirement is for cheer positions only). Completion of NFHS Cheer & Dance Exam (this requirement is for cheer positions only).
