Valley Vista High School - Surprise is looking for a Soccer coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls Soccer Freshman Coach (VVHS) position at Valley Vista High School - Surprise is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Girls Soccer Freshman Coach (VVHS) is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Valley Vista High School - Surprise, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Girls Soccer Freshman Coach (VVHS) encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Girls Soccer Freshman Coach (VVHS) also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Girls Soccer Freshman Coach (VVHS) role at Valley Vista High School - Surprise is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.
Job.Location: Valley Vista High School - Surprise, AZ - Surprise, Arizona

Job.JobDescription:
Dysart Unified is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer position at Dysart Unified School District is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer at Dysart Unified School District sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Dysart Unified School District, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Classified Exempt Substitute - Athletic Trainer position at Dysart Unified School District is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.Location: District Human Resources, Surprise, AZ - Surprise, Arizona

Job.JobDescription:
Kofa High School - Yuma is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB DESCRIPTION Yuma Union High School District #70 Assistant Coach - Out of District Purpose Statement: The position of Assistant Coach is done for the purpose/s of assisting the head coach in designing and implementing the athletic program for assigned sport(s) in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; assist with conducting practices, motivating students, and instructing student athletes in game strategies and techniques. Essential Functions: Assists the Head coach with instructing players in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises as directed by the Head coach. Assesses player's skills, monitors players during competition and practice, and keeps the Head coach informed of the athletic performance of students. Assists with determining game strategy. Assists the Head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competition. Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. Maintains the equipment room in orderly condition and assumes responsibility for its security. Distributes equipment, supplies, and uniforms to students as directed by the Head coach. Assists the Head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competition. Participates in special activities to include parent's night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. Performs support tasks such as distributing and maintaining eligibility forms, emergency data cards, and other related records. Models nondiscriminatory practices in all activities. Other Functions: Maintains standards of pupil behavior and contributes to a supportive school climate. Takes all necessary and reasonable precautions to protect students, equipment, materials and facilities. Maintains accurate, complete, and correct records as required by law, district policy, and administrative regulation. Serves as a role model for students and staff in demonstrating positive attitudes, appropriate attire and grooming, and an effective work ethic. Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to perform multiple, non-technical tasks with a need to occasionally upgrade skills in order to meet changing job conditions. KNOWLEDGE is required to perform basic math; read a variety of manuals, write documents following prescribed formats, and/or present information to others; and analyze situations to define issues and draw conclusions. ABILITY is required to schedule activities; gather and/or collate data; and use job-related equipment. Flexibility is required to work with others in a variety of circumstances; work with data utilizing defined but different processes; and operate equipment using standardized methods. Ability is also required to work with a diversity of individuals and/or groups; work with a variety of data; and utilize job-related equipment. Problem solving is required to identify issues and create action plans. Problem solving with data requires independent interpretation of guidelines; and problem solving with equipment is limited to moderate. Ability is required to establish and maintain effective working relationships; work as part of a team; understand and carry out oral and written instructions; adapt to changing work priorities; communicate with diverse groups; maintain confidentiality; and meet deadlines and schedules. Working Environment: The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: Duties are normally performed in a school environment or outdoors. Duties may be occasionally performed on field trips away from school. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. Experience: Job Related Experience Education: High school diploma or equivalent Required Testing: Certificates & Licenses: Drivers License & Evidence of Insurability NFHS - Fundamentals of Coaching Concussion in Sports Continuing Educ./Training: As Needed Clearances: I.V.P. Fingerprint Card/Criminal Justice Fingerprint/Background Clearance

Job.JobTitle: NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV
Job.JobDescription:
Nogales Unified School District is looking for a Volleyball Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV position at Marcia Renee Travers provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Marcia Renee Travers, the NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the NHS Asst Girls Volleyball Coach - JV at Marcia Renee Travers is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Job.JobTitle: Boys' Beach Volleyball Coach - Buckeye Union High School
Job.JobDescription:
Buckeye Union High School Dist. is looking for a Volleyball Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
This is for the 2026 Fall Season. This is an emerging sport for the district.
Not Benefits Eligible.
TITLE: Athletic Coach EMPLOYMENT TERMS: Seasonal (Fall, Winter, Spring sports)
SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Stipend
GENERAL STATEMENT of RESPONSIBILITIES: To provide the students of the Buckeye Union High School District with a first-class Athletic program by developing a cohesive team that demonstrates sportsmanship and fundamental skills; adhering to AIA rules and regulations.
PRINCIPAL DUTIES: 1. Responsible for the conduct of the student/athlete from the time he/she enters the program until he/she leaves it, including conduct on trips, in the locker room, on the practice field/court, and at other schools or locations. 2. Assists the student/athlete in his/her charge in maintaining a high academic standing. 3. Models good sportsmanship at all times. 4. Assists the Head Coach in submitting an eligibility roster to the Athletic Director’s office prior to the first contest. 5. Keeps the Athletic Director informed about any discipline problems with student/athletes. 6. Adheres to all AIA rules and regulations. 7. Assists in maintaining an inventory of all equipment in the sport at the end of each season. 8. Maintains an open line of communication with the parents/guardians of student/athletes. 9. Attends scheduled meetings. 10. Attends all practices and games. 11. Demonstrates a high degree of professionalism as a role model for student/athletes. 12. Follows district policies and procedures. 13. Other duties as assigned.
QUALIFICATIONS: Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport. Valid Arizona teacher certification, or Arizona coaching certification, or Level 1 Certification issued by National Federation of High School Associations (NFHS) (This requirement is only for Head/Varsity positions) Obtain and maintain a valid Arizona Identity Verified Prints (IVP) Fingerprint Clearance Card (AZ IVP FPCC). Obtain and maintain First Aid/CPR certification. Completion of NFHS online education course entitled “Fundamentals of Coaching” Course. Completion of NFHS online education course entitled, “Concussion in Sports - What You Need to Know.”
SKILLS REQUIRED: Providing customer service. Following directions and meeting standards. Providing attention to detail in assignments. Comprehending reference books and manuals. Organizing and maintaining supplies. Utilizing a computer and relevant software applications. Utilizing communication and interpersonal skills as applied to interaction with coworkers, supervisors, the general public and others to sufficiently exchange or convey information and to receive work direction.
KNOWLEDGE OF: Standard practices, methods and materials of assigned work. Occupational hazards and applicable safety principles and practices. Uses and properties of supplies and equipment. Customer service principles. Specialized equipment relevant to area of assignment. Modern office technology.
COMMITMENT & DEPENDABILITY: Willingness to be an integral member of a successful team. Dedication to provide the highest level of service. Adherence to policies and procedures. Resourceful and adaptable.
POSITION EXPECTATIONS: Time Management: Must be able to prioritize tasks assigned by supervisor. Stress Management: Periodic stress due to deadlines and multiple schedules. Public Contact: Interact primarily with students, public, staff and parents.
Job.Location: Buckeye Union High School - Buckeye, Arizona
Job.Experience: At least 3 year(s) of relevant experience preferred.
Job.CompanyDescription: Buckeye Union High School Dist. coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Girls' Flag Football Coach - Buckeye Union High School
Job.JobDescription:
Buckeye Union High School Dist. is looking for a Football Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
This is for the 2026 Fall Season.
This is an emerging sport for the district.
Not benefits eligible.
TITLE: Athletic Coach
EMPLOYMENT TERMS: Seasonal (Fall, Winter, Spring sports)
SALARY CLASSIFICATION: Stipend - Based on Extra Duty Salary Schedule
GENERAL STATEMENT OF RESPONSIBILITIES:
The Athletic Coach will be responsible for providing a positive working relationship with students, faculty, staff, and parents. Responsibilities will include coaching student athletic programs and ensuring adherence to AIA guidelines.
PRINCIPLE DUTIES:
Coach student athletes in area of assigned responsibility Organize and supervise a total sports program Establish and maintain good rapport with students, staff, faculty and parents. Confirms that all athletes have met AIA and district eligibility requirements before participation in scheduled events. Communicates team policies to all athletes (V, JV, FR) Follows district and school drug/alcohol codes Reports Team Roster and Athletic Scores to the Athletic Director's office. Assists Athletic Director with athletic events for assigned athletic team.
OCCUPATIONAL CONDITION: Medium risk - Work is routinely performed in outdoor environments, including exposure to noise and being on your feet for extended periods of time. Requires standing in inclement weather including summer heat.
QUALIFICATIONS:
Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport. Valid Arizona teacher certification, or Arizona coaching certification, or Level 1 Certification issued by National Federation of High School Associations (NFHS) (This requirement is only for Head/Varsity positions) Obtain and maintain a valid Arizona Identity Verified Prints (IVP) Fingerprint Clearance Card (AZ IVP FPCC). Obtain and maintain First Aid/CPR certification. Completion of NFHS online education course entitled "Fundamentals of Coaching". Course. Completion of NFHS online education course entitled, "Concussion in Sports - What You Need to Know."
SKILLS REQUIRED:
Providing customer service. Following directions and meeting standards. Providing attention to detail in assignments. Comprehending reference books and manuals. Organizing and maintaining supplies. Utilizing a computer and relevant software applications. Utilizing communication and interpersonal skills as applied to interaction with coworkers, supervisors, the general public and others to sufficiently exchange or convey information and to receive work direction.
KNOWLEDGE OF:
Standard practices, methods and materials of assigned work. Occupational hazards and applicable safety principles and practices. Uses and properties of supplies and equipment. Customer service principles. Specialized equipment relevant to area of assignment. Modern office technology.
COMMITMENT & DEPENDABILITY:
Willingness to be an integral member of a successful team. Dedication to provide the highest level of service. Adherence to policies and procedures. Resourceful and adaptable.
POSITION EXPECTATIONS: Time Management: Must be able to prioritize tasks assigned by supervisor. Stress Management: Periodic stress due to deadlines and multiple schedules. Public Contact: Interact primarily with students, public, staff and parents.
EVALUATION PROCEDURE: The evaluation is done by the Athletic Director.
SUPERVISION RECEIVED: The Athletic Coach reports directly to the Athletic Director on site.

Job.JobDescription: Essential Functions: (Essential functions, as defined under the Americans with Disabilities Act, may include the following tasks, knowledge, skills and other characteristics. This list is ILLUSTRATIVE ONLY, and is not a complete listing of all functions and tasks performed by positions in this job class.) Required Qualifications: Current, valid Arizona Level One IVP fingerprint clearance card, issued by the Arizona DPS Valid Arizona driver’s license with clean driving record Ability and willingness to drive a small van to transport students to athletic events Coaching and/or playing experience in assigned sport Knowledge and background in assigned sport Professional development activities in pedagogy and methodology of the assigned sport Must be CPR certified prior to second full season Supervises: Athletes and team assigned. Assumes supervisory control over all athletes in program when such control is needed. Job Goal: To carry out the aims and objectives of the sport program as outlined by the head coach and school administration. To instruct athletes in individual and team fundamentals, strategy and physical training necessary to realize a degree of individual and team success Duties and Responsibilities: Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Governing Board and is responsible for implementation Has knowledge of the existing school district, state and regional regulations; implements same consistently Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all students and parent requests or grievances through proper channels. Is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance Maintains discipline and works to increase morale and cooperation within the school sports program and school community Administrative Duties: Assists head coach in scheduling, providing transportation to tournaments and special sport events. Assists in preparation for scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees. Provides to the athletic director documentation necessary to fulfill school, district, and state requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. Equipment and Facilities: Is accountable to the head coach for all equipment. Collects the cost of any equipment lost or not returned. Arranges issuing, marking and storing of equipment and submits an annual inventory and current records. Coordinates all equipment processing with the equipment manager. Monitors locker rooms, equipment room, training room, and coaches' offices and authorizes who may enter. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building. Coordinate with custodial staff on duty. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use. Nondiscrimination/Equal Opportunity Policy In CFSD, we value a workforce that reflects the diversity of our students. We are committed to a policy of non-discrimination and equal employment opportunity for all applicants and employees. All qualified applicants for employment will be considered without regard to an individual's race, color, sex (which includes a prohibition against sexual harassment as described in District policy ACA-Sexual Harassment), sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, religion, age, national origin or ancestry, ethnicity, religion, or disability.
Job.Experience: Coaching and/or playing experience in assigned sport

Job.JobTitle: High School Assistant Coach - Girls Volleyball
Job.JobDescription:
Located in beautiful southeast Arizona, Sierra Vista Unified School District is seeking passionate, dedicated coaches to join our athletic team! Award-Winning District * 8 Outstanding Schools * Competitive Supplemental Coaching Salaries * Culture of Caring & Support Apply Today & Make a Difference in a Student Athlete's Life.
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Ability to obtain the National Federation of State High School Associations Level I Certification 2. Successful completion of the NFHS course titled "Bullying, Hazing and Inappropriate Behaviors" 3. Ability to obtain CPR/First Aid Certification 4. Ability to obtain a valid Arizona IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card 5. Valid Arizona Teaching Certificate or Arizona Coaching Certificate - optional 6. Knowledge, skill and or experience in the implementation of Positive Behavior Intervention Supports (PBIS) strategies or be willing to receive training in those strategies as applicable.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES 1. Utilize, with fidelity, the District Positive Behavior Intervention Support (PBIS) program at the building level. 2. Ensure information obtained through the course of duties associated with this position is held in the highest of confidentiality. 3. Perform all duties in accordance with Federal and State laws, district procedures and Board policies; including mandatory reporting procedures.
FUNCTION: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem.

Located in beautiful southeast Arizona, Sierra Vista Unified School District is seeking passionate, dedicated coaches to join our athletic team! Award-Winning District * 8 Outstanding Schools * Competitive Supplemental Coaching Salaries * Culture of Caring & Support Apply Today & Make a Difference in a Student Athlete's Life.
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Ability to obtain the National Federation of State High School Associations Level I Certification 2. Successful completion of the NFHS course titled “Bullying, Hazing and Inappropriate Behaviors” 3. Ability to obtain CPR/First Aid Certification 4. Ability to obtain a valid Arizona IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card 5. Valid Arizona Teaching Certificate or Arizona Coaching Certificate - optional 6. Knowledge, skill and or experience in the implementation of Positive Behavior Intervention Supports (PBIS) strategies or be willing to receive training in those strategies as applicable.
KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES 1. Utilize, with fidelity, the District Positive Behavior Intervention Support (PBIS) program at the building level. 2. Ensure information obtained through the course of duties associated with this position is held in the highest of confidentiality. 3. Perform all duties in accordance with Federal and State laws, district procedures and Board policies; including mandatory reporting procedures.
FUNCTION: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem.
POSITION SUMMARY The assistant coach will work under the management of the Varsity Coach for their respective sport. Each coach must work within the framework of the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) to provide leadership in the ongoing development and improvement of student athletes in their sport through coordinating, planning, evaluation, and implementation of programs. All coaches have the responsibility of carrying on their respective sport within the best interest of Sierra Vista Unified School District and its students within the bounds of the rules set forth by the AIA and the National Federation of State High School Associations (NFHS). The coach must demonstrate and apply leadership, integrity, responsibility, self-control, and knowledge of rules and regulations at all times. It is necessary and important that the coach act in a responsible and professional manner. The coach must set a good example and provide a healthy lifestyle.
GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Coach individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. 2. Plan and schedule a regular program of practice in season. 3. Maintain necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork. 4. Oversee the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present. 5. Enforce discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times and oversee penalties for breach of such standards by individual students.
ORGANIZATIONAL RELATIONSHIPS: The Assistant Coach reports to the Varsity Coach and the Assistant Principal of Athletics/Activities. The Assistant Coach will interact cooperatively with all other staff positions. All elements of good human relations will be practiced.
SEASONAL POSITION - VIEW SUPPLEMENTAL PAY Policy GCBC-EC and Policy GCBC-ED

NON-DISCRIMINATION/EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Works with administrators, purchasing staff, and vendors to procure needed athletic equipment and supplies 5. Maintains inventory of all sporting equipment and supplies 6. Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork. 7. Complies with district and school procedures

POSITION SUMMARY The high school head coach will work within the framework of the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) to provide leadership in the ongoing development and improvement of student athletes in their sport through coordinating, planning, evaluation, and implementation of programs. All coaches have the responsibility of carrying on their respective sport within the best interest of Sierra Vista Unified School District and its students within the bounds of the rules set forth by the AIA and the National Federation of State High School Associations (NFHS). The coach must demonstrate and apply leadership, integrity, responsibility, self-control, and knowledge of rules and regulations at all times. It is necessary and important that the coach act in a responsible and professional manner. The coach must set a good example and provide a healthy lifestyle.
GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Applies knowledge of sport to develop players at all levels of ability 2. Coaches individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. 3. Select, train, and manage additional coaches in program 4. Works with administrators, purchasing staff, and vendors to procure needed athletic equipment and supplies 5. Maintains inventory of all sporting equipment and supplies 6. Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records, and similar paperwork. 7. Oversees the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present. 8. Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times and oversee penalties for breach of such standards by individual students. 9. Follows all procurement and fundraising rules and procedures 10. Encourages the highest standards of conduct and scholastic achievement among all athletes. 11. Complies with district and school procedures
ORGANIZATIONAL RELATIONSHIPS: The Varsity Coach reports to the Assistant Principal of Athletics/Activities. The Varsity Coach will interact cooperatively with all other staff positions. All elements of good human relations will be practiced.
SEASONAL POSITION - VIEW SUPPLEMENTAL PAY PLAN GCBC-EC
FOR FURTHER INFORMATION, CONTACT: Assistant Principal Athletic Office/Buena High School, 515-2824 Human Resources - 515-2760 or human.resources@svps.k12.az.us
NON-DISCRIMINATION/EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

Job.JobDescription:
Cactus Shadows High School - Scottsdale is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES: Supports District schools and principals in their effort to foster an optimal educational environment for students. To instruct student athletes in fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Students shall also receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not over-ride the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. The position includes other unusual aspects such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments
It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment.
MAJOR TASKS:
A staff of high school assistant coaches and middle school coaches in conjunction with the district activities director and the respective Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by Designs conferences, clinics and staff meetings to ensure staff awareness of overall program Trains and informs staff, encourages professional growth by encouraging clinic attendance according to local policy Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation, and at season's end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants Maintains discipline, adjusts concerns and works to increase morale and cooperation Assists the district activities director in scheduling, providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. Coordinates program with maintenance and school employees Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment and site Advises the athletic director and recommends policy, method or procedural changes Provides training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete who is considered as a Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct By his/her presence at all practices, games and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance and safeguards for each participant Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies Completes reports of all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office within 24 Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians Determines discipline, delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible Assists athletes in their college or advanced educational selection Participates in the budget process with the district activities Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations Is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment lost or not Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices Permits athletes to be in authorized areas at the appropriate times Examines locker rooms before and after practices and Checks on general cleanliness of the facility Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building when custodians are not on duty Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care and proper use Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for pre-season meetings Promotes the sport within the school by recruiting athletes that are not participating in Promotes sports outside the school through news media, little league programs or in any other publicity vehicle as approved

Job.JobDescription:
Cactus Shadows High School - Scottsdale is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Baseball Coach - CSHS - 2025-2026 SY at Cactus Shadows High School - Scottsdale is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Baseball Coach - CSHS - 2025-2026 SY manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Cactus Shadows High School - Scottsdale, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Baseball Coach - CSHS - 2025-2026 SY demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Baseball Coach - CSHS - 2025-2026 SY follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Baseball Coach - CSHS - 2025-2026 SY at Cactus Shadows High School - Scottsdale allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.district: Casa Grande Union High Sch. Dist.
Job.JobDescription:
Casa Grande Union High Sch. Dist. is looking for a Football Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
POSITION: Flag Football Coach LOCATION: Vista Grande High School REPORTS TO: Athletic Director/Principal JOB GOAL: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship, and an increased sense of self-esteem.
Qualifications: - High School Diploma or equivalent required. - Must possess a valid IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card. - Must meet coaching requirements of the Casa Grande Union High School District and the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA). - Experience in coaching the particular sport/activity is preferred. - Knowledge of the fundamental skills and techniques, as well as the rules and regulations of the sport/activity.
Skills: - Judgment and Decision Making - Considers the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one. - Instructing - Teaching others how to do something. - Coordination - Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions. - Persuasion - Persuading others to change their minds or behavior. - Monitoring - Monitoring/Assessing performance of yourself, other individuals, or organizations to make improvements or take corrective action. - Time Management - Managing one's own time and the time of others. - Speaking - Talking to others to convey information effectively. - Active Listening - Giving full attention to what other people are saying, taking time to understand the points being made, asking questions as appropriate, and not interrupting at inappropriate times. - Critical Thinking - Using logic and reasoning to identify the strengths and weaknesses of alternative solutions, conclusions or approaches to problems. - Reading Comprehension - Understanding written sentences and paragraphs in work related documents. - Writing - Communicating effectively in writing as appropriate for the needs of the audience. - Complex Problem Solving - Identifying complex problems and reviewing related information to develop and evaluate options and implement solutions. - Judgment and Decision Making - Considering the relative costs and benefits of potential actions to choose the most appropriate one. - Teamwork - Ability to work with others in a team environment. - Leadership - Ability to lead and guide individuals and teams. - Organization - Ability to organize and manage multiple tasks. - Problem Sensitivity - The ability to tell when something is wrong or is likely to go wrong. It does not involve solving the problem, only recognizing there is a problem. - Deductive Reasoning - The ability to apply general rules to specific problems to produce answers that make sense. - Inductive Reasoning - The ability to combine pieces of information to form general rules or conclusions (includes finding a relationship among seemingly unrelated events). - Information Ordering - The ability to arrange things or actions in a certain order or pattern according to a specific rule or set of rules (e.g., patterns of numbers, letters, words, pictures, mathematical operations). - Interpersonal - Ability to work with and relate to people. - Management of Personnel Resources - Motivating, developing, and directing people as they work, identifying the best people for the job. - Management of Material Resources - Obtaining and seeing to the appropriate use of equipment, facilities, and materials needed to do certain work. - Management of Financial Resources - Determining how money will be spent to get the work done, and accounting for these expenditures. - Learning Strategies - Selecting and using training/instructional methods and procedures appropriate for the situation when learning or teaching new things. - Active Learning - Understanding the implications of new information for both current and future problem-solving and decision-making. - Originality - The ability to come up with unusual or clever ideas about a given topic or situation, or to develop creative ways to solve a problem. - Fluency of Ideas - The ability to come up with a number of ideas about a topic (the number of ideas is important, not their quality, correctness, or creativity). - Oral Comprehension - The ability to listen to and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences. - Oral Expression - The ability to communicate information and ideas in speaking so others will understand. - Speech Clarity - The ability to speak clearly so others can understand you. - Speech Recognition - The ability to identify and understand the speech of another person. - Written Comprehension - The ability to read and understand information and ideas presented in writing. - Written Expression - The ability to communicate information and ideas in writing so others will understand. - Selective Attention - The ability to concentrate on a task over a period of time without being distracted. - Time Sharing - The ability to shift back and forth between two or more activities or sources of information (such as speech, sounds, touch, or other sources). - Adaptability - Adapts to changes in the work environment. Manages competing demands. Changes approach or method to best fit the situation. Able to deal with frequent change, delays, or unexpected events. - Attendance/Punctuality - Ensures work responsibilities are covered when absent. Arrives at meetings and appointments on time. - Dependability - Follows instructions, responds to management direction. Takes responsibility for own actions. Keeps commitments. Commits to long hours of work when necessary to reach goals. Completes tasks on time or notifies appropriate person with an alternate plan. - Initiative - Volunteers readily. Undertakes self-development activities. Seeks increased responsibilities. Takes independent actions and calculated risks. Looks for and takes advantage of opportunities. Asks for and offers help when needed. - Innovation - Displays original thinking and creativity. Meets challenges with resourcefulness. Generates suggestions for improving work. Develops innovative approaches and ideas. Presents ideas and information in a manner that gets others' attention.
RESPONSIBILITIES: Responsibilities and duties may include, but are not limited to, the following: - Advocates for their department - Effectively communicates with staff, community, parents and players regarding activities and student participation in sports - Enforces AIA regulations and Board policies - Where appropriate evaluates support staff - Oversees the maintenance of equipment and buildings used by the athletic department - Keeps and accurate inventory of all equipment - Is a mentor and coach for the students - Is responsible for daily operations of their sport including transportation, student eligibility and the physical/emotional well being of the students. - Fulfills all duties/responsibilities during the sport season and all pre-season duties associated with the sport. - Regularly attends coaching clinics and workshops - Provides proper supervision, administration, and security of locker rooms, practice areas, and buses/vans at all times.
TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: - Stipend Position - Must pass Department of Public Safety Fingerprint clearance check. - Must be United States citizen or have valid work visa at date of hire with the ability to maintain. All changes in visa status must be reported to Human Resources immediately. Photo identification and work visa, (if necessary), will be needed for fingerprint clearance card and I-9 documentation prior to first day of work.
EVALUATION: - Performance of this position will be evaluated in accordance with provisions of the Board's policy on Evaluation of Professional Personnel.
REQUIRED DOCUMENTATION: - Proof of Mumps, Measles and Rubella, (MMR) vaccination. - Valid government issued identification or current work visa with expiration date. - Unofficial copies of transcripts will be accepted with applications, official transcripts will be due upon job offer*
* Transcripts issued in a language other than English must be accompanied by a full translation, (word for word) by an acceptable translator. Additionally, each foreign transcript must be evaluated for equivalency to United States accredited coursework by an acceptable agency such as those agencies certified by the National Association of Credential Evaluation Services, (NACES). Applications containing foreign transcripts that are not accompanied with the above required documentation will not be considered.
Casa Grande High School District #82 prohibits discrimination in employment and educational programs based on race, color, religion, sex, age, disability, national origin, military status, genetic test information, sexual orientation or gender identity or expression and provides equal access to the Boy Scouts and other designated youth groups.
Revised 4/2019 Position Type: Part-Time

Job.JobDescription:
POSITION: Coach LOCATION: District Wide – As Assigned REPORTS TO: Athletic Director and/or Assistant Principal
JOB GOAL: The Coach is to provide additional educational opportunities outside of the regular curriculum by providing leadership and mentoring skills to students will they participate in a sport. They provide a safe environment where a student can develop personal skills and teamwork.
QUALIFICATIONS: Qualifications to perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skills, and/or abilities required to be successful:
Must be at least 21 years old and hold a high school diploma or GED Must possess an Arizona IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card Must have valid CPR and First Aid certifications Must have valid coaching certifications in Fundamentals of Coaching, Concussion in Sports, and Heat Illness Prevention Knowledge of athletic equipment, facilities organization, and maintenance Knowledge of public relations and interpersonal skills to work with parents, students, staff, and the community Ability to apply knowledge of current research and theory to instructional programs Ability to plan and implement lessons based on the division and school objectives and the needs and abilities of the student Ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with students, peers, parents, and community members Ability to speak clearly and concisely both in oral and written communication Ability to perform duties with awareness of all district requirements and Board of Education policies Such alternatives to the above qualifications as the Board may find appropriate and acceptable Skills: Adaptability, Communication, Critical Thinking, Decision Making, Leadership, Organizing, Problem Solving, Relationship Building, Teamwork
RESPONSIBILITIES: Performs all duties in a safe and prudent manner as directed by the supervisors Adheres to all Board of Education policies and regulations Adheres to all District administrative procedures Follows the District’s discipline procedures and maintains order in the area of assignment in a fair and just manner Maintains confidentiality for student information Takes all necessary and reasonable precautions to protect students, equipment, materials, and facilities Supervises pupils in out-of-classroom activities as assigned Serves as a role model for students in how to conduct themselves as citizens and as responsible, intelligent human beings Helps instill in students the belief in and practice of ethical principles and democratic values Maintains a cooperative relationship with the school staff, students, and the immediate community Participates in in-service training programs as assigned Keeps current with the latest trends and practices in the area of assignment Performs other duties as assigned
TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT: Seasonal (Stipend - Based on Experience)
EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated in accordance with the provisions of the Board’s policy on Evaluation of Support Services Personnel.

The Arizona Department of Education requires that all persons applying for any athletic coaching assignments, Grades 7-12 (Head or Assistant Coach) hold a valid elementary, secondary, special education or substitute certificate or an Athletic Coaching Certificate. The 25-26 High School Assistant Track Coach (9-12) at PAGE HIGH SCHOOL is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the 25-26 High School Assistant Track Coach (9-12) are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at PAGE HIGH SCHOOL use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The 25-26 High School Assistant Track Coach (9-12) recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the 25-26 High School Assistant Track Coach (9-12) collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The 25-26 High School Assistant Track Coach (9-12) maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at PAGE HIGH SCHOOL. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

The 25-26 NAIC Baseball Coach (7-8) role at PAGE HIGH SCHOOL is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the 25-26 NAIC Baseball Coach (7-8) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At PAGE HIGH SCHOOL, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The 25-26 NAIC Baseball Coach (7-8) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At PAGE HIGH SCHOOL, the 25-26 NAIC Baseball Coach (7-8) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the 25-26 NAIC Baseball Coach (7-8) at PAGE HIGH SCHOOL is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Wrestling Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling role at ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The 2025/26 ASST. WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL.
ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.

Rim Country Middle School is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
TITLE: HIGH SCHOOL AND MIDDLE SCHOOL ASSISTANT COACH DEPARTMENT: School QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Must possess valid National Federation of High Schools (NFHS) approved Certificates in the following: 1. Fundamentals of Coaching 2. Concussion in Sports 3. First Aid, Health & Safety 2. Familiar with Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) organization and its Bylaws/Policies and Procedures and other requirements (high school level only). 3. Possess or have the ability to obtain a valid Arizona Identity Verified Prints (IVP) Fingerprint Clearance Card within forty-five (45) days of employment. ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS FOR CHEER/SPIRITLINE ASSISTANT COACHES: 1. Must possess a valid American Association of Cheerleading Coaches and Advisors (AACCA) certification. 2. Must have successfully completed the NFHS Cheer Safety Exam with a passing score of 75% or higher. JOB GOALS: The Assistant Coach carries out the objectives of the sports program as directed by the Head Coach. The Assistant Coach supports the Head Coach and instructs athletes with regard to individual and team fundamentals, strategy and physical training, while demonstrating behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. The Assistant Coach strives to further develop his/her knowledge and skills through coaching clinics, observations, consultation. PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: The following tasks are essential for this position. This list is not inclusive of all responsibilities. Individuals may not perform all of these tasks, or may perform similar related tasks not listed. 1. Complies with all HIPAA and FERPA related confidentiality requirements. 2. Develops positive relations with students, parents, school staff and the public, including members of the media, boosters, officials, players, other coaches, school administration, and fans. 3. Serves as a positive role model for students, coaching staff, and the community. 4. Demonstrates knowledge of proven coaching theories, strategies, and techniques and works with student athletes of varying abilities, socio-economic, and cultural backgrounds to effectively implement these attributes. 5. Instills commitment, discipline, sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork to all athletes and coaching staff. 6. The Assistant Coach performs other related duties as assigned by the Head Coach or Athletic Director/Coordinator. MINIMUM KNOWLEDGE, SKILL AND ABILITY: Knowledge of: 1. AIA rules, policies (high school level only), and athletic department handbook. 2. Effective coaching theories, strategies, and techniques appropriate for school athletes. 3. Federal and state laws, rules and regulations governing education and sports program implementation. Skill and Ability to: 1. Motivate student athletes to participate and sustain their interests in the athletic program while excelling in instructional curriculum. 2. Create beneficial relationships and communicate effectively with supervisors, staff, students, vendors, other coaches and community members. 3. Apply leadership techniques. 4. Implement educational policies and procedures. 5. Supervise and evaluate coaching staff. 6. Analyze and problem solve. Physical Requirements and Working Conditions: The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 1. Regularly required to talk or listen. 2. Frequently required to stand for long periods and to walk long distances. 3. Occasionally required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; climb or balance; and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. 4. Must occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. 5. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus. REPORTS TO: Head Coach and/or Athletic Director/Coordinator EVALUATION: Performance of this job will be evaluated annually by the Head Coach and/or Athletic Director/Coordinator in accordance with Governing Board policy.

Job.JobTitle: Head Football Coach – Mohave High School
Job.JobDescription:
The Head Football Coach – Mohave High School at Colorado River Union High School District is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Head Football Coach – Mohave High School manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Colorado River Union High School District, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Head Football Coach – Mohave High School demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Head Football Coach – Mohave High School follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Head Football Coach – Mohave High School at Colorado River Union High School District allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
