Job.JobTitle: 2025-26 Festival Sports Girls Basketball Coach - Sunrise MS
Job.JobDescription:
Sunrise MS is looking for a Basketball Temporary to join our program. Apply now.
Purpose Statement
The job of Assistant Coach is done for the purpose/s of designing and implementing the athletic program for assigned sport(s) in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; providing supervision of other coaching personnel; providing supervision of students during all aspects of the program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program activities; serving as a positive role model to student athletes; and serving in a liaison capacity for the school and program with other schools both within and/or outside the district, to the community, and to various organizations. Individuals in this job classification are assigned to a position for assigned sport(s) and oversees all aspects of the respective athletic program(s).
This job reports to Athletic Director
Essential Functions Assesses student athletes for the purpose of providing feedback to students on their individual performance and/or determining team placement. Attends a variety of meetings, workshops, conferences, etc. for the purpose of receiving and conveying information related to their role. Conducts a variety of supporting activities (e.g. parent orientations, recognition events, banquets, etc.) for the purpose of providing information regarding the program and/or recognizing participants' accomplishments. Develops team and individual practice regimes for student athletes (e.g. strength, speed, agility, nutritional, injury prevention, etc.) for the purpose of enhancing and improving their performance. Develops game strategies/plans (e.g. analyzes opponent strengths and weaknesses, analyzing athlete abilities, creating plays/strategies, etc.) for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition. Guides other coaching personnel as may be appropriate in instructional techniques, organization of practices, supervision guidelines and responsibilities, etc. for the purpose of providing guidance and mentoring. Identifies program needs for the purpose of providing recommendations of expenditures for activities, equipment, supplies, etc. that will enhance the assigned athletic program. Implements practice schedules and related events (e.g. usage of facilities and equipment, staff assignments, etc.) for the purpose of ensuring efficient program operation. Instructs student athletes for the purpose of promoting individual growth in athletic skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. game/student athlete statistics, insurance, signed permission forms, emergency information, schedules, equipment inventories, etc.) for the purpose of complying with district, athletic conference, state requirements, and/or report scores and strategies to local news media. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing appropriate intervention (e.g. counseling, tutoring, etc.) to students identified as needing assistance to maintain their athletic eligibility. Oversees assistant coaches, student managers, volunteers, etc. for the purpose of providing direction and monitoring activities. Promotes athletic program through a variety of activities (e.g. conducts off-season clinics, information on skill-camps, attends community meetings, etc.) for the purpose of increasing interest and participation. Responds to inquiries of students, parents, other school personnel, media representatives, college representatives, etc. for the purpose of providing information, assistance and/or direction. Keeps their school athletic director informed when issues arise that impact the school's athletic program (e.g. liability concerns, parent concerns, etc.). Provides appropriate and prudent supervision for all athletes and students involved in the program. Explains and discusses the PVUSD Athletic Code of Conduct with all athletes in their program. Maintains high standards of personal conduct. Coaches should serve as role models for their athletes. Ensures that all coaches involved with their program, whether paid or volunteer are cleared through the PVUSD Human Resources Department. Other Functions Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the work unit. Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to adapt to changing work priorities; administering first aid; applying pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; coordinating activities with other schools, districts and/or agencies; organizing and communicating information and concepts; and overseeing financial transactions. KNOWLEDGE is required for community support organizations and public relations; equipment used in activity/program; injury prevention and appropriate treatment protocols; pertinent laws codes, policies, and/or regulations; participant eligibility, attendance and discipline guidelines; public relations protocols; relevant professional standards and practices; and safety practices and procedures. ABILITY is required to communicate with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; providing leadership, direction, and team building; traveling to off campus athletic events; traveling to off campus competitions; and working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness. Purpose Statements The job of Assistant Coach is done for the purpose/s of designing and implementing program activities in accordance with applicable rules and regulations related to the sport; providing supervision of student athletes during all aspects of their participation in program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program; serving as a positive role model to student athletes to the community, and to various organizations. Individuals in this job classification are assigned to a position for a specific sport at the Varsity, JV/Frosh or Junior High level.
This job reports to Athletic Director
Essential Functions Assesses student athletes for the purpose of providing feedback to students on their individual performance and/or determining team placement. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing appropriate intervention (e.g. counseling, tutoring, etc.) to students identified as needing assistance to maintain their athletic eligibility. Supervises student athletes during all areas of participation for the purpose of ensuring activities are conducted in an environment with minimal risk of injury to the participants. Teaches sports techniques, playing rules, strategies, and playing tactics for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition.

Job.JobDescription:
Paradise Valley is looking for a Track & Field Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assistant Girls Track Coach Location: Shadow Mountain High School FTE: .5 Temporary Position Addenda: $1,215.50 Season Dates: 2/9/2026 - 5/9/2026
**ALL COACHES MUST BE A DISTRICT APPROVED VOLUNTEER. PLEASE ALSO APPLY FOR Chaperone /Community Volunteer/Volunteer Coach**
Purpose Statement The job of Assistant Coach is done for the purpose/s of designing and implementing program activities in accordance with applicable rules and regulations related to the sport; providing supervision of student athletes during all aspects of their participation in program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program; serving as a positive role model to student athletes to the community, and to various organizations. Individuals in this job classification are assigned to a position for a specific sport at the Varsity, JV/Frosh or Junior High level.
This job reports to Athletic Director
Essential Functions Administers first aid for the purpose of providing emergency care for injuries material/equipment. Assists the Head Coach and Athletic Director for the purpose of implementing and maintaining the services and programs of the Athletic Department. Assists with supervising student athletes during all applicable times for the purpose of ensuring their safety and welfare while participating in sport specific activities. Attends a variety of meetings, trainings, workshops, etc. for the purpose of receiving and conveying information related to sport. Coordinates various activities (e.g. practices, Parent Night, recognition, etc.) for the purpose of delivering services in conformance to established guidelines and/or objectives. Develops and implements practice sessions for the purpose of developing the student athletes' skills and abilities for the sport. Distributes, collects and inventories uniforms and sport specific equipment for the purpose of ensuring that all are properly stored and secured and/or returned at the end of the season. Identifies program needs for the purpose of providing recommendations of expenditures for activities, equipment, supplies, etc. that will enhance the assigned program. Implements team activities for the purpose of providing effective training and a positive and safe environment for participants. Informs student athletes on a variety of information (e.g. practice/game schedules and locations, sport rules/regulations, injury prevention, etc.) for the purpose of providing information required for participation in the sport. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. student/parent contact information, physicals, signed Code of Conduct, attendance, etc.) for the purpose of complying with District and AIA policies. Models sportsmanship and maintains appropriate conduct towards student athletes, officials, and spectators for the purpose of providing a positive and safe environment. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of ensuring compliance with AIA and District requirements. Oversees assistant coaches, student managers, volunteers, etc. for the purpose of providing direction and monitoring activities. Reports incidents and/or injuries for the purpose of maintaining personal safety of students, providing a positive and safe learning environment and adhering to District and AIA policies and procedures. Responds to inquiries from a variety of individuals (e.g. students, parents, District staff, etc.) for the purpose of providing information, assistance and/or direction regarding the specific sport. Transports student athletes for the purpose of ensuring students' arrive in a timely manner and under the supervision of a District employee.
Other Functions Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the work unit.
Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to perform single, technical tasks with a need to occasionally upgrade skills in order to meet changing job conditions. Specific skill based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: adhering to safety practices; administering first aid; operating equipment used in the sport; operating standard office equipment including using pertinent software applications; and preparing and maintaining accurate records.
KNOWLEDGE is required to perform basic math, including calculations using fractions, percents, and/or ratios; read a variety of manuals, write documents following prescribed formats, and/or present information to others; and understand complex, multi-step written and oral instructions. Specific knowledge based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: age appropriate activities; injury prevention and appropriate treatment protocols; pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; participant eligibility, attendance and discipline guidelines; public relations protocols; relevant professional standards and practices; safety practices and procedures.
ABILITY is required to schedule activities, meetings, and/or events; gather and/or collate data; and consider a wide variety of factors when using equipment. Flexibility is required to independently work with others in a wide variety of circumstances; work with data utilizing defined and similar processes; and operate equipment using defined methods. Ability is also required to work with a diversity of individuals and/or groups; work with data of varied types and/or purposes; and utilize a variety of types of job-related equipment. Problem solving is required to analyze issues and create action plans. Problem solving with data requires independent interpretation of guidelines; and problem solving with equipment is moderate to significant. Specific ability based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: communicating with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; setting priorities; working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends.
Responsibility Responsibilities include: working under limited supervision following standardized practices and/or methods; directing other persons within a small work unit; utilization of some resources from other work units is often required to perform the job's functions. There is a continual opportunity to impact the organization’s services.
Work Environment The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: occasional lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, some climbing and balancing, some stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness.
Experience: Job related experience is required. Education: High school diploma or equivalent. Equivalency: Required Testing Certificates and Licenses Continuing Educ./Training Clearances: Criminal Background Clearance Valid Arizona DPS Level I IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card www.azdps.gov/services/public/fingerprint FLSA Status Non Exempt Position Type: Athletics/Activities > Coaching

Job.JobDescription:
Dysart High School - El Mirage is looking for a Softball Assistant Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
SUMMARY: Coaches student athletic programs. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Coach student athletes in area of assigned responsibility. Organize and supervise a total sports program. Establish and maintain good rapport with students, employees, community members and vendors. Maintain a high level of ethical behavior and confidentiality. Other job related duties as assigned. EDUCATION: Valid Arizona teacher certification, or Arizona coaching certification, or Level 1 Certification issued by National Federation of High School Associations (NFHS) (This requirement is only for Head/Varsity positions) EXPERIENCE: Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport. LICENSING/CERTIFICATIONS: Obtain and maintain a valid Arizona Identity Verified Prints (IVP) Fingerprint Clearance Card (AZ IVP FPCC). Obtain and maintain First Aid/CPR certification. Completion of NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching Course. Completion of NFHS online education course entitled, "Concussion in Sports - What You Need to Know." KNOWLEDGE OF: Standard practices, methods and materials of assigned work. Occupational hazards and applicable safety principles and practices. Uses and properties of supplies and equipment. Customer service principles. Specialized equipment relevant to area of assignment. Modern office technology. SKILL IN: Following directions and meeting standards. Providing attention to detail in assignments. Comprehending reference books and manuals. Organizing and maintaining supplies. Providing customer service. Utilizing a computer and relevant software applications. Utilizing communication and interpersonal skills as applied to interaction with coworkers, supervisors, the general public and others to sufficiently exchange or convey information and to receive work direction. ADA AND OTHER REQUIREMENTS: Positions in this class require: pushing, pulling, stooping, crouching, reaching, standing, walking, fingering, grasping, feeling, talking, hearing, seeing and repetitive motions. Medium Work: Exerting up to 50 pounds of force occasionally, and/or up to 20 pounds of force frequently, and/or up to 10 pounds of force constantly to move objects. Standing for extended periods of time. Working Conditions: Work is routinely performed outdoor environment including exposure to noise and being on your feet. Requires standing in inclement weather including summer heat. The information contained in this job description is for compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and is not an exhaustive list of the duties performed by this position. Additional duties are performed by the individuals currently holding this position and additional duties may be assigned. The Dysart Unified School District is an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer in compliance with Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, Civil Rights Act Title VII of 1972, Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972, Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 and the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) of 1990. Dysart Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, sex, disability or age in employment or in any of its educational programs or in the provisions of benefits and services to students. Position Type: Part-Time Non-Benefited Salary: $3,061 Supplemental
Job.Location: Dysart High School - El Mirage, AZ
Job.Experience: Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport.

The Assistant Track Coaches at Kingman High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Track Coaches plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Kingman High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Track Coaches expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Track Coaches supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Track Coaches may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Kingman High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Job.JobTitle: Swim/Dive Coach- Apache Junction High School
Job.JobDescription:
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: * Coach student athletes in area of assigned responsibility * Organize and supervise a total sports program * Establish and maintain good rapport with students, employees, and community members * Maintain a high level of ethical behavior and confidentiality * Promote safe practices to lessen participant injuries * Establish a sense of comradery among our players through teambuilding, fundraising, service projects, etc. * Attend any school district and/or AIA meetings as directed by supervising coach or Athletic Director * Other job-related duties as assigned
QUALIFICATIONS: * Certificate verifying completion of the NFHS online “Fundamentals of Coaching” course, or approved equivalent (AIA Rule 17.1) * Certificate verifying completion of the NFHS online “Concussion in Sports - What You Need to Know” course (AIA Rule 17.1) * Current Certification in “Hands on” CPR and First Aid * Must have a Fingerprint Clearance Card with IVP # and pass background clearance check * Knowledge of essential skills, techniques, rules and safety concerns for the sport to be coached * Skill in communicating with all stakeholders * Knowledge necessary to adhere to rules set out by AIA (www.aiaonline.org) and Apache Junction Unified School District #43
EDUCATION and/or EXPERIENCE Bachelor's degree (B. A.) from four-year college or university; or one to two years related experience and/or training; or equivalent combination of education and experience. Arizona Teaching Certificate or team coach with certificated personnel.
OTHER INFORMATION: * May be required to work outside normal working hours * May be required to travel to perform work functions * Must utilize online platforms required for specific sports like MaxPreps, HUDL, and others
PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The requirements listed below are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the duties of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. * Ability to solve practical problems and deal with a variety of concrete variables in situations where only limited standardization exists. Ability to interpret a variety of instructions furnished in written, oral, diagram, or schedule form. * While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to stand, move about, sit, reach with hands and arms, handle, finger or feel, talk and hear. The employee is frequently required to bend, stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. The employee is frequently required to lift and carry up to 25 pounds. The employee is occasionally required to run, climb or balance. * Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, color vision, peripheral vision, depth perception and ability to adjust focus. * Duties may require employee to be exposed to outside weather conditions including extreme heat or cold, wind, or rain. High humidity and/or dusty conditions may also be encountered. * The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. Notice of Non-Discrimination: The Apache Junction Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of age, race, color, national origin, religion, sex (including sexual preference/identity) or disability in its employment practices.
Job.Experience: one to two years related experience and/or training; or equivalent

Job.JobDescription:
The 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) at Megan Moore is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Megan Moore, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Megan Moore operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Megan Moore with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the 25-26 NAIC Track Coach (7-8) role at Megan Moore.

Tanque Verde Unified is looking for an Administration/District Athletic Director to join our program. Apply now.
Tanque Verde Unified School District is seeking a District Athletic Director. This position provides leadership, oversight, and strategic direction for all interscholastic athletic programs serving students in grades 7-12. This position ensures athletic programs align with the educational mission of Tanque Verde Unified School District by promoting academic success, leadership development, sportsmanship, equity, safety, and community engagement. The District Athletic Director oversees staffing, budgeting, fundraising, compliance, communications, and the overall growth, organization, and visibility of District athletic programs. Essential Duties and Responsibilities Program Leadership & Administration Oversee and manage all District athletic programs for grades 7-12. Serve as the District liaison to the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) and local athletic leagues. Ensure compliance with District policies, state regulations, league rules, and governing athletic associations. Attend and actively participate in local and state athletic leadership training, meetings, and oversight activities. Participate in weekly District administrative meetings and collaborate with school and District leadership. Establish and maintain a positive, student-centered athletic culture emphasizing academic achievement, character development, leadership, and sportsmanship. Coaching Leadership & Professional Development Recruit, hire, train, evaluate, and mentor all middle school and high school athletic coaches. Provide regular coaching professional development, including scheduled meetings, training sessions, and leadership support. Conduct performance evaluations and support growth and improvement plans. Maintain consistent communication with coaching staff regarding expectations, policies, and program goals. Athletic Operations & Event Management Organize, schedule, and oversee all athletic competitions and events. Manage athletic transportation, travel logistics, and game-day operations. Oversee game-day communication, including coordination with schools, officials, transportation, and families. Ensure clear and timely travel communication for student-athletes, coaches, and parents. Student-Athlete Support & Development Manage student-athlete academic eligibility in collaboration with school administration and counseling staff. Develop and oversee student-athlete leadership development opportunities. Assist student-athletes and families with the college recruitment process, including eligibility guidance, exposure opportunities, and coordination with coaches. Advocate for equitable access and opportunities for all student-athletes. Financial Management Develop, manage, and monitor the District’s grades 7-12 athletic budget. Ensure responsible fiscal oversight, transparency, and alignment with District financial practices. Oversee equipment purchasing, inventory, and resource allocation. Fundraising, Grants & Camps Serve as the District athletic lead for capital fundraising initiatives and athletic-related grants. Collaborate with booster clubs, community partners, and District staff to secure funding for athletic facilities, equipment, and program enhancements. Organize and oversee District athletic camps, clinics, and special athletic programs. Communication, Promotion & Community Engagement Promote athletic teams, student-athletes, and coaches at the District, local, and state levels. Serve as liaison for press releases and promotion of athletic activities and achievements. Share regular written celebrations and highlights of athletic accomplishments with the District community. Conduct parent meetings and maintain open communication with families. Strengthen relationships with community organizations, media outlets, and local partners. Serve as a visible ambassador for Tanque Verde Unified School District athletics. Qualifications Required Qualifications Bachelor’s degree in education, athletic administration, sports management, or a related field. Experience in secondary school athletics as an administrator and/or coach. Knowledge of interscholastic athletic regulations, budgeting, scheduling, and program management. Must hold a valid Fingerprint Clearance Card. Strong leadership, organizational, and communication skills. Preferred Qualifications Master’s degree in educational leadership, athletic administration, or related field. Experience with fundraising, grant writing, and capital campaigns. Proven success mentoring coaches and developing comprehensive athletic programs. Job Requirements Evening, weekend, and seasonal work required. Regular attendance at athletic events, meetings, and District functions. Travel between District sites and athletic venues both in and out of town as needed. Contract Information 10.5-month administrative position. Salary and benefits commensurate with experience. The job description is intended to be generic in nature. It is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the position. It is not intended to be an exhaustive list of skills, efforts, duties, or responsibilities associated with the position.
Job.Location: Tanque Verde Unified School District Office
Job.SalaryRange: Salary and benefits commensurate with experience
Job.EmployerFullName: Tanque Verde Unified School District Office

Job.JobDescription:
Tanque Verde High School is looking for a Softball Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
JV Head Softball Coach Tanque Verde High School is seeking a JV Head Softball Coach. Previous basketball coaching experience preferred. Promote sportsmanship and foster good character in student athletes. Provide for the safety and welfare of all participants. Supervise students in practice areas, locker rooms, on the bus during trips and at all times during the season. Exercise patience, tolerance and diplomacy in relations with all players, parents, co-workers, contest officials and spectators. Interscholastic activities are an extension of the classroom and as such, your actions, behavior and interactions with our student athletes should be reflective of this.
All head coaches shall have a valid Arizona Teaching Certificate, Arizona Coaching Certificate, or have completed each of the following criteria from the National Federation of State High School Associations online at www.nfhslearn.org. * Within forty-five (45) days from date of hire: * Fundamentals of coaching * Sport specific course * First Aid/CPR *Fingerprint Clearance Card

Job.district: Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist.
Job.JobTitle: Coach Head Football (Head Football Coach – Mohave High School)
Job.JobDescription:
Mohave High School is looking for a Football Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position Description Head Football Coach – Mohave High School Qualifications: Valid Arizona teaching or substitute certificate issued by ADE. Valid IVP Fingerprint Card issued by the Arizona Department of Public Safety. Has the ability or organize and supervise a total program. Has successful coaching experience in Football. Has substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of Football. Reports to: Athletic Director Supervises: A staff of assistant coaches in conjunction with the Athletic Director. Goal: To instruct students in the fundamental skills, strategy and training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. To provide students instruction that will lead to the formation of the values of sportsmanship, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence. Duties and Responsibilities: Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic/activity policies approved by the school board and is responsible for its implementation by the entire staff of the program. Has knowledge of existing system, state, conference and region regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. Establishes and administers a ninth through twelfth grade program in Football. Plans, schedules, documents and carries out a regular organized program of practice. Plans, schedules, documents and carries out a regular organized program of practice. Establishes and/or maintains a pre-season program of interest and advocacy for football. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. Design clinic and staff meetings to insure staff awareness of overall program. At seasons end, analyzes staff effectiveness and evaluates all assistants. Evaluations to be completed within two weeks of the conclusion of the activity. Assists the Athletic Director in scheduling. Maintains necessary forms, records and paperwork as required. Oversees the safety conditions of the equipment and facilities in the activity of responsibility. Evaluates assigned officials if activity is a Varsity AIA contest on provided forms and turns in to the Athletic Director the day after the event or the following Monday. Maintains a record of team statistics and requirements for learning. Provides written training rules and any other regulations of the sport/activity to each student who is participating. Provides a copy of said guidelines to the Athletic Director. Determines discipline, delineates procedure concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contacts parents when a student is suspended or dropped. Establishes performance criteria and communicates them to the participants of the activity. Gives attention to a student participant's grades and conduct. Adheres to eligibility rules. Selects and directs student managers and statisticians. Participates in the budgeting function with the Athletic Director by recommending equipment for the next season. Recommends equipment guidelines as to type, style, color or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within budget appropriations. Is accountable for all equipment and arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment. Submits an annual inventory within two weeks of the completion of the season on the athletic/activity form. Examines locker rooms before and after practice and games, checking on general cleanliness and maintenance. Secures all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving building if custodians are not on duty. Reports any problems to maintenance.
Job.CompanyDescription: Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist. coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription: Middle School Baseball QUALIFICATIONS: 1. The head coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student-athletes through his/her instructions and actions. 2. The head coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 3. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program including coordinating with high school coaches. 4. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. 5. Has knowledge of how to effectively use GameChanger for pitch counts and rest. JOB GOAL: To instruct student-athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Additionally, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who will provide overall objectives and final evaluation SUPERVISES: In several instances, the head coach must advise, coordinate and support the staff of middle school assistant coaches and student managers in conjunction with the athletic director and respective principal. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Sanders Unified School District and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. 2. Has knowledge of existing system, state and NAIC regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. 3. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to demonstrate and promote the development of the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship in student athletes. 4. Maintains discipline in accordance with school, district, and NAIC guidelines. Gives constant attention to a student athlete’s grades and conduct. 5. Trains and informs the coaching staff and encourages professional growth through clinic attendance according to local policy. 6. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians. 7. Coordinates with the High School coach the fundamental philosophy, skills and techniques to be taught by staff. 8. Determines discipline and delineates procedures concerning due process when the enforcement of discipline is necessary and contacts parents when a student is dropped or becomes ineligible. 9. Understands the proper administrative line of command and refers all requests or grievances through proper channels. 10. The coach is aware of all public/staff/departmental meetings that require attendance. 11. Delegates specific duties, supervises implementation and, at season’s end, analyzes staff effectiveness and in collaboration with the athletic director evaluates all assistants. 12. Performs such other duties which may be assigned by the athletic director. ADMINISTRATIVE DUTIES: 1. Assists in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices and adheres to scheduled facility times. 2. Provides assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant, by his/her presence at all practices, games, and while traveling. 3. Provides documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examinations, parental consent and eligibility. 4. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. 5. Initiates programs and policies concerning injuries, medical attention and emergencies. 6. Completes paperwork on all disabling athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office within 24 hours. 7. Advise the athletic director about his/her sport and recommends policy, method or procedural changes. FINANCE AND EQUIPMENT: 1. The coach is accountable for all equipment and collects the cost of any equipment that is lost or not returned. Arranges for issuing, storing and reconditioning of equipment and submits annual inventory and current records concerning same. 2. Properly marks and identifies all equipment before issuing or storing. 3. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' offices, authorizes who may enter, issue or requisition equipment. 4. Permits the athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. 5. Examine locker rooms before and after practices and games, checking on general cleanliness of the facility. Responsible for cleanliness and maintenance of specific sport equipment. PUBLIC RELATIONS: 1. Organizes parents, coaches, players and guests for preseason meetings. 2. Promotes the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports program. 3. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with parents, officials, volunteers and fans. MUST POSSESS AN IVPP FINGERPRINT CLEARANCE CARD THRU ARIZONA DEPARTMENT OF PUBLIC SAFETY (can be obtained here): https://psp.azdps.gov/services/fccFormTriage

Flagstaff Unified is looking for a Softball Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The JV Softball Coach position at Flagstaff Unified is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the JV Softball Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The JV Softball Coach at Flagstaff Unified sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Flagstaff Unified, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The JV Softball Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The JV Softball Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the JV Softball Coach position at Flagstaff Unified is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Corona Foothills Middle School is looking for a Baseball Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Baseball Coach at Corona Foothills Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Baseball Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Corona Foothills Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Baseball Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Baseball Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Baseball Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Corona Foothills Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.Location: Corona Foothills Middle School 16705 S. Houghton Road Corona de Tucson, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Softball Coach at Margaret Steuer is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Assistant Softball Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Margaret Steuer, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Assistant Softball Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Assistant Softball Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Margaret Steuer operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Margaret Steuer with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Assistant Softball Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Assistant Softball Coach role at Margaret Steuer.

Flagstaff Unified is looking for a Baseball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
FLAGSTAFF UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT No. 1 JV Baseball Head Coach Coconino High School 2025 - 2026 School Year
Position Title: JV Baseball Head Coach Department/Section: Coconino High School Supervisor: Athletic Director/ Principal Compensation: Special Service Contract ($3,000.00)
Summary: Coconino High School is seeking a dedicated and passionate individual to lead our Junior Varsity Baseball team. The ideal candidate will possess a strong knowledge of the game, a commitment to player development, and the ability to inspire and motivate young athletes. This position requires effective communication skills, a collaborative spirit, and a focus on fostering a positive team culture while promoting sportsmanship and academic success.
Responsibilities: - Develop and implement practice plans and game strategies that enhance player skills and team performance. - Conduct regular practices and training sessions, focusing on skill development, teamwork, and physical conditioning. - Organize and manage all aspects of the JV baseball program, including scheduling games, coordinating transportation, and overseeing team logistics. - Foster a positive and inclusive team environment that encourages sportsmanship, respect, and personal growth among student-athletes. - Monitor and support the academic progress of players, ensuring they maintain eligibility and prioritize their education. - Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school administration regarding team expectations, schedules, and individual player development. - Collaborate with the varsity coaching staff to ensure alignment in training, player development, and program goals. - Assist in the recruitment and retention of student-athletes for the baseball program. - Attend all games, practices, and team meetings, maintaining a visible and supportive presence for the team. - Uphold and promote the values and mission of Coconino High School and its athletic programs.
Qualifications & Requirements: - Previous coaching experience at the high school level or higher, preferably in baseball. - Strong understanding of baseball fundamentals, strategies, and rules. - Excellent leadership and motivational skills, with the ability to connect with student-athletes. - Effective communication and interpersonal skills, both verbal and written. - Ability to work collaboratively with coaching staff, players, and parents. - Commitment to the academic and athletic development of student-athletes. - First Aid and CPR certification (or willingness to obtain). - A valid driver's license and the ability to travel to games and events as needed. - Background check clearance as required by school district policies. - IVP Fingerprint Card - NFHS Concussion in Sports and Fundamentals of Coaching Certificates

Flagstaff Unified is looking for a Softball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Summary: Coconino High School is seeking passionate and dedicated individuals to join our coaching staff as Assistant Softball Coaches. This position offers an exciting opportunity to work with student-athletes in a supportive environment that promotes skill development, teamwork, and personal growth. The ideal candidates will have a strong understanding of the game of softball and a commitment to fostering a positive and inclusive atmosphere for all players.
Responsibilities: - Assist the head coach in planning and implementing practice sessions to develop players' skills and team strategies. - Provide individualized instruction and feedback to student-athletes to enhance their performance. - Help organize and supervise team activities, including games, practices, and team meetings. - Assist in the recruitment and retention of student-athletes for the softball program. - Collaborate with the head coach and other staff to create a positive team culture and promote sportsmanship. - Monitor the academic progress of student-athletes and encourage a balance between academics and athletics. - Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school administration regarding team policies, schedules, and expectations. - Support the head coach in maintaining equipment and facilities, ensuring a safe environment for all participants. - Attend coaching clinics and workshops to stay updated on the latest coaching techniques and strategies. - Uphold the values and mission of Coconino High School and promote the overall well-being of student-athletes.
Qualifications & Requirements: - Previous coaching experience or a strong background in softball, either as a player or coach. - Knowledge of the rules, strategies, and techniques of softball. - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes. - Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage multiple tasks effectively. - Commitment to fostering an inclusive and supportive environment for all team members. - First Aid/CPR certification is preferred but not required. - Availability to work evenings and weekends during the softball season. - A valid Arizona driver's license and the ability to pass a background check. - NFHS Course Certifications: Fundamentals of Coaching and Concussion in Sports

Job.CompanyDescription: Mesa County Valley D51 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Trinidad High School is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Football Coach at Trinidad High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Head Football Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Trinidad High School, the Head Football Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Head Football Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Trinidad High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Head Football Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Sargent School District is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The High School Track Coach role at Sargent School District is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the High School Track Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Sargent School District, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The High School Track Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Sargent School District, the High School Track Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the High School Track Coach at Sargent School District is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.SalaryRange: $3,015.00 on the Extra Duty Salary Schedule

Job.JobDescription:
Colorado Springs School District 11 is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Baseball Head Coach role at Matthias Wilson is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Baseball Head Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Matthias Wilson are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Baseball Head Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Baseball Head Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Baseball Head Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Matthias Wilson, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Baseball Head Coach at Matthias Wilson, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Mountain View High School is looking for a Football Full time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Football Coach: Multiple Positions position at Mountain View High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Assistant Football Coach: Multiple Positions is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Mountain View High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Assistant Football Coach: Multiple Positions encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Assistant Football Coach: Multiple Positions also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Assistant Football Coach: Multiple Positions role at Mountain View High School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Estes Park Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Assistant Track Coach role at Estes Park Middle School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Middle School Assistant Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Estes Park Middle School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Middle School Assistant Track Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Estes Park Middle School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Middle School Assistant Track Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Middle School Assistant Track Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Middle School Assistant Track Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Estes Park Middle School.
Job.Experience: Experience in coaching preferred

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Estes Park Middle School is looking for a Soccer Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach at Estes Park Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Estes Park Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Middle School Girls Assistant Soccer Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Estes Park Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Sheridan School D2 is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB TITLE: Head Football Coach LOCATION: Sheridan High School WORK YEAR: Seasonal WORK STATUS: Classified, Exempt REPORTS TO: Athletic/Activities Director
THE FOLLOWING RESPONSIBILITIES RELATE TO HEAD COACHES/ASSISTANT COACHES OR BOTH: Plan and complete recruiting activities to maximize student involvement. Attend all Athletic Director meetings. Conduct parent meetings with clear, documented expectations for players and parents. Increase the number of participants through effective recruiting. Develop life skills in student athletes. Create practice plans specifically designed for incremental skill development. Monitor academic progress and student eligibility. Teach and encourage sportsmanship and integrity. Submit rosters in a timely manner. Maintain professional behavior during practices and games. Head coaches must establish the roles of all assistant coaches. Conduct effective practices to develop strength, endurance, and athletic skills. Conduct activities to build individual participants into a cohesive team. Provide appropriate, systematic feedback to all student athletes Build and improve relationships among peer coaches and teachers. Build and improve relationships with other sports teams in the school district at the same or different levels. Ongoing professional development to improve overall student/athlete skill. Responsible for acquisition, distribution, maintenance and repair of all equipment and uniforms. Responsible for raising and/or acquiring the necessary funds for program success Head coaches should successfully motivate and use the talents of assistant coaches in the program. Demonstrate appropriate concern for visiting teams and officials. Skillfully use First Aid and CPR training to prevent, minimize and treat predictable injuries. Complete required reports and requisitions. Ensure that all student fees are collected and/or resolved prior to first competition. Get team pictures taken.
QUALIFICATIONS: Knowledge in specific sport, coaching, and training techniques. Ability to work as an integral part of the coaching staff. The head coach and assistant coach will be responsible for teaching young men and women appropriate skills, and also developing integrity, hard work, and responsibility in these young men and women. These coaching positions will be filled between August and April of each school year and will be renewed annually. Coaches will be responsible for the maintenance of equipment, uniforms, etc. High School coaches must be CHSAA certified.
CLEARANCE: Must clear background check through the CBI and the FBI through Fingerprint submission.
STIPEND: Stipend placement will be on Level 1 of the Additive Salary Schedule. A full copy of the Additive Salary Schedule is located at http://www.ssd2.org/SalarySchedules.aspx.
APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS: All interested and qualified applicants must complete an online application at www.ssd2.org. Incomplete applications and resumes received in lieu of applications will not be considered. Selected applicants will be contacted for an interview. This position will close once the vacancy has been filled.
