Job.JobTitle: Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026
Job.JobDescription:
Sonoran Trails Middle School - Phoenix is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026 position at Sonoran Trails Middle School - Phoenix provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026 prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Sonoran Trails Middle School - Phoenix, the Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026 works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026 encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Girls Middle School Track & Field Coach - STMS - starts Feb. 2026 at Sonoran Trails Middle School - Phoenix is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Location: Sonoran Trails Middle School - Phoenix, Arizona

Job.JobDescription: SUMMARY: Under basic supervision, collaborates with physicians to optimize student athlete activity and participation in athletics. Works to create a safe playing environment for students by monitoring and controlling environmental risks. PRIMARY DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Essential functions, as defined under the Americans with Disabilities Act, may include any of the following representative duties, knowledge, and skills. This is not a comprehensive listing of all functions and duties performed by incumbents of this class; employees may be assigned duties which are not listed below; reasonable accommodations may be made as required. The job description is subject to change at any time by the employer. Essential duties and responsibilities may include, but are not limited to, the following: Provides guidance to coaches and student athletes regarding injury prevention and daily health habits. Provides services to both teams during home events and travels with athletic teams as needed. Provides water, supplies, and athletic taping for practice(s) and competitions. Provides parents regarding athletic health care and treatment. Supervises the utilization of the facilities arid equipnient needed by the students and staff for any rehabilitation or treatment of athletic related injuries. Assists in the selection and fitting of protective equipment, including but not limited to taping, pads, and braces. Supervises the clearance of injured athletes prior to, and during the sports seasons. Manages the ordering of supplies and equipment pertaining to the athletic training room. Maintains athletic training room inventory and ensures equipment is in good working order. Stores arid inventories supplies and equipment. Allocates each team appropriate medical equipment and supplies. Provides the Athletic Coordinator with an annual budget estimate for supplies and equipment. Files all necessary reports associated with athletic injuries and incidents. Maintains daily treatment log. Promotes a positive and competitive attitude throughout the entire athletic department and models an athletic lifestyle. Completes other duties as assigned or required. MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS: Education and Experience: Bachelor's Degree in Athletic Training; AND 750 hours of clinical rotations; OR an equivalent combination of education, training, and experience. Required License and Certification: Arizona Fingerprint Clearance Card. Arizona Athletic Trainer Certification. First aid, CPR, and AED Certification. Required Knowledge of: Arizona Interscholastic Association Bylaws. National Athletic Trainers Association rules, regulations, and code of ethics. District and Department policies and procedures. Required Skills in: Assessing, identifying, and treating various athletic injuries. Establishing and maintaining cooperative working relationships with coworkers and other individuals in contact during the course of work; communicating clearly and concisely. Coordinating and directing athletic events. Practicing methods involved in providing quality customer service. May involve occasional lifting and carrying up to 50 pounds or lifting student athletes
Job.Location: Rio Rico High School - Rio Rico, Arizona
Job.Experience: Bachelor's Degree in Athletic Training; AND 750 hours of clinical rotations; OR an equivalent combination of education, training, and experience.

Job.JobTitle: SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12)
Job.JobDescription:
The SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12) role at Megan Moore is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12) is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Megan Moore, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12) sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Megan Moore, the SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12) is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the SY 26-27 High School Flag Football Coach (9-12) at Megan Moore is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Page Unified is looking for a Golf coach to join our program. Apply now.
The SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) role at Megan Moore emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Megan Moore, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Megan Moore at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The SY 26-27 High School Golf Coach (9-12) must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Megan Moore.

TITLE: HIGH SCHOOL AND MIDDLE SCHOOL HEAD COACH
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 2. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. 3. The head coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 4. The head coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student athletes through his/her actions. 5. Must secure and maintain IVP fingerprint clearance card from AZ DPS. 6. Must secure and maintain First Aid/CPR certification. 7. Must secure and maintain NFHS Coaching Certification. 8. Must secure and maintain NFHS Concussion Certification. 9. Must secure and maintain NFHS Heat Illness Prevention Certification. 10. Must secure and maintain NFHS Bullying, Hazing & Inappropriate Behaviors Certification.
ADDITIONAL QUALIFICATIONS FOR CHEER/SPIRITLINE HEAD COACHES: 1. Must possess a valid American Association of Cheerleading Coaches and Advisors (AACCA) certification. 2. Must have successfully completed the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) Information Course. 3. Must have successfully completed the NFHS Rules Interpretation Course. 4. Must have successfully completed the NFHS Cheer Safety Exam with a passing score of 75% or higher.
JOB GOALS: The Head Coach carries out the objectives of the sports program as directed by the Varsity Head Coach or Athletic Director/Coordinator as applicable. The Head Coach also instructs athletes with regard to individual and team fundamentals, strategy and physical training, while instructing and demonstrating behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self discipline, self confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes.
PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: The following tasks are essential for this position. This list is not inclusive of all responsibilities. Individuals may not perform all of these tasks, or may perform similar related tasks not listed. 1. Complies with all HIPAA and FERPA related confidentiality requirements. 2. Develops positive relations with students, parents, school staff and the public, including members of the media, boosters, officials, players, other coaches, school administration, and fans. 3. Serves as a positive role model for students, coaching staff, and the community. 4. Demonstrates knowledge of proven coaching theories, strategies, and techniques and works with student athletes of varying abilities, socio-economic, and cultural backgrounds to effectively implement these attributes. 5. Instills commitment, discipline, sportsmanship, respect, and teamwork to all athletes and coaching staff. 6. The Head Coach performs other related duties as assigned by the Varsity High School Head Coach and/or Athletic Director/Coordinator as applicable.
MINIMUM KNOWLEDGE, SKILL AND ABILITY: Knowledge of: 1. AIA rules, policies (high school level only), and athletic department handbook. 2. Effective coaching theories, strategies, and techniques appropriate for school athletes. 3. Federal and state laws, rules and regulations governing education and sports program implementation. 4. Budget preparation and management. 5. District's constituents (i.e., boosters, etc.) 6. Supervision and evaluation of coaching staff. 7. Developing and evaluating extra-curricular programs.
Skill and Ability to: 1. Motivate student athletes to participate and sustain their interests in the athletic program while excelling in instructional curriculum. 2. Create beneficial relationships and communicate effectively with supervisors, staff, students, vendors, other coaches and community members. 3. Apply leadership techniques. 4. Implement educational policies and procedures. 5. Supervise and evaluate coaching staff. 6. Analyze and problem solve. 7. Forecast trends; plan and implement goals. 8. Develop and manage athletic program budgets as needed.
Physical Requirements and Working Conditions: The physical requirements described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. 1. Regularly required to talk or listen. 2. Frequently required to stand for long periods and to walk long distances. 3. Occasionally required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; climb or balance; and stoop, kneel, crouch, or crawl. 4. Must occasionally lift and/or move up to 25 pounds. 5. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, distance vision, peripheral vision, depth perception, and ability to adjust focus.
REPORTS TO: Varsity Head Coach and/or Athletic Director/Coordinator
FLSA STATUS: Non-Exempt Position Type: Part-Time Salary: $4,000 to $4,000 Stipend
Job Requirements High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Contact Information Christine DeCarlo Human Resources Specialist 902 W Main St Payson, Arizona 85547 Phone: 9284742070 Email: hr@pusd10.org

Job.district: Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist.
Job.JobDescription:
Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist. is looking for a Football Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Coach Head Football at Colorado River Union High School District is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Coach Head Football are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Colorado River Union High School District use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Coach Head Football recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Coach Head Football collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Coach Head Football maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Colorado River Union High School District. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.EmployerFullName: Colorado River Union High School District
Job.CompanyDescription: Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist. coaching jobs
Job.CompanyName: Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist.

Job.JobTitle: MIDDLE SCHOOL CO-HEAD (MALE) HEAD COACH TRACK AND FIELD
Job.JobDescription:
Sanders Unified is looking for a Track & Field Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS: 1. The co-coach must serve as an appropriate role model for student-athletes through his instructions and actions. 2. The co-coach must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and, at the same time, must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. 3. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program including coordinating with high school coaches. 4. Has previous successful coaching experience in assigned sport. JOB GOAL: To instruct student-athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Additionally, the coach must instruct and demonstrate behaviors that lead to socially acceptable character development, self-discipline, self-confidence, and pride of accomplishment in the student athletes. REPORTS TO: The athletic director, who will provide overall objectives and final evaluation SUPERVISES: In several instances, the co-coach must coordinate with his counterpart and support the staff of middle school student managers in conjunction with the athletic director. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Has a thorough knowledge of all the athletic policies approved by the Sanders Unified School District and is responsible for their implementation by the entire staff of the sports program. 2. Has knowledge of existing system, state and NAIC regulations; implements same consistently and interprets them for staff. 3. Serves as a mentor to each athlete to demonstrate and promote the development of the character traits of trustworthiness, respect, responsibility, fairness, caring, and citizenship in student athletes. 4. Maintains discipline in accordance with school, district, and NAIC guidelines. Gives constant attention to a student athlete's grades and conduct. 5. Trains and informs the coaching staff and encourages professional growth through clinic attendance according to local policy. 6. Directs student managers, assistants and statisticians.

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Volleyball Coach role at Kathy Frausel is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Boys Volleyball Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Kathy Frausel are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Boys Volleyball Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Boys Volleyball Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Boys Volleyball Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Kathy Frausel, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Boys Volleyball Coach at Kathy Frausel, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Job.JobDescription:
Flagstaff High School is looking for a Baseball Temporary to join our program. Apply now.
The JV Baseball Head Coach at Flagstaff High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the JV Baseball Head Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Flagstaff High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The JV Baseball Head Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The JV Baseball Head Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the JV Baseball Head Coach at Flagstaff High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.Location: Flagstaff High School 400 W Elm Ave Flagstaff, AZ 86001

Job.JobDescription:
Old Vail Middle School is looking for a Volleyball Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Volleyball Coach position at Old Vail Middle School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Boys Volleyball Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Boys Volleyball Coach at Old Vail Middle School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Old Vail Middle School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Boys Volleyball Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Boys Volleyball Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Boys Volleyball Coach position at Old Vail Middle School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.Location: Old Vail Middle School 13299 E. Colossal Cave Road Vail, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
The 2026/27 WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling at ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the 2026/27 WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL, the 2026/27 WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The 2026/27 WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents ALCHESAY HIGH SCHOOL well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The 2026/27 WRESTLING COACH - Girls Wrestling follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Flagstaff Unified is looking for a Baseball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Track Coach role at Kathy Frausel emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Track Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Kathy Frausel, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Track Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Kathy Frausel at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Track Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Track Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Track Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Kathy Frausel.
Job.Location: Old Vail Middle School, 13299 E. Colossal Cave Road Vail, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
Air Academy High School is looking for a Tennis Part time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach at Air Academy High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Assistant Girls Tennis Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Air Academy High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Assistant Girls Tennis Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Air Academy High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.Location: Air Academy High School, Colorado Springs, CO

Job.JobDescription:
Discovery Canyon Campus High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Part time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Cheer Coach role at Discovery Canyon Campus High School is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Assistant Cheer Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Discovery Canyon Campus High School, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Assistant Cheer Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Discovery Canyon Campus High School, the Assistant Cheer Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Assistant Cheer Coach at Discovery Canyon Campus High School is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.Location: Discovery Canyon Campus High School, Colorado Springs, CO

Job.JobDescription:
Pine Creek High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Part time to join our program. Apply now.
The Dance/Poms Coach at Pine Creek High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the Dance/Poms Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Pine Creek High School, the Dance/Poms Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The Dance/Poms Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Pine Creek High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The Dance/Poms Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.
Job.Location: Pine Creek High School, Colorado Springs, CO

Job.JobDescription:
Discovery Canyon Campus High School is looking for a Soccer Part time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach at Discovery Canyon Campus High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Discovery Canyon Campus High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Girls Soccer Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Girls Soccer Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Discovery Canyon Campus High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.Location: Discovery Canyon Campus High School, Colorado Springs, CO

Widefield D3 is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Coach at Widefield School District 3 is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Athletic Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Widefield School District 3, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Athletic Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Athletic Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Widefield School District 3 operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Widefield School District 3 with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Athletic Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Athletic Coach role at Widefield School District 3.
Job.Location: 1820 MAIN ST, Colorado Springs, CO, 80911-1152, United States

West Grand District 1-Jt is looking for a Volleyball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: High School Volleyball Head Coach Reports to: Jr/Sr High School Athletic & Activities Director Salary: TBD by years of experience and education Work year: Seasonal Job Summary: Join our staff as the high school volleyball head coach and lead a team of highly dedicated student athletes. The head volleyball coach promotes an appreciation of physical fitness, teamwork and sportsmanship. Qualifications: • High school diploma or equivalent. • A teacher with a current teaching license is preferred but not a requirement. • Current CPR and AED Certification. • Previous coaching experience in this sport at the high school level is desirable. • Have a valid driver’s license, be able to drive, and qualify under the district’s insurance carrier when driving a district vehicle. • Desire to continue career improvement. • Excellent oral and verbal communication skills. • Proven problem-solving skills. Essential Functions: • Coordinate and supervise Volleyball program and team. • Promote an appreciation of physical fitness, teamwork, and sportsmanship. • Design a training program and game program. • Support the value of an education. • Help students achieve gains in athletic skill and team performance. • Maintain good public relations for the Volleyball program • Ensure that health and safety precautions are observed during all activities. • Immediately report all major injuries incurred during practice sessions or competition to the Activities and Athletic Director and Principal. • Ensure proper purchasing, inventory, and care of equipment. • Directs and supervises instructional and conditioning camps throughout the year. • Teach fundamentals for all aspects of this sport. • Communicate with parents and the community to promote this sport. • Follow school, league, district, and CHSAA regulations and policies. • Other responsibilities as assigned.
Questions regarding this position, please contact Josh Pedersen, Jr/Sr High School Athletic & Activities Director, pedersenj@wgsd.us or 970-724-3425
Questions regarding the application process, please contact Lacey Paeglow, Human Resources at paeglowl@wgsd.us or 970-724-1006
Job.SalaryRange: TBD by years of experience and education

Job.JobDescription:
Kenai Peninsula Borough is looking for a Co-Curricular to join our program. Apply now.
Email To A Friend Print Version
Asst. Hockey Coach
Job # 6023 Date Posted: 10/18/2025
Location:
Kenai Central High
Date of Availability:
ASAP
Date Closing:
Open Until Filled
POSITION: Assistant Hockey Coach – Kenai Central High School
POSITION SUMMARY: To assist the head coach with the organization and implementation of the sport program.
REPORTS TO: Kenai Central High School Athletic Director
MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS:
Prior hockey coaching experience.
High school diploma or equivalent.
Ability to work cooperatively with students, coaches, and administrators.
Ability to follow verbal and written instructions.
Proficient in the rules and fundamentals of hockey.
Must be able to meet the physical demands of the sport.
Must be able to attend practices and competitions as scheduled by the head coach.
Must possess or be willing to obtain an Alaska coaching certification.
Must have a valid CPR and First Aid certification, or the ability to obtain one.
Must have successfully completed the NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching, Concussion in Sports, Sudden Cardiac Arrest, and Protecting Students from Abuse courses.
PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS:
Experience coaching hockey at the high school level.
Knowledge of KPB and ASAA policies and procedures.
DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
Assist the head coach in all aspects of the hockey program.
Attend all practices, meetings, and competitions as directed by the head coach.
Help supervise student-athletes during practices, games, and travel.
Adhere to all KPB and ASAA rules and regulations.
Promote sportsmanship and positive behavior among student-athletes.
Communicate effectively with student-athletes, parents, and school staff.
Assist with equipment management and inventory.
Perform other duties as assigned by the head coach or athletic director.
COMPENSATION: The rate of pay for this position is according to the negotiated agreement.
APPLICATION PROCESS: Interested applicants must complete an online application through the KPB School District website.
Kenai Peninsula Borough School District is an Equal Opportunity Employer.
It is the policy of the Kenai Peninsula Borough School District to not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, age, or disability in its employment practices as required by Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, as amended; Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972; the Age Discrimination in Employment Act of 1967, as amended; and Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, as amended.
Job.Experience: Prior hockey coaching experience

JOB DESCRIPTION: SOFTBALL HEAD COACH PART TIME
Qualifications: - Current Alabama Teacher's Certification (preferred) or classified dependent upon qualifications of the person holding the position - Previous Experience in sports as a participant and/or coach (preferred) but not required - Completion of all requirements set forth by the AHSAA Coaches' Education Program - Must have or be willing to obtain CDL - Substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of softball and a willingness to examine new theories and procedures
Essential Duties: - To instruct student-athletes in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical conditioning necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success - To work with the athletic director in accomplishing the mission and goals of the athletic department - Instruct athletes in fundamentals, develop character, model expected behavior
HOW TO APPLY: All applicants should complete the State of Alabama on-line application and attach it to the specific job number in order to be eligible for hire. SPECIAL NOTE: For employees starting work after the beginning date of the work term, the salary will be pro-rated and based on the number of contract days left in the school year, divided by the remaining months in the contract year.

Empire High School is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Throws Track & Field Coach position at Empire High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Throws Track & Field Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Throws Track & Field Coach at Empire High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Empire High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Throws Track & Field Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Throws Track & Field Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Throws Track & Field Coach position at Empire High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
