Mountainside High School Wittmann is looking for a Basketball Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
JOB TITLE: MHS Basketball Coach - Boys JV EMPLOYMENT TERM: Season consists of approximately 10 weeks and a conference tournament that may require road travel. SUPERVISOR: Athletic Director CLASSIFICATION: Coaching SALARY: Stipend Based DESCRIPTION Coaching is a part-time position and is responsible for the planning, promotion and administration of a quality basketball program. Coach will plan and promote athletic practices, schedule transportation, coordinate fundraisers when needed, and other team events. Coach will enforce all school, conference and Arizona Interscholastic Association rules and regulations. The primary focus of the season will be to improve the middle school basketball program and to create a strong foundation of skills and leadership for participants. Coach will stress Pursuing Victory with Honor principles during practices and games. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND/OR RESPONSIBILITIES Must be available to be at practices and games. Use effective teaching and coaching methods to include demonstrations, knowledge of rules and concepts. Must have the ability to accept and carryout assigned program responsibilities. Must communicate effectively with people from varied social, educational, and economic backgrounds. Must be able to work without constant supervision, make decisions, and have good judgment. Must have a high level of professional appearance and demeanor. Must be mature, dependable, and have a willingness to help others and serve as a positive role model for students. Must model and reinforce Pursuing with Honor’s “Six Pillars of Character”. Coach is responsible for making sure eligibility policy is followed. Coach is responsible for care and inventory of equipment uniforms. Coach is responsible to ensure every student athlete has had a physical before season starts. MINIMUM EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE Obtain and maintain a valid Arizona Fingerprint Clearance Card. Obtain and maintain First Aide/CPR Certification. Coaching experience. PREFERRED EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE Previous successful coaching experience in the assigned sport. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS AND ABILITIES KNOWLEDGE OF Standard practices, methods and materials of assigned work. Occupational hazards and applicable safety principles and practices. Specialized equipment relevant assigned sport. Technology related to assigned sport. SKILLED IN Following directions and meeting standards. Providing attention to detail in assignments. Comprehending reference books and manuals. Organizing and maintaining supplies. Providing customer service. Utilizing a computer and relevant software applications. Utilizing communication and interpersonal skills as applied to interaction with coworkers, supervisors, the general public and others to sufficiently exchange or convey information and to receive work direction. Position Type: Part-Time Salary: $700 Stipend
Job.Experience: Coaching experience.

Job.JobDescription:
The Coach Assistant Track (BMHS) position at Clairinda Weatherwax provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Coach Assistant Track (BMHS) prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Clairinda Weatherwax, the Coach Assistant Track (BMHS) works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Coach Assistant Track (BMHS) encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Coach Assistant Track (BMHS) at Clairinda Weatherwax is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Location: Bradshaw Mountain High School West - Prescott Valley, Arizona
Job.Experience: 1 year preferred
Job.CompanyDescription: Humboldt Unified School Dist. coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Safford Unified is looking for a Part-Time to join our program. Apply now.
JOB TITLE: Volunteer Coach REPORTS TO: Athletic Director/Principal SUPERVISES: Assistant coaches and athletes in the program JOB DESCRIPTION: To provide each athlete an opportunity to participate in athletics and develop physical skills, a sense of worth and competence, knowledge and understanding of the sport and principles of fair play. SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. Determine athletic eligibility per Athletic Director request. 2. Maintains inventory control of all assigned equipment. 3. Supervises and instructs athletes during practice, competition, in locker rooms, and on activity buses. 4. Becomes knowledgeable of the fundamentals and rules of the sport being coached. 5. Supervises the care and treatment of athletic injuries. 6. Keeps parents, students, faculty and local media informed. 7. Attends District meetings and clinics. 8. Keeps updated by reading current literature and/or attending clinics when deemed appropriate. 9. Maintains all equipment used in assigned sport according to health and safety standards. 10. Recommends, supervises and evaluates assistant coaches. 11. Carries out coaching duties in harmony with all school, athletic association and conference policies. 12. Builds and submits an annual budget. 13. Helps maintain and secure all facilities (gym, locker rooms, training and equipment rooms including doors and gates, etc.) as applicable. 14. Conducts self in an honorable, professional manner. 15. Conducts an annual parent night in which goals, rules, etc. are communicated to parents or, with administrative approval, utilizes an appropriate written communication. (Team Handbook) 16. Conferences with parents, teachers, and the Principal as needed regarding an athlete's behavior and academic performance. 17. Serves as a positive role model for the athletes. 18. Teaches Pursuing Victory With Honor lifestyle issues ranging from dehydration to tobacco use. 19. Makes sure all District and school athletic codes are complied with. Reports violators to the appropriate administrator. 20. Is certified in CPR and completes the new NFHS fundamentals of coaching course. 21. Performs other tasks relative to the program as assigned by the administration. Position Type: Part-Time Job Categories: Athletics & Activities > Coaching

Job.JobTitle: Coach Asst Football (Asst Football Coach - Defensive Coordinator / Offensive Coordinator/Quarterbacks)
Job.JobDescription:
Colorado River Union High Sch. Dist. is looking for a Football Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Asst Football Coach Mohave High School Defensive Coordinator Stipend : $2,300.00 per year Job description: Assistant Football Coach - Offensive Coordinator/Quarterbacks Job Summary: We are seeking a passionate and experienced Sports Coach to join our team. As a Sports Assistant Coach, you will be responsible to assist the head coach in teaching and coaching football sports activities to students. This is an exciting opportunity to make a positive impact on the lives of young athletes. Must haves: You must have an IVP Fingerprint Card issued by the Arizona Department of Public Safety. Coaching Certificates: Concussion in Sports, First Aid, Health & Safety and Fundamentals of Coaching Driver License Social Security Card Responsibilities: - Assist in planning and delivering engaging sports coaching sessions for students in Football. - Teach fundamental skills, techniques, and strategies of the respective sport - Provide individualized instruction and feedback to help students improve their performance - Create a safe and inclusive environment for all participants - Manage behavior and maintain discipline during training sessions - Develop training programs and practice schedules - Organize and supervise team practices, games and tournaments - Collaborate with the head coach and staff members to ensure effective program delivery - Monitor student progress and provide regular updates to parents or guardians Experience: - Previous experience in sports coaching or teaching is required - Strong knowledge and expertise in one or more sports disciplines. - Experience working with children or students of different age groups - Ability to effectively communicate with students, parents, and staff members - Excellent organizational skills to plan practices and manage schedules - Knowledge of behavior management techniques Job Type: Seasonal Schedule: After school Work Location: In person Location: Mohave High School, Bullhead City, AZ 86442

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Volleyball Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
The Boys Volleyball Coach at Esmond Station K-8 is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Boys Volleyball Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Esmond Station K-8 use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Boys Volleyball Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Boys Volleyball Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Boys Volleyball Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Esmond Station K-8. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.Location: Esmond Station K-8, 9400 S. Atterbury Wash Way Tucson, AZ 85747

Job.JobDescription:

Job Details Job ID: 5444258 Application Deadline: Posted until filled Posted: Nov 18, 2025 7:00 AM (UTC) Starting Date: Immediately Job Description GENERAL RESPONSIBILITIES: Supports District schools and principals in their effort to foster an optimal educational environment for students. To instruct student athletes in fundamental skills, strategy and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. Students shall also receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
The success of athletic programs has a strong influence on the community's image of the entire system. The public exposure is a considerable responsibility and community/parent pressure for winning performance is taxing, but must not over-ride the objectives of good sportsmanship and good mental health. The position includes other unusual aspects such as extended time, risk injury factor and due process predicaments
It is the express intent of this job description to give sufficient guidance to function. In cases not specifically covered, it shall be assumed that a coach shall exercise common sense and good judgment.

Boys Basketball Assistant Coach
Description: Previous sport-specific experience preferred. Promote sportsmanship and foster good character in student athletes. Provide for the safety and welfare of all participants. Supervise students in practice areas, locker rooms, on the bus during trips and at all times during the season. Exercise patience, tolerance, and diplomacy in relations with all players, parents, coworkers, contest officials and spectators. Interscholastic activities are an extension of the classroom and as such, your actions, behavior, and interactions with our student athletes should be reflective of this. Previous basketball coaching experience is required.

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Baseball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Baseball Coach role at Esmond Station K-8 is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Baseball Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Esmond Station K-8, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Baseball Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Esmond Station K-8, the Baseball Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Baseball Coach at Esmond Station K-8 is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.
Job.Location: Esmond Station K-8, 9400 S. Atterbury Wash Way Tucson, AZ 85747

Job.JobDescription:
Old Vail Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Track Coach at Old Vail Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Assistant Track Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Old Vail Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Assistant Track Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Assistant Track Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Old Vail Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Old Vail Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Assistant Track Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Assistant Track Coach role at Old Vail Middle School.
Job.Location: Old Vail Middle School, 13299 E. Colossal Cave Road Vail, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
Cienega High School is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Boys Volleyball Coach at Cienega High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Boys Volleyball Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Cienega High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Boys Volleyball Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Boys Volleyball Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Boys Volleyball Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Cienega High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.Location: Cienega High School, 12775 E. Mary Ann Cleveland Way Vail, AZ 85641

Job.JobDescription:
Coconino High School is looking for a Volleyball Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Summary Coconino High School is seeking a dedicated and enthusiastic individual to lead our varsity volleyball program as the Head Coach. This role is pivotal in developing student-athletes both on and off the court, fostering a competitive and positive team environment. The ideal candidate will possess a strong knowledge of volleyball, excellent leadership skills, and a passion for mentoring young athletes. The Head Coach will work closely with players, parents, and school administration to promote the sport and ensure the success of the program.
Responsibilities - Develop and implement a comprehensive training program that enhances the skills and performance of student-athletes. - Organize and conduct daily practices, ensuring a focus on skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. - Plan and manage the varsity volleyball schedule, including practices, games, and tournaments. - Recruit and evaluate potential team members, fostering an inclusive and supportive team culture. - Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school staff regarding team expectations, schedules, and progress. - Monitor the academic performance and personal development of student-athletes, promoting a balanced approach to athletics and education. - Collaborate with the athletic department to ensure compliance with school policies, league regulations, and safety protocols. - Analyze game performance and provide constructive feedback to players to enhance their skills and understanding of the game. - Foster relationships with the community and promote the volleyball program through outreach and engagement initiatives. - Attend coaching clinics and workshops to stay updated on best practices and trends in volleyball coaching.
Requirements - NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching and Concussion in Sports Certifications - Previous coaching experience at the high school or collegiate level, particularly in volleyball. - Strong knowledge of volleyball techniques, strategies, and rules. - Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. - Ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes to achieve their best.

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Track & Field Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Track Coach at Kathy Frausel is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Assistant Track Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Kathy Frausel, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Assistant Track Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Assistant Track Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Kathy Frausel operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Kathy Frausel with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Assistant Track Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Assistant Track Coach role at Kathy Frausel.

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Baseball Coach at Dean Nourse is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Assistant Baseball Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Dean Nourse use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Assistant Baseball Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Assistant Baseball Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Assistant Baseball Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Dean Nourse. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.
Job.Location: Rincon Vista Middle School, 10770 E. Bilby Road, Tucson, AZ 85747

Job.JobDescription:
Vail Unifiied is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Cheer Coach role at Demencio Solorzano is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Head Cheer Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At Demencio Solorzano, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Head Cheer Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At Demencio Solorzano, the Head Cheer Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Head Cheer Coach at Demencio Solorzano is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobDescription:
Pine Creek High School is looking for a Baseball Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Description Summary: Responsible for assisting the head coach with organizing the Baseball program at the high school level. Provides the best possible learning experience for student athletes and prepares them for competition. Promotes sportsmanship and works cooperatively with students, other coaches, parents, and administrators.
Essential Duties and Responsibilities:
* Adheres to district athletic and league policies and procedures. * Assists in the planning and organization of practices and games. * Attends all practices and games. * Teaches fundamental skills of the sport. * Follows safety procedures and injury protocol. * Maintains and inventory of equipment and supplies. * Supervises and monitors student athletes at all times. * Serves as a role model for student athletes. * Encourages sportsmanship and team unity. * Maintains positive and professional working relationships with students, coaches, parents, and administrators. * Other duties as assigned.
Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:
* Knowledge of the sport. * Ability to work with high school age student athletes. * Ability to communicate effectively with students, parents, coaches, and administrators. * Ability to manage and supervise student athletes. * Ability to handle and maintain confidential information. * Ability to work as part of a team.
Physical Demands: The following are some of the physical demands commonly associated with this position. They are included for informational purposes and are not all-inclusive. All physical demands, if listed, are considered essential functions
* Uses eyes, hands, and finger coordination enabling the use of equipment and writing utensils * Typical demands require staff to lift and carry up to 20 pounds, and may be higher for some assignments depending on position and student need; climb stairs, ladders, and/or stools; reach, hold, grasp and turn objects; use fingers to operate computer keyboards; feel the shape, size and temperature of objects
Cognitive Functions: The following are some of the cognitive functions commonly associated with this position. They are included for informational purposes and are not all-inclusive. The staff member may be required to analyze, communicate, compare, compile, compute, coordinate, copy, evaluate, instruct, negotiate, synthesize, reason, and use interpersonal skills.
Required Qualifications: ASEP Certification - American Sport Education Program, CHSAA Certification - Colorado High School Activities Association, High school graduate -high school diploma or equivalent, NFHS Coaching Fundamentals Certification - National Federation of State High Schools
Preferred Qualifications: Teacher License: Initial - Colorado Department of Education, Teacher License: Professional - Colorado Department of Education

Interviews are site-based and not conducted by the Human Resources office. If you have questions regarding the status of an open or recently closed position, you may contact the site where the vacancy is located
ABOUT DISTRICT 49 School District 49, the fastest growing district in the Pikes Peak region, hosts 23 exceptional schools and programs within four distinct innovation zones, providing preK-12-aligned curriculum, online and alternative programming and homeschool enrichment. Home to more than 19,000 students and 1,500 staff members, District 49 aims to be the best choice to learn, work and lead. As one of Colorado's only two recognized Districts of Innovation, District 49 is a challenging and stimulating place to lead. As part of our commitment to innovate, we host a robust portfolio of conventional, charter, online, and blended schools, including homeschool support as well as career and technical education.
We are passionate about providing individualized educational experiences that launch every student toward success. Our teachers, administrators and staff members are committed to the creation of educational environments where all children can maximize their individual learning potential. Further, we are committed to meeting students’ needs in all domains: academic, social and physical. An emphasis in all activities will be toward empowering students to take an active role in their own learning.
See attached job description for details about this position.
EOE M/F/D/V
APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS The entire application process is completed online via the Applitrack system. For your application to be deemed complete, you must complete the application in its entirety including all applicable disclosure questions.
NON-DISCRIMINATION DISCLOSURE District 49 does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, or age in its programs and activities and provides equal access to the Boy Scouts and other designated youth groups. Vista Ridge High School is looking for a Basketball coach to join our program. Apply now.
ATHLETIC COACH PERSONNEL VACANCY REGULAR SUPPORT BOYS BASKETBALL COACH VISTA RIDGE HIGH SCHOOL
ABOUT THE POSITION School District 49 is actively seeking a regular support coach for Boys Basketball at Vista Ridge High School.
Reports to: Head Coach, Athletic Director and Building Administration Stipend Amount: Dependent upon the extra-curricular salary schedule. Payments are typically made in one lump sum payment of $3,036. Benefits: No benefits available Interviews are site-based and not conducted by the Human Resources office. If you have questions regarding the status of an open or recently closed position, you may contact the site where the vacancy is located
ABOUT DISTRICT 49 School District 49, the fastest growing district in the Pikes Peak region, hosts 23 exceptional schools and programs within four distinct innovation zones, providing preK-12-aligned curriculum, online and alternative programming and homeschool enrichment. Home to more than 19,000 students and 1,500 staff members, District 49 aims to be the best choice to learn, work and lead. As one of Colorado's only two recognized Districts of Innovation, District 49 is a challenging and stimulating place to lead. As part of our commitment to innovate, we host a robust portfolio of conventional, charter, online, and blended schools, including homeschool support as well as career and technical education.
We are passionate about providing individualized educational experiences that launch every student toward success. Our teachers, administrators and staff members are committed to the creation of educational environments where all children can maximize their individual learning potential. Further, we are committed to meeting students' needs in all domains: academic, social and physical. An emphasis in all activities will be toward empowering students to take an active role in their own learning.
See attached job description for details about this position.

Job.JobDescription:
Ellicott High School is looking for a Soccer coach to join our program. Apply now.
Ellicott School District 22 Job Description
High School Head Boys Soccer Coach Pay: $3,000.00 for the season
Reports to: Head Coach/Director of Activities/Building Principal
Qualifications: 1. Possess all certificates required for the State of Colorado. 2. Possess current CPR certification. 3. Possess current First Aid certification.
Knowledge and Skills 1. Care and Prevention of Athletic Injuries course. 2. Have fundamental knowledge of the sport. 3. Ability to select from a variety of instruction methods to teach activity objectives.
Nature and Scope of Job: To instruct all students in the fundamental skills and knowledge of the sport or activity. To provide an atmosphere for successful individual development through monitoring, evaluating, and reinforcing.
Essential Functions: 1. Ability to develop drill and practice activities that teach and enhance individual/team skill development that makes up the game. 2. Develop and maintain an inventory list of equipment. 3. Prepares and implements a schedule of checking out, maintaining, and checking in equipment. 4. Ability to plan and enforce appropriate safety and health rules and regulations. 5. Ability to develop a supervision plan that ensures the safety and welfare of students at all times: locker room, trips, practices, etc. 6. Maintains confidentiality about students and others. 7. Presents a professional appearance. 8. Ability to be an effective communicator with all audiences - players, parents, supervisors, peers and spectators. 9. Ability to receive, understand and follow directions. 10. Be able to model/demonstrate activities and equipment used to develop basic skills or physical condition. 11. Ability to observe, diagnoses, and prescribe appropriate remediation activities during practice and game conditions for players and team. 12. Must know and be able to apply the developmental stages of each point of the game to the needs and ability levels of the team/players. 13. Assimilate innovations and adapt to changes when applicable. 14. Support the philosophies of Ellicott School District 22 and Ellicott High School activities. 15. May be responsible for supervising students on out-of-town activities. This may involve staying overnight.
Physical and Environmental Demands (Sport Specific): 1. All Sports a. Ability to sit on a bus for hour after hour. b. Ability to carry and/or provide aid to an injured player. c. Occupationally exposed to blood borne pathogens and other potentially infectious materials based on task and procedures required. 2. Basketball a. Ability to coach at a high intensity level during practices and games. b. Ability to move quickly from drill to drill with a definite direction and focus. c. Ability to remove an injured player from the court. d. Ability to coach in a variety of temperatures. 3. Track/Cross Country a. Ability to hear and be heard for up to 120 meters. b. Ability to demonstrate agility drills and stretching exercises. c. Ability to handle exposure to fall/springs conditions outdoors- climate, hot/cold, snow, wind, sun, etc. 4. Wrestling a. Ability to direct activities (coaching) for up to 14 hours. b. Ability to demonstrate wrestling techniques. c. Ability to withstand high temperatures for prolonged periods of time. 5. Volleyball a. Ability to toss balls to a specific spot or target. b. Ability to demonstrate various skills and verbally describe. c. Ability to visually analyze various physical and mental Volleyball skills. d. Ability to run drills for periods of up to 1-2 hours without fatiguing. e. Ability to coach in a variety of temperatures. 6. Football a. Ability to remove an injured player from the field. b. Ability to run drills for periods of up to 1-2 hours without fatiguing. c. Ability to hear and be heard outdoors for up to 50 yards.
Stipend: Refer to the extracurricular pay schedule.

Valley School District RE-1 is looking for a Soccer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Boys Soccer Coach is responsible for supporting the team throughout the season. Leadership is to be provided by the assistant coach in the following areas: 1. Assisting with coordination of all levels of teams. 2. Development of expectations and direction of the soccer program. 3. Preparation of teams for competition. 4. Teaching fundamental and advanced skills. 5. Following school district regulations and policies. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: * Acts under the direction of the head coach. * Assumes functions and duties of the head coach in their absence. * Coaches individual participants in the skills necessary for effective performance in the activity, as individuals & team members. * Plans and directs a regular program of practice in season. * Maintains competency in rules, regulations, and coaching techniques for the sport/activity. * Maintains discipline and sportsmanlike conduct of all participants and enforces the policies of the District uniformly when disciplining participants. * Coordinates freshman participants at interscholastic competition and in practice. * Supervises student athletes when traveling. * Oversees the safety conditions for students and facility, and the areas in which the sport is conducted during all times students are present. * Demonstrates cooperative conduct. * Shares in responsibility for equipment, distribution, collection and inventory. * Only minimum duties are listed. Other functions may be required as given or assigned. REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS: * Must be 21 years of age, or older * High School Diploma or equivalent * Complete and submit an RE-1 Valley Schools online application * Willing and able to drive to away games, camps, scrimmages * Knowledgeable in the techniques and strategies for the sport/activity. * Ability to read & interpret documents such as safety rules, operating & maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. * Ability to follow both oral and written directions & instructions from the athletic director. * Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. * Ability to effectively communicate, orally and in writing, with students, parents, staff and the community. * Ability to handle stressful situations. * Ability to maintain confidentiality of employment and student matters. * Ability to perform duties with an awareness of all district requirements and board policies. * Ability to effectively manage time and responsibilities. * Other qualifications as deemed necessary by the athletic director. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS: * At least 3 years of high school and/or college coaching experience. * Knowledge of all aspects of senior high school activity programming. * Proven leadership qualities and successful experience in working with high school students. * Background/experience in coaching high school soccer, or equivalent Application and Selection Procedure: * Candidates: Apply online at https://www.applitrack.com/re1valley/onlineapp/ If you do not have internet access, a computer is available in the Human Resources Division, Hagen Education Center, 301 Hagen, Sterling, Colorado. * Applications are reviewed to match candidates with position qualifications, and a limited number of candidates are selected for interviews. For the final selection, employment is contingent upon completing the post-offer screening process, including a background check and possible medical examination. Sterling is located in Logan County in the Northeastern corner of Colorado on Interstate 76 alongside the Platte River. Having the largest population, approximately 14,699 (2020), in Logan County and much of the surrounding area, Sterling offers shopping, restaurants, numerous parks, a recreation center, access to medical care and is located near North Sterling Reservoir. Sterling is approximately 1 1/2 hours from Denver International Airport and 2 hours from Denver and the front range (I-25 corridor). RE-1 Valley School District has two high schools, one middle school, one junior high school, three elementary schools and two preschools and serves approximately 1,910 students in the Sterling and Iliff/Crook areas, employing about 400 staff. RE-1 Valley employs highly qualified, dedicated staff who believe our mission is to prepare all students for productive, successful lives beyond school through a balanced curriculum, activities, community experiences, global awareness, high expectations and interaction among students, parents and community. Visit the District website at www.re1valleyschools.org for more information including updated job postings and salary schedules.
Job.Experience: At least 3 years of high school and/or college coaching experience (Preferred)
Job.SalaryRange: Stipend opportunities - $3018.00 to be paid at the end of the soccer season

Job.JobDescription:
Greeley West High School is looking for a Track & Field Co-Curricular/Athletics/Activities/High School Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
JOB DESCRIPTION Summary/Objective: This person will work closely with the head coach supporting their plan. They will also be in charge of running their own aspects of practice given the skill they are put in charge of. Another aspect of their duties will be to supervise students until they have been picked up from practice and/or meets.
Essential Functions: Organizing and supervising game help. Assists with supervision at activities.
Additional Qualifications: Applicant must complete concussion training.
Other Duties: Knowledge, Skills, and Mental Ability Knowledge of Colorado and district content standards. Knowledge and skills sufficient to perform all duties of the position. Prefers leadership situations relating to meeting the social, emotional, psychological and academic needs of students. Prefers a work environment where flexibility, ability to deal with conflict, a high tolerance for stress, an ability to quickly internalize information and make informed decisions, and long hours are a part of the assignment. Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice. Regular attendance is required for this position.
Supervisory Responsibilities: None
Work Environment/ Physical Demands: Ability to sit for long periods of time, numerous distractions, visual, auditory and ambulatory acuity important. Occasional lifting of up to 30 pounds.
Travel: None

Job.JobDescription:
Escalante Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
Durango School District 9-R is accepting applications for an Assistant Track Coach at Escalante Middle School. This coaching position includes instructing middle school students (grades 7 and 8) in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training of track. The 25/26 Middle School Track season runs March 2026 - May 2026.
Preferred Qualifications Knowledge of effective coaching practices/techniques of track Ability to build rapport with middle school students Ability to build individual and team character and confidence Ability to relate to students of varying athletic abilities Ability to conduct oneself in an ethical and exemplary manner at all times Ability to work positively with coaches, staff, and administration Ability to communicate effectively with student athletes and parents CPR/1st Aid

Florence Jr/Sr High School is looking for a Softball Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
REPORTS TO: Athletic Director and/or Building Principal. SUPERVISES: May supervise assistant coaches, volunteers, student managers, or support staff as assigned, in coordination with the Athletic Director and Principal. JOB GOAL: To instruct student-athletes in fundamental softball skills, game strategy, teamwork, and physical conditioning while promoting sportsmanship, character development, self-discipline, confidence, and pride in accomplishment. The coach is responsible for creating a positive, inclusive, and safe environment that supports both athletic and academic success. GENERAL EXPECTATIONS • Understand that the athletic program plays an important role in representing the school district within the community. • Balance competitive goals with the district’s commitment to student well-being, sportsmanship, and positive mental health. • Recognize that the position may require extended hours, physical activity, and attention to student safety and supervision. • Exercise sound judgment, professionalism, and common sense in situations not specifically addressed by policy or procedure. DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES Program & Policy Compliance • Maintain knowledge of and comply with Fremont RE-2 School District Board of Education policies, CHSAA or league regulations, and school athletic procedures. • Ensure consistent implementation of all applicable rules, eligibility requirements, and safety standards. • Follow the established administrative chain of command for communication, requests, and concerns. Coaching & Staff Responsibilities • Establish and teach fundamental softball skills, rules, and team philosophy appropriate for junior high athletes. • Assist with training, guiding, and supporting assistant coaches or volunteers. • Foster teamwork, discipline, positive morale, and respectful behavior among athletes and staff. • Evaluate assistant coaches as required and provide feedback to the Athletic Director or Principal. Administrative Duties • Assist the Athletic Director with scheduling games, practices, transportation, and special events. • Ensure practices and contests begin and end on time and that facilities are used appropriately. • Coordinate with maintenance and school staff regarding field preparation and facility use. • Maintain required documentation including physicals, eligibility forms, parental consent, and injury reports. • Recommend improvements to policies, procedures, or program operations as appropriate. Student-Athlete Responsibilities • Serve as a mentor to student-athletes, promoting respect, responsibility, fairness, teamwork, and citizenship. • Monitor student-athletes' academic progress and conduct, emphasizing the importance of classroom success. • Supervise student-athletes at all practices, games, and during travel. • Communicate team rules, expectations, and consequences clearly to athletes and parents. • Implement injury prevention practices and follow district procedures for medical care and emergencies. • Complete and submit all required injury and incident reports in a timely manner. • Communicate with parents when disciplinary action or eligibility concerns arise. Equipment & Facilities • Assist with inventory, issuance, care, and return of uniforms and equipment. • Ensure equipment is properly stored, maintained, and secured. • Promote respect for school property, facilities, and equipment. • Monitor locker rooms, fields, and practice areas for cleanliness and safety. • Secure facilities when leaving if custodial staff are not present. Finance • Work within the approved athletic budget. • Provide input on equipment needs and budget planning for future seasons. • Assist with accountability for lost or damaged equipment when required. Public Relations • Conduct preseason meetings with parents and athletes. • Maintain positive, professional relationships with parents, officials, staff, and the community. • Promote the softball program within the school and community in an appropriate and approved manner. • Coordinate with the Athletic Director regarding any communication with media or public outlets. Fremont RE-2 School District does not discriminate on the basis of disability, race, creed, color, sex, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, religion or need for special education services, access to, treatment or employment in its educational programs or activities. Additionally, a lack of English language skills is not a barrier to admission or participation in activities. The following individuals have been designated to handle inquiries regarding the non-discrimination policies: Dr. William Wilson, Superintendent, and EEO/Affirmative Action/Title IX/Section 504 Compliance Officer for complaints involving employees, and Jason Cellan, Title IX Compliance Officer for complaints involving students. Both individuals can be located at 403 W. 5th Street, Florence, Colorado, 81226. (719) 784-6312. Complaint procedures have been established for students, parents, employees, and members of the public. (Policy AC, AC-R, AC-E-1, AC-E-2)

Job.JobDescription:
Berthoud High School is looking for a Tennis Athletics/Activities/Extra Duty/HS Assistant Tennis to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach role at Berthoud High School emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Assistant Girls Tennis Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Berthoud High School, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Berthoud High School at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Assistant Girls Tennis Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Berthoud High School.

Berthoud High School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/Extra Duty/HS Assistant Track to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Track Coach (Sprint) at Berthoud High School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Assistant Track Coach (Sprint) plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Berthoud High School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Assistant Track Coach (Sprint) expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Assistant Track Coach (Sprint) supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Assistant Track Coach (Sprint) may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Berthoud High School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

ELLICOTT SCHOOL DISTRICT 22 CORE VALUES WE VALUE: - Providing a safe and caring environment for learning - High expectations for the growth and academic achievement of each student - Developing meaningful relationships with students, families, and the community through respect and understanding - Respectful communication at all levels of the educational organization - Engaging students, parents, and the community as partners in education through involvement and empowerment - Attracting and retaining high quality personnel who are knowledgeable, care deeply about our students, and understand and embrace the diversity of our community

Job.JobTitle: Middle School 7th Grade Volleyball Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Platte Valley Middle School is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Desired: 1. Two years of experience as a Volleyball player or as a coach. 2. Knowledge of Volleyball skills, rules, and applicable drills.
Reports to and evaluated by: Head Coach/Athletic director.
Job Goal: To promote a middle school Volleyball program in which the student can excel to her full potential while competing in an atmosphere that adds to her total education including his physical well-being, moral character and social and mental development.
Essential Duties & Performance Responsibilities: 1. Assists in the coordination of the Volleyball program at the Middle School level. 2. Assists in the development and organization of practice programs to provide both individual and team development. 3. Instructs student-athletes on sport knowledge, rules and techniques 4. Acts as a liaison for the Middle School Volleyball program to the staff, administration, high school Volleyball program and general public. 5. Assists in the planning and responsibilities for hosted tournaments. including but not limited to set up, tear down, etc. (with support of Head Coach and athletic director) 6. Maintains professional growth through attendance of coaching clinics and conferences. 7. Assists in the coordination of managers and event support. 8. Performs other tasks and assumes other responsibilities as the AD, Principal, Superintendent and/or the board may assign.
Terms of Employment: For duration of approved athletic season, pending background check.
Evaluation: Performance of this position will be evaluated in accordance with the board's policy on evaluation of coaching personnel.

Job Posting Title: Assistant Coach - Poms - HS Job Description: An athletic coach with Douglas County School District will be charged with the organization and implementation of a successful program. Candidates must demonstrate the ability to develop a competitive program that is committed to the creation of student athletes who demonstrate a commitment to academics, strong fundamental skills and sportsmanship. The successful candidate will be expected to collaborate with administrators, teachers, coaches, sponsors, students, parents and members of the community to ensure that the school stands out with pride for our community. All applicants and district transfers need to apply online.

Chaparral High School is looking for a Lacrosse Part time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS role at Chaparral High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Chaparral High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Chaparral High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS at Chaparral High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Pine Creek High School is looking for a Soccer Part time to join our program. Apply now.
Responsible to the Athletic Director and/or a head coach; provides coaching and leadership for the high school athletic program. Responsible for assisting with the organization and administration of the athletic program. Responsible for adhering to the policies and procedures of Academy District 20, the Colorado High School Activities Association (CHSAA) and the Athletic Department. Experience and/or background in the sport is highly desirable. Must have knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and must be willing to examine new theories and procedures. Must have ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. Must be able to work with the community, staff, parents and students. Must have successful high school coaching experience in the sport, or other demonstrated coaching expertise. CPR and First Aid certifications are required. Must meet CHSAA coaching certification requirements.
Reports consistently for work as scheduled Performs other duties as assigned Attains proficient or higher evaluations on established Performance Standards Actively seeks feedback from supervisor(s) and/or colleagues Communicates high expectations for athlete behavior and reinforces expectations Communicates regularly with the Athletic Director Consistently uses responsibilities/routines/procedures that are effective with student athletes in the program Creates a safe, well-managed, and well-organized environment that supports team growth and development Effectively creates a competitive environment in which all athletes have a chance to experience both individual and team success Effectively identifies and applies a variety of strategies and tactics to maximize opportunities for success in competitions for the individual and/or the team Equipment is consistently maintained, inventoried, stored, and re-ordered prior to the beginning of the season. Evaluates assistant coach(es) where applicable. Has communication procedures that are clearly defined, consistently used, and proactive when possible Includes and values stakeholders Is aware of injuries to athletes and communicates with athletic trainer and parents, is proactive with injuries, makes sure athletes are medically released to return to play Plans for daily, weekly, monthly, and annual character development of all athlete/athletes in the program Ability to follow oral and written instructions Ability to greet and interact with the public in a courteous and professional manner Ability to maintain confidentiality Ability to manage simultaneous demands and set clear priorities Ability to work days, hours, locations, and assignments as directed by the supervisor within the position responsibilities Commitment to the education of students as a primary responsibility Demonstrated ability to manage simultaneous demands and set clear priorities Demonstrated ability to work well with others in a team setting Demonstrates citizenship, compassion, courage, discernment, excellence, honesty, hope, integrity, patience, perseverance, reliability, respect, responsibility, and trustworthiness Demonstrates effective organizational and time management skills Detail-oriented Energetic, creative, innovative, flexible
