Job.JobDescription:
Pueblo County High School is looking for a Softball Athletics/Activities/Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Coach - Softball: 2026 at Pueblo County High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Coach - Softball: 2026 manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Pueblo County High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Coach - Softball: 2026 demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Coach - Softball: 2026 follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Coach - Softball: 2026 at Pueblo County High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.Experience: Playing experience and/or coaching experience
Job.CompanyDescription: Pueblo County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Lewis-Palmer High School is looking for a Football coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Football Coach at Lewis-Palmer High School is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Assistant Football Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at Lewis-Palmer High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Assistant Football Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Assistant Football Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Assistant Football Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at Lewis-Palmer High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Boys Golf Head and Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Canon City Fremont RE-1 is looking for a Golf Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS: All coaches of interscholastic activity programs who assume full responsibility for students under their direction at any level shall be either: • A certified teacher or administrator holding a current certificate issued by the Colorado Department of Education; or • Employed as a teacher, counselor and or administrator at least 50% of the school day... [... much content from scrolled sections ...] RESPONSIBILITIES AND EXPECTATIONS... COACHING TECHNIQUES: 1. Use sound and acceptable teaching practices. 2. Run well-organized practice sessions. 3. Complete pre-season planning well in advance of the starting date. 4. Adhere to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention. When injuries occur, follow a prescribed routine and maintain good communications with patient, trainer, and parents. 5. Construct a well-organized game plan. 6. Develop a sound system for equipment accountability. 7. Keep assistant coaches, volunteers, student managers, and statisticians well informed of what is expected. 8. Cooperate fully with building staff, other programs, media, support staff, administration, etc. 9. Cooperate fully with maintenance staff, transportation, and others involved in support of the program. COACHES' RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. To the players on the team The coach must promote and teach only ethical, aggressive and fair play, while stressing good sportsmanship at all times. The coach shall have a responsibility to not only teach skills and techniques pertinent to the sport, but to continually model and monitor behavior and academics. It shall be the responsibility of the coach to aid in the development of the overall person. The coach should be fair and unprejudiced with players and sensitive to individual differences, needs, interest, temperaments, attitudes and tolerances. 2. To the school and district A coach has a high visibility in the community and may have a great impact on the athlete, the home, the work place, and community organizations. A coach's actions should constantly be geared toward creating confidence in the coach, the school, and the district. A coach owes his/her school maximal effort and loyalty at all times. He/she must constantly strive for excellence throughout the program, department, and school. A coach should treat the faculty, staff, and student body with dignity and respect. A coach should give support to all endorsed activities of the school. A coach should, at every opportunity, urge the student body to be polite, courteous, and fair to all people involved in a contest. The coach should maintain a high level of discipline and expectations and be firm, fair, and consistent in the enforcement of those expectations. 3. To the profession A coach at Canon City Schools should continue professional growth in both the academic teaching area and the athletic coaching area. The coach should participate in professional growth opportunities whenever possible. 4. To fellow coaches Although the head coach must assume leadership responsibilities, independent thought by all assistant coaches should be encouraged, and the lines of communications should be such that ideas may be exchanged in the proper manner. Disagreements with fellow coaches should be discussed privately and in a timely manner.
Job.Location: Cañon City High School, 1313 College Avenue, Canon City, CO 81212

GENERAL FUNCTION The Head Strength and Conditioning Coach is responsible for the development and implementation of individual and group strength training programs for high school athletes. Mountain Range High School is looking for a Lacrosse Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Adams 12 Five Star Schools is deeply committed to a community of excellence, equity, and inclusion in everything we do. We commit to eliminate the predictability of who is included in excellence and who is not. Adams 12 Five Star Schools puts students first and supports our district's mission to elevate student success for every student every day in every classroom. Candidates who can contribute to our goal are encouraged to apply and to identify their strengths in this area.
LICENSES, REGISTRATIONS or CERTIFICATIONS: Head Coaches must hold a current certificate in AED/CPR upon hire. Assistant coaches must hold a current certificate in First Aid/CPR or a willingness to obtain. All coaches must be a CDE licensed teacher or hold a valid Coaches registration with CHSAA upon hire. All Coaches in Colorado must hold a current concussion certification from the NFHS or Children’s Hospital Colorado upon hire. All Coaches must pass the CHSAA annual Coaches Exam.
EDUCATION AND RELATED WORK EXPERIENCE: Previous coaching experience and/or participation at the high school or college level is preferred.
TECHNICAL SKILLS, KNOWLEDGE & ABILITIES: Ability to supervise students, enforce district, school, league and CHSAA rules and regulations. Possess knowledge and skills in the specific sport. Ability to plan, organize and successfully direct specific sport. Ability to perform, as well as teach, advanced techniques in specific sport. Ability to promote and follow Board of Education policies, Superintendent Policies and building and department procedures. Ability to communicate, interact and work effectively and cooperatively with all people including those from diverse ethnic and educational backgrounds. Ability to recognize the importance of safety in the workplace, follow safety rules, practice safe work habits, utilize appropriate safety equipment and report unsafe conditions to the appropriate administrator.
NECESSARY SKILLS AND BACKGROUND: Hold a current certificate in First Aid/CPR or a willingness to obtain. Previous coaching experience and/or participation at the high school or college level. Must be able to supervise students, enforce district, school CHSSA rules and regulations. Possess knowledge and skills in the specific sport. Ability to plan, organize, successfully direct specific sport. Able to perform, as well as teach, advanced techniques in specific sport.
APPLICATION INFORMATION: When completing the application, please include current/previous coaching experience in the Work Experience section of the application or paste your resume, with coaching experience listed, to the online application.
SALARY INFORMATION: The salary to be paid will be based on the sport and previous years of experience, per Certified Master Agreement language. Compensation for performing coaching responsibilities if offered and assigned by the district shall be as set forth in Athletic Coaches Salary Schedule.
BENEFITS INFORMATION: Colorado Paid Sick Leave, employee assistance program, voluntary life insurance through PERA, and 401(k), 403(b) & 457(b) plans are available to non-benefited employees regardless of hours worked. To learn more see our Benefits Overview
THIS POSTING IS NOT INCLUSIVE OF THE JOB DESCRIPTION. Rev. 7/24

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Girls Swim Diving Coach (High School)
Job.JobDescription:
Roosevelt High School is looking for a Swimming and Diving Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
Roosevelt High School Athletic Statement of Purpose “Develop Champions for Life”: To encourage Roosevelt student athletes to become champions. To foster an environment where academic integrity, social responsibility, respect for others, and competitive athletic excellence are highly valued. To discover and nurture the total person who enriches the community and brings honor to Roosevelt High School throughout life. High School Assistant Coach
Terms: This position does not qualify for benefits. Salary is based on experience and per our Athletic Salary Schedule Column II ($1,175 - $1,675).
GENERAL STATEMENT OF RESPONSIBILITY
The assistant coach in each sport assigned is directly responsible to the head coach of that sport. He/she should have a working knowledge of the rules and regulations of the Re-5J Board of Education and the athletic standards of Roosevelt High School. He/she should also be familiar with the rules and regulations of the League and the Colorado High School Activities Association.
PROFESSIONAL QUALIFICATIONS
1. Teaching assignment at Roosevelt High School. If not staff, must have successfully completed certified coaching class and be certified by CHSAA. 2. Successful experience in his/her chosen coaching area. 3. Successful background check. 4. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as may be found appropriate and acceptable.
PERSONAL QUALIFICATIONS
1. Encourage the student body and spectators to be courteous and polite to the visiting team. 2. Always display good sportsmanship, winning or losing. 3. Maintain poise and control at all times, especially at the contests. 4. Teach the team to play fairly and within the rules. 5. Be a good host to the visiting team and their coaches. 6. Recommend discipline to the head coach for players who disregard good sportsmanship either on the field/floor or on the bench. 7. Respect the officials’ judgement and interpretation of the rules. If an appeal is necessary, follow appropriate procedures. 8. Coach the team and not officiate the game. 9. Publicity attempt to shake hands with officials and the opposing coach prior to and after the game.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SEASONAL
1. Assist the head coach in carrying out his/her responsibilities. 2. Assist the head coach is the issuance of equipment, registration of the players, and providing accurate information needed to compile eligibility lists and other reports. 3. Assume responsibility for care of equipment and facilities. 4. Assume control over athletes and teams assigned to him/her and assist in helping supervise all athletes in the program. 5. Be in regular attendance at practice, contests, and meetings. 6. Discipline, when necessary, in firm and positive manner. 7. Provide the head coach with information needed in making reports and/or other releases. 8. Emphasize safety and be aware of the best training and injury.
END OF SEASON
1. Assist in the return and inventory of school equipment. 2. Recommend athletes for letter awards. 3. Recommend equipment to be purchased.

Job.JobTitle: Boys Golf Head and Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Canon City Fremont RE-1 is looking for a Golf Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Season: Fall Season 2026 Location: Cañon City High School Salary: Stipend is based on the Supplemental Salary Schedule and years of experience: Starting supplemental of $1,871-$3,996 Other Information: All coaching assignments are one-year contracts. QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS: All coaches of interscholastic activity programs who assume full responsibility for students under their direction at any level shall be either: A certified teacher or administrator holding a current certificate issued by the Colorado Department of Education, or Employed as a teacher, counselor and or administrator at least 50% of the school day in a private school; or A registered volunteer with Canon City Schools RESPONSIBILITIES AND EXPECTATIONS COACHES' PROFESSIONAL AND PERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AND EXPECTATIONS: 1. Rapport A coach must be able to develop good rapport with numerous individuals and community groups; team personnel, the student body, the professional staff, (faculty, administration, maintenance, etc.), the community as a whole, spectators, officials, fellow coaches in the conference, media representatives and the parents of his/her players. Good rapport and demonstrated competency are invaluable for the coach. 2. Cooperation The district and school expect the highest level of cooperation by all coaches and athletic staff members in the execution of their duties. Coaches must work cooperatively with their school athletic director, administration, other staff, team and parents. 3. Leadership and Public Relations Diligence, enthusiasm, integrity, honesty and a love for the game are all part of the professional pride that should be exhibited by any coach. Personal appearance, dress- to include proper coaching attire and shoes, physical condition, etc. should be exemplary. Public demeanor and decorum should always reflect positively on the coach, team, athletic department, school and community. 4. Discipline Every athletic team requires a high level of discipline among coaches, players, and support staff. In this regard, the implementation of discipline and the attitude with which it is implemented is the coach's responsibility. Individually, the coach becomes a model of all that the program represents—observation of school codes, rules of the game, ideals of good sportsmanship, behavior of participants throughout the season and especially where the student body is concerned. Desire to do well, to win gracefully, and to lose with poise and a positive attitude should be emphasized. Staff, players and spectators should be motivated to achieve established goals as a team and as individual players. 5. Improvement All coaches must constantly take advantage of opportunities presented for self-improvement. Regular attendance of district meetings, rules clinics, special workshops and training opportunities in specific fields is imperative. Membership should be maintained in professional organizations, coaches' associations, and similar groups whose programs are geared toward greater achievement and improved performance. Keeping abreast of current literature in professional journals, newspapers, and magazines, and utilizing enrichment material available in other media forms is also expected. COACHING TECHNIQUES: 1. Use sound and acceptable teaching practices. 2. Run well-organized practice sessions. 3. Complete pre-season planning well in advance of the starting date. 4. Adhere to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention. When injuries occur, follow a prescribed routine and maintain good communications with patient, trainer, and parents. 5. Construct a well-organized game plan. 6. Develop a sound system for equipment accountability. 7. Keep assistant coaches, volunteers, student managers, and statisticians well informed of what is expected. 8. Cooperate fully with building staff, other programs, media, support staff, administration, etc. COACHES' RESPONSIBILITIES: 1. To the Team and Program 2. The Head Coach - Specific Responsibilities 3. The Assistant Coach - Specific Responsibilities TO APPLY: Apply online at www.canoncityschools.org For more information contact Jamie Davis at 719-276-5872. Email:jamie.davis@canoncityschools.org
Job.CompanyDescription: Canon City Fremont RE-1 coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Brush Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach at Brush Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Brush Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Brush Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Brush Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Middle School Boys and Girls Track and Field Assistant Coach role at Brush Middle School.
Job.CompanyDescription: Brush School District RE-2 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Brush High School is looking for a Baseball Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
The High School Baseball Assistant Coach at Brush High School is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the High School Baseball Assistant Coach manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Brush High School, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The High School Baseball Assistant Coach demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The High School Baseball Assistant Coach follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the High School Baseball Assistant Coach at Brush High School allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Job.JobTitle: High School Girls Junior Varsity Soccer Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Buena Vista High School is looking for a Soccer Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
Job Summary Position is responsible for assisting the head coach with organizing the high school soccer program at Buena Vista High School. The assistant coach assists with conducting practices, motivating students, instructing student athletes in game strategies and techniques, and coaching at competitions. Essential Duties 1. Assists the head coach with instructing students in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. 2. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises as directed by the head coach. 3. Assesses student’s skills, monitors students during competition and practice, and keeps the head coach informed of the athletic performance of students. 4. Assists in determining game/event strategy. 5. Assists the head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competition. 6. Follows established procedures set by Athletic Trainer, in the event of an injury. 7. Models good sportsmanship behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards students, officials, administrators and spectators. 8. Helps distribute and check back in all equipment, supplies and uniforms issued to the students. 9. Assists the head coach in selecting award winners at the end of the season. 10. Participates in special activities to include parent’s night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. 11. Models nondiscriminatory practices in all activities. Other Duties 1. Attends required coaches meetings and fulfills all necessary requirements which include CHSAA coaches test, concussion training and be First Aid/CPR/AED certified. 2. Attends clinics and other professional activities to enhance coaching performance. Job Specifications To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirement listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodation may be made to enable individuals with disabilities, who are otherwise qualified, to perform the essential functions. Minimum Qualifications (Knowledge, Skills and/or Abilities Required) Some experience as an assistant or head soccer coach at the youth, middle school or high school level. Must possess effective coaching techniques and skills. Must possess some knowledge of the rules, regulations, strategies, and techniques of the sport. Must possess the ability to establish and maintain effective working relationships with school administrators, parents and students. Working Conditions & Physical Requirements Must have the ability to sit and stand for extended periods of time; exhibit manual dexterity; see and read printed material; hear and understand speech at normal levels, outdoors and on the phone; speak in audible tones so that others may understand clearly; physical agility to lift up to 25 pounds to shoulder height and 50 pounds to waist height; and to bend, to stoop, to sit on the floor, to climb stairs, to walk and to reach overhead. Work Environment The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Duties are normally performed in a school environment or outdoors.
Job.Experience: Some experience as an assistant or head soccer coach at the youth, middle school or high school level.

Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Volleyball Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position is responsible for assisting with the coaching of student athletes in game strategies and techniques to prepare them for athletic competition. Position motivates student athletes to develop an appreciation of the sport.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
Assists with organizational meetings for team prospects and encourages potential athletes to participate in the sport. Assesses players' skills and assigns team positions. Assists with developing a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both individual and team development. Works with the head coach and/or athletic director in scheduling facilities for practices and competition. Assists coaching and instructing players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. Observes players, during competition and practice to determine the needs for individual or team improvement. Determines game strategy based on the team's capabilities. Establishes and maintains standards of behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times. Monitors the academic performance of team members to ensure that eligibility requirements are met. Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding the athletic performance of their children. Follows state, regional, and district regulations governing the athletic program. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. Acts as a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with the news media, booster clubs, service clubs, and other organizations. Follows established procedures for the proper care, maintenance, & requisitioning of equipment, supplies, and uniforms. Works with the head coach and/or athletic director to develop a policy for awards and submits a list of award winners at the end of the season. Participates in special activities to include parents' night, banquets, award nights, and pep assemblies. Interacts in a positive manner with staff, students and parents Promotes good public relations by personal appearance, attitude and conversation
KNOWLEDGE/SKILLS:
Experience working with teenaged athletes Knowledge and previous experience coaching Ability to communicate ideas and directives clearly and effectively, both orally and in writing Effective, active listening skills Ability to work effectively with others
Job.Location: Harding HS, 379 Bond St, Bridgeport, CT 06610
Job.Experience: Experience working with teenaged athletes Knowledge and previous experience coaching

Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Football Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position is responsible for coaching student athletes in game strategies and techniques to prepare them for athletic competition. Position motivates student athletes to develop an appreciation of the sport.
RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Holds organizational meetings for team prospects and encourages potential athletes to participate in the sport. • Assesses players’ skills and assigns team positions. • Develops a regular practice schedule and organizes practice time to provide both individual and team development. • Works with the athletic director in scheduling facilities for practices and competition. • Supervises and assigns duties to assistant coaches, as necessary. • Coaches and instructs players, individually or in groups, regarding the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. • Observes players during competition and practice, to determine the needs for individual or team improvement. • Determines game strategy based on the team’s capabilities. • Establishes and maintains standards of pupil behavior and provides proper supervision of athletes at all times. • Monitors the academic performance of team members to ensure that eligibility requirements are met. • Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete’s injury. • Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding the athletic performance of their children. • Follows state, regional, and district regulations governing the athletics program. • Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. • Acts as a team representative and promotes the sport by communicating with the news media, booster clubs, service clubs, and other organizations. • Follows established procedures for the proper care, maintenance, & requisitioning of equipment, supplies, and uniforms. • Works with the athletic director to develop a policy for awards and submits a list of award winners at the end of the season. • Participates in special activities to include parents’ night, banquets, awards nights, and pep assemblies. • Interacts in a positive manner with staff, students and parents • Promotes good public relations by personal appearance, attitude and conversation
KNOWLEDGE/SKILLS:
• Experience working with high school students • Knowledge of academic area • Ability to communicate ideas and directives clearly and effectively, both orally and in writing • Effective, active listening skills • Ability to work effectively with others • Organizational and problem-solving skills • Basic knowledge of all other subject matter • Ability to work effectively with others • Basic computer skills • Excellent reading comprehension • Excellent math comprehension
QUALIFICATIONS:
• Minimum High School Diploma Required • CT SDE coaching certification or Connecticut Teaching Certificate • First Aid Certification • CPR Certification • Concussion Management Training • Experience as a coach at high school or college level preferred; combination of head coach, assistant coach, player and/or education may be substituted • Three years’ high school coaching experience preferred • Must possess effective coaching techniques and skills • Must possess knowledge of rules, regulations, strategies and techniques of the sport • Bilingual in Spanish, preferred
APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS:
• Letter of Intent • Resume • Photocopy of coaching permit or application for coaching permit • Photocopy of valid First Aid card – SIGNED, front and back • Photocopy of valid CPR card – SIGNED, front and back • Certificate verifying concussion management training (Original Certificate will be required from candidates selected for interview)
Stipend is in accordance with the BEA Collective Bargaining Agreement. This job description is not, nor is it intended to be, a complete statement of all duties, functions, responsibilities and qualifications which comprise this position.
Job.Experience: 3+ years preferred

Job.JobDescription:
Bristol Eastern High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Athletics & Activities/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Summary: To help each participating student, achieve a high level of skill and appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship and an increased level of self-esteem. Essential duties and responsibilities: Other duties may be assigned. Coach's individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. Plans and schedules a regular program of practice in season. Oversees the safety conditions of the facility or areas in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present. Maintain accurate statistics, records and results of the season. Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times and establishes and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual students. Maintains competency in rules, rule interpretations, game procedures, coaching techniques & general information about all aspects of the sport. Adheres to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention and follow up. Works closely with the head coach in scheduling interscholastic contest. Accounts for all equipment or delegates the responsibility. Develops a consistent and positive public communications procedure, through the school and media, to inform the parents and the general public about the athletic program. Conducts oneself at all athletic events in a responsible and professional manner. Supervisory Responsibilities: Supervises students of a particular program. Qualification Requirements: To perform this posting successfully an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. Education and/or Experience: Valid coaching certificate and the ability to comply with all mandated State requirements. Demonstrated experience in coaching within the specific sport field. Language Skills: Ability to read and interpret documents such as safety rules, operating and maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. Ability to speak effectively before groups of students or employees of the district. Mathematical Skills: Ability to apply mathematical skills to job responsibilities. Reasoning Ability: Ability to apply common sense understanding to carry out instructions furnished in written, oral or diagram form. Other Skills and Abilities: Exhibit qualities of leadership and organizational ability and reflect a spirit of cooperation in working with staff and school administration. Ability to apply knowledge of current research and theory in specific field, to establish and maintain effective working relationships with students, staff and the community. Ability to communicate clearly and concisely both in oral and written form. Ability to perform duties with awareness of all district requirements and Board of Education policies. Physical Demands: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this posting. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this posting the employee is frequently required to walk and stand. Occasionally the employee will sit or run. The employee will frequently bend or twist at the trunk while performing the duties of the posting. The employee will frequently squat, stoop, kneel, reach above the head, reach forward & repeat the same hand motion many times while performing the duties of the posting. The specific vision requirements of the posting include good depth perception and occasionally being able to see objects/persons at a distance. While performing the duties of the posting the employee is required to hear conversation in a quiet as well as a noisy environment and be able to tell where a sound is coming from. The employee must be able to communicate to the team in order to give directions. Work Environment: The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this posting. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this posting the employee will continuously work inside or outside depending on the sporting event. Occasionally the employee must be able to meet deadlines with severe time constraints and interact with the public and other workers. The employee has direct responsibility for the safety, well-being and work output of those individuals being supervised. The noise level in the environment is usually moderate. Salary: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule. Application: Those currently employed by the Bristol Board of Education complete an online application, all others fill out an online application at www.applitrack.com/Bristol/onlineapp.

SUMMARY: To instruct athletes in the fundamental skills, strategy, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success while at the same time helping the athlete to achieve an appreciation for the values of discipline, sportsmanship, and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Other duties may be assigned. 1. Has full responsibility for the overall supervision of their program, including the junior varsity and freshman teams. 2. Is in charge of assignments and duties of assistant coaches working in their sport. 3. Is responsible for the general upkeep, protection, and inventory of the equipment and uniforms used in their sport including the junior varsity and freshman teams. 4. Is responsible for assuring that assistant coaches and themselves know the existing state and league regulations and abide by them at all times. 5. Assist the athletic coordinator in providing transportation and requirements for tournament and special sport events. 6. Attend all league meetings regarding scheduling and rule updates specific to their sport. 7. Responsible for developing their schedules for all levels of play. 8. Communicates all schedule changes to the Director of Athletics or athletic coordinator. 9. Provides proper safeguards for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment storage areas. 10. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled sport events or practices, and adheres to scheduled facility times. 11. Makes recommendations for awards and is responsible for clarification of the award policy to athletes. 12. Participates in the budgeting process with the Director of Athletics by establishing requirements for the next season. Is responsible for explaining equipment guidelines as to type, color, style, or technical specifications. Is responsible for operating within the established budget. 13. Has a thorough knowledge of policies and procedures in the student-athlete handbook and CIAC handbook. Is responsible for explaining and reinforcing rules of training and code of conduct in the student-athlete handbook along with the CIAC eligibility rules to all members of the team. 14. Establishes the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques to be taught by the staff. 15. Follows and has a good understanding of the basic NCAA recruiting rules for their specific sport. 16. Monitors equipment rooms and coaches' office, i.e.: permits athletes to only be in authorized areas of the building at the appropriate times. Never leave athletes unsupervised during scheduled practices. 17. Examines locker rooms, equipment rooms, and playing safe areas before and after team use checking general cleanliness and safety. 18. Secures all doors, lights, windows, gates, locks, and alarm when leaving the building or outside facility when custodians are not on duty. 19. Will evaluate assistant coaches and programs and submit necessary postseason reports to the Director of Athletics at the conclusion of the season. 20. Is responsible for the actions and conduct of their team whenever they are under their jurisdiction. 21. Is responsible for the general health and welfare of student-athletes in their sport. 22. Communicates instructions and commands using clear, simple, and positive language. 23. Pays appropriate attention to athletes who are injured or ill or otherwise incapacitated. Is responsible for following the protocol set by the athletic department in regards to injured athletes. 24. Organizes pre-season meetings for players and coaches. 25. Is in charge of evaluating performance of student-athletes and providing suitable feedback, balancing critique with positive and motivating comments. 26. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with media, parents, officials, opponents, and fans. 27. Instills in each player a respect for equipment and school property, its care, and use. 28. Directs student managers, assistants, and statisticians. 29. Maintains current Connecticut coaching certification with the State of Connecticut Department of Education. 30. Performs other duties that are consistent with the nature of the position and that may be requested by the Director of Athletics and/or principal. NOTE: Coaches must meet certification requirements set by the State of Connecticut. ****Coaching Certificate Required.**** QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: 1. Valid Connecticut coaching certification or temporary emergency coaching permit. 2. Updated CPR/First Aid training approved by Connecticut State Department of Education. 3. Has the ability to organize and supervise a total sports program. 4. Has substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport. EXPECTATIONS: Staff assigned to these positions is expected to perform their duties with the following expectations in mind: • All supervisory responsibilities must be fulfilled. In particular, students must be supervised at all times. • Ability to read, interpret directions, write reports and communicate effectively with students, parents and other staff. • Ability to calculate figures and perform any math functions associated with the position and provides budget input when requested. • Ability to apply basic computer knowledge to the activity at hand. • Ability to perform duties with all district and Board of Education guidelines and policies in mind. • Knowledge of and/or experience in the activity. • Ability to meet the physical requirements of the position that may vary widely depending on the activity. START DATE: February 1, 2026 SALARY: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule. APPLICATION: Internal applicants can fill out an internal application by January 16, 2026. All others fill out an online application at www.applitrack.com/bristol/onlineapp
Job.Experience: Has substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport.
Job.SalaryRange: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule.

Job.JobDescription:
Bristol Eastern High School is looking for a Tennis Athletics & Activities/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
SUMMARY: To help each participating student achieve a high level of skill, an appreciation for the values of discipline and sportsmanship and an increased level of self-esteem. ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES: Other duties may be assigned. • Coaches individual participants in the skills necessary for excellent achievement in the sport involved. • Plans and schedules a regular program of practice in season. • Oversees the safety conditions of the facility or area in which assigned sport is conducted at all times that students are present. • Maintains accurate statistics, records and results of the season. • Enforces discipline and sportsmanlike behavior at all times and establishes and oversees penalties for breach of such standards by individual students. • Maintains competency in rules, rule interpretations, meet procedures, coaching techniques and general information about all aspects of the sport. • Enforces criteria for eligibility in interscholastic competition in this sport and is consistent in establishing criteria for eligibility in competition. • Adheres to a highly efficient and technically sound program of injury prevention and follow up. • Works closely with the athletic director in scheduling intramural and interscholastic contests. • Recommends purchase of equipment, supplies and uniforms. • Maintains necessary attendance forms, insurance records and similar paperwork. • Accounts for all equipment or delegates the responsibility. • Develops a consistent and positive public communications procedure through the school and media to inform the parents and the general public about the athletic program. NOTE: Coaches must meet certification requirements set by the State of Connecticut. Coaching Certificate Required. QUALIFICATION REQUIREMENTS: To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to meet the expectations listed below. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. EXPECTATIONS: Staff assigned to these positions is expected to perform their duties with the following expectations in mind: • All supervisory responsibilities must be fulfilled. In particular, students must be supervised at all times. • Ability to read, interpret directions, write reports and communicate effectively with students, parents and other staff. • Ability to calculate figures and perform any math functions associated with the position and provides budget input when requested. • Ability to apply common sense and deal with problems in a practical and reasonable manner. • Ability to apply basic computer knowledge to the activity at hand. • Ability to perform duties with all district and Board of Education guidelines and policies in mind. • Knowledge of and/or experience in the activity. • Ability to meet the physical requirements of the position that may vary widely depending on the activity. SALARY: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule. START DATE: March 1, 2026 APPLICATION: Those currently employed the Bristol Board of Education, fill out an internal application before 4 p.m. on Thursday, January 22, 2026. All others fill out an online application at www.applitrack.com/bristol/onlineapp.
Job.Experience: Knowledge of and/or experience in the activity.
Job.SalaryRange: Appropriate placement on the negotiated teachers A-2 schedule.

Job.JobTitle: JV Baseball Coach / Assistant Baseball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Baseball Assistant JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
2025-2026 SCHOOL YEAR Bridgeport Public Schools Human Resources Department 45 Lyon Terrace, Room 324 Bridgeport, CT 06604
ASSISTANT BASEBALL COACH CENTRAL HIGH SCHOOL STIPEND- $2,989.00
ASSISTANT BASEBALL COACH CENTRAL HIGH SCHOOL
COACHING REQUIREMENTS: 1. Possession of each of the following Certifications and Training: · Connecticut Teaching Certificate or Connecticut Coaching Permit · First Aid Certification · CPR Certification · Concussion Management Training 2. Demonstration of thorough applicable knowledge of the above-listed coaching position 3. Availability for off-season duties related to the coaching program
APPLICATION REQUIREMENTS: · Letter of Intent · Resume · Photocopy of Coaching Permit or application of Coaching Permit · Photocopy of valid First Aid card – SIGNED front/back · Photocopy of valid CPR card – SIGNED front/back · Certificate verifying Concussion Management Training · Original Certificate will be required from candidates selected for interview
Stipend is in accordance with the BEA Collective Bargaining Agreement
This job description is not, nor is it intended to be, a complete statement of all duties, functions, responsibilities, qualifications, physical, and cognitive, which comprise this position. The above is intended to be a fair representation of the "typical" demands of the position. In all cases, these relationships, functions, and their applications are subject to change by the Superintendent.
It is the policy of the Bridgeport Board of Education not to discriminate on the basis of race, color, sex, sexual orientation, marital status, religion, age, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, present or past mental disorder, mental retardation, or learning ability, in any of its education programs, activities, or employment practices. Job.JobTitle: Junior Varsity Assistant Coach - Softball ~ Caesar Rodney High School
Job.JobDescription:
Caesar Rodney High School is looking for a Softball Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
Junior Varsity Assistant Coach � Softball ~ Caesar Rodney High School All candidates must send a letter of interest, updated resume and 3 letters of recommendation directly to the the School Principal: Freeman Williams III, Principal Caesar Rodney High School 239 Old North Road Camden DE 19934 (302)697-2161 freeman.williams@cr.k12.de.us
PRE-EMPLOYMENT REQUIREMENTS: All persons employed by the Caesar Rodney School District are required to submit a criminal background check and a tuberculosis test. Anyone offered employment is required to provide the following: proper identification (Social Security Card and Driver's License), documentation of eligibility for employment in the U.S.A., completion of I-9 form, and completion of paperwork for the Delaware Child Protection Registry.

Job.JobTitle: Spring Track and Field Assistant Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Beacon Middle School is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
Spring Track and Field Assistant Coach Beacon Middle School
Qualifications: Previous coaching experience preferred. Knowledge of the sport is essential. Ability to work with students, parents, and other coaches. Good communication skills. Commitment to the athletic program and the school.
Responsibilities: Assist the head coach in all aspects of the program. Supervise athletes during practices, games, and travel. Teach the fundamentals and skills of the sport. Promote sportsmanship and fair play. Adhere to all school and DIAA rules and regulations.
5. Performance Monitoring: - Track and analyze team and player performance metrics. - Implement changes and improvements based on performance statistics. 6. Administrative Duties: - Assist in managing the team's schedule, including practices, games, and travel. - Ensure compliance with DIAA rules and regulations. Professional Responsibilities: - Able to establish and maintain collaborative working relationships. - Willingness to accept guidance and suggestions. - Able to communicate effectively both orally and in writing. - Able to work independently and exercise initiative. - Able to maintain confidentiality. - Continue professional growth and development through opportunities provided by CHSD and DIAA. - Able to work with other cultures represented in the community. Competencies: Success in this position requires the following knowledge, skills, and abilities 1. Knowledge - Must have knowledge of DIAA rules and regulations. - Must complete training regarding Title IX and Athletics. - Must complete Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and Concussion Training every two years. - Must be familiar with Cape Henlopen School District’s Policy 118 (Prohibited Actions of Employees) & Policy 551 (Electronic Communication & Social Media Policy). - Equity initiatives & resources that promote a culture of diversity and inclusion embracing differences as a strategic opportunity towards leading the District’s efforts to build a culture of equality and embodiment for all students, families, employees and the community. - Must be aware of relevant policies and procedures mandated by the State of DE for coaches and employees such as Criminal Background Checks, Child Protection Registry Forms & TB Tests. - Must have experience and a detailed knowledge of online systems such as Recruiting and Hiring and Google Docs. - Must possess online, digital skills to maintain records and to complete evaluations of coaching staff. 2. Skills - Active listening - Giving full attention to what others are saying and taking the time to understand what is being said. - Social perceptiveness - Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do. - Coordination & Flexibility - Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions, and an openness to accepting new assignments and duties. - Communicating - Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person, and communicating with the public. - Planning and organization - Coordinating schedules in a timely and efficient manner. 3. Abilities - Interpersonal Relationships - Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with students and staff, and maintaining them over time. - Oral comprehension - Able to listen and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences. The foregoing statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this job and are not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and competencies that may be required. Must have completed and submitted online application prior to closing date. Your application is not submitted until you click on the submit button and get verification. Questions? Contact Kevin.Smith@cape.k12.de.us or 302-645-7099
Job.Experience: Previous coaching experience preferred

Job.JobDescription:
Beacon Middle School is looking for an Athletics/ Extracurricular/Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
GENERAL INFORMATION: Beacon Middle School Boys Lacrosse Assistant Coach
General Description: The Assistant Coach is responsible for supporting the head coach to ensure that the head coach can lead the team, organize and implement game plans, and foster a positive and competitive environment. The ideal candidate will work collaboratively with the head coach and demonstrate an understanding of the sport.
Minimum Qualification A minimum of a high school diploma or GED is required. Advanced education above high school is preferred. Essential Responsibilities 1. Team Leadership and Development: Develop and implement training programs to enhance player performance. Provide mentorship and support to players, fostering their physical, mental, and emotional development. Evaluate player performance and provide constructive feedback. 2. Game Strategy and Planning: Analyze opponents and develop game plans and strategies to maximize team performance. Make real-time decisions during games to adapt strategies as needed. Lead meetings/practices when asked by the head coach to review performance and plan future games. 3. Team Management: Collaborate with the head coach and other staff to ensure cohesive team operations. Assist with the selection of players Ensure all players adhere to team rules, policies, and standards. 4. Communication and Collaboration: Maintain open lines of communication with players, coaching staff, and families. Foster a positive and inclusive team culture. 5. Performance Monitoring: Track and analyze team and player performance metrics. Implement changes and improvements based on performance statistics. 6. Administrative Duties: Assist in managing the team's schedule, including practices, games, and travel. Ensure compliance with DIAA rules and regulations.
Professional Responsibilities Able to establish and maintain collaborative working relationships. Willingness to accept guidance and suggestions. Able to communicate effectively both orally and in writing. Able to work independently and exercise initiative. Able to maintain confidentiality. Continue professional growth and development through opportunities provided by CHSD and DIAA. Able to work with other cultures represented in the community.
Competencies: Success in this position requires the following knowledge, skills, and abilities 1. Knowledge Must have knowledge of DIAA rules and regulations. Must complete training regarding Title IX and Athletics. Must complete Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and Concussion Training every two years. Must be familiar with Cape Henlopen School District's Policy 118 (Prohibited Actions of Employees) & Policy 551 (Electronic Communication & Social Media Policy). Equity initiatives & resources that promote a culture of diversity and inclusion embracing differences as a strategic opportunity towards leading the District's efforts to build a culture of equality and embedment for all students, families, employees and the community. Must be aware of relevant policies and procedures mandated by the State of DE for coaches and employees such as Criminal Background Checks, Child Protection Registry Forms & TB Tests. Must have experience and a detailed knowledge of online systems such as Recruiting and Hiring and Google Docs. Must possess online, digital skills to maintain records and to complete evaluations of coaching staff. 2. Skills Active listening - Giving full attention to what others are saying and taking the time to understand what is being said. Social perceptiveness - Being aware of others' reactions and understanding why they react as they do. Coordination & Flexibility - Adjusting actions in relation to others' actions, and an openness to accepting new assignments and duties. Communicating - Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person, and communicating with the public. Planning and organization - Coordinating schedules in a timely and efficient manner. 3. Abilities Interpersonal Relationships - Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with students and staff, and maintaining them over time. Oral comprehension - Able to listen and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences.
The foregoing statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this job and are not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and competencies that may be required.
Must have completed and submitted online application prior to closing date.

Job.JobDescription:
Mariner High is looking for a Volleyball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Qualifications MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS High School diploma or equivalent. Valid Florida teaching certificate or valid Department of Education athletic coaching certificate. Valid FHSAA certification. Valid Adult and Child First Aid/CPR/AED certificate issued by the American Heart Association or American Red Cross. Valid Florida Driver's License. PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS Coaching experience at the high school level. Experience mentoring youth and fostering a positive team culture. Familiarity with academic eligibility requirements for student-athletes. KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS, AND ABILITIES Knowledge of the sport's rules, techniques, and safety practices. Strong communication, organization, and leadership skills. Ability to collaborate with school staff, parents, and community partners. Ability to work evenings, weekends, and during school breaks as needed. Adoption: 10/07/2025 Responsibilities ESSENTIAL JOB FUNCTIONS Typical duties include but are not limited to: Plan, organize, and conduct regular practice sessions. Develop and implement game strategies and tactics. Teach fundamental skills, techniques, and sports rules to student-athletes. Supervise student-athletes during practices, games, and school-sponsored trips. Monitor academic progress and support students in balancing athletics and academics. Promote discipline, responsibility, and good sportsmanship among players. Ensure compliance with school, district, and state athletic regulations. Manage and maintain team equipment and uniforms. Collaborate with the athletic director on scheduling, budget, and facility use. Maintain open communication with parents and guardians. Provide mentorship and guidance to student-athletes on personal development and college opportunities. Enforce all school policies and codes of conduct. Perform other job-related duties as assigned. Adoption: 10/07/2025 Additional Job Information Supplemental

Job.JobDescription:
PINE VIEW SCHOOL is looking for a Supplemental Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Coach (Supplemental Coach) position at PINE VIEW SCHOOL provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Coach (Supplemental Coach) prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At PINE VIEW SCHOOL, the Coach (Supplemental Coach) works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Coach (Supplemental Coach) encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Coach (Supplemental Coach) at PINE VIEW SCHOOL is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Job.CompanyDescription: Monroe County School District coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Monroe County School District is looking for a Baseball Head JV Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Coach JV Baseball position at Monroe County School District is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Head Coach JV Baseball works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Head Coach JV Baseball at Monroe County School District sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Monroe County School District, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Head Coach JV Baseball encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Head Coach JV Baseball is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Head Coach JV Baseball position at Monroe County School District is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.
Job.SalaryRange: $2,178.75 at the end of the season

The material contained herein is subject to change. All open positions offered by the DeKalb County School District are subject to specific job requirements. The DeKalb County School District is an equal opportunity employer, and all hiring decisions are subject to federal, state, and local employment laws and regulations.
Title: Community Coach
The successful candidate will work under the supervision of the Principal and the Head Coach in the sport that you express an interest. Candidate must be willing to assist the Head Coach in game techniques and strategies to prepare them for competition, and determine the need for individual or team improvement; candidate must be willing to accept directions from the Head Coach at all times. A Lay Coach will be required to assist with maintaining inventory of athletic equipment, maintain up to date knowledge of first aid techniques, and maintain locker room and playing facilities.
Community Coaches are temporary positions and must be renewed annually.
EDUCATION AND/OR EXPERIENCE: • High School Diploma or GED equivalent required. • Experience and knowledge coaching sports preferred.
CERTIFICATES, LICENSES, PERMITS: • Georgia High School Association Lay Coaching Certificate required. • Valid certificate in first aid required.

MUNDY'S MILL HIGH is looking for a Football coach to join our program. Apply now.
Instructs and coaches K-12 students on the fundamentals of sports. Works with the coaching staff with setting rules, policies and regulations for the team. Assists Head Coach in making systematic issuance of equipment. Assists Head Coach with coordinating and monitoring try-outs. Assists Head Coach with coordinating and supervising practice schedule. Assists in the supervisory control over athletes and teams assigned. Guides and encourages secondary school age student athletes to make appropriate decisions about further participation in sport(s) at a level appropriate to their ability. Works closely with other staff to coordinate completion and opportunities. Initiates where appropriate. Works with other partners to help monitor the impact of delivery and healthy lifestyles. All other duties as assigned and required, in order to perform the above services. JOB REQUIREMENTS: The GHSA requires that all community coaches complete the “PREPARE/ First Aid” and the “Principles for Coaching” exams within three (3) months of their class date. Prospective coaches are not allowed to coach until both exams have been successfully completed. Understanding and knowledge of rules and regulations presented by the GHSA. All community coaches are required to meet all policies and regulations in accordance with the GHSA Constitution and By-Laws. The GHSA strongly recommends all community coach prospects be 21 years of age or older. Ability to manage projects and duties with minimal supervision. Ability to effectively manage time and priorities. Strong teamwork and communication skills. Georgia High School Association Coaching exam required. Analytical: emphasize safety precautions; be aware of best training procedures and injury preventative actions. Requires regular, frequent contact with internal and external personnel and decision-making responsibility to discuss issues of moderate importance. Excellent interpersonal skills are required for working with intra-organizational teams. Responsible for multiple functions of supervision (athletes, equipments and staff as needed) with a high-level accountability for effective operation and results. Variable work environment – Office Setting, School grounds, Stadiums. Requires frequent travel for off-site competition/training locations Work requires physical effort 70% of the time or more outdoors. Moderate to lifting based on sport. NOTE: Contact the school for additional details about the posting. This position pays a stipend

EFFINGHAM COUNTY MIDDLE is looking for a Certified to join our program. Apply now.
The Athletic Director - Middle School at EFFINGHAM COUNTY MIDDLE is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Athletic Director - Middle School plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at EFFINGHAM COUNTY MIDDLE reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Athletic Director - Middle School expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Athletic Director - Middle School supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Athletic Director - Middle School may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at EFFINGHAM COUNTY MIDDLE. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
