By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at EBENEZER MIDDLE. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

EBENEZER MIDDLE is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Certified to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Athletic Cheerleading Coach at EBENEZER MIDDLE is a high‑engagement coaching role that calls for energy, clear communication, and a steady presence for student‑athletes. This position is built for a coach who enjoys being actively involved in every aspect of the program, from leading warm‑ups and teaching tactics to meeting with families and coordinating logistics. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Head Athletic Cheerleading Coach are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at EBENEZER MIDDLE use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Head Athletic Cheerleading Coach recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Head Athletic Cheerleading Coach collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Head Athletic Cheerleading Coach maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at EBENEZER MIDDLE. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: Health/Physical Education Teacher - Assistant Football Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Cedartown High School is looking for a Football coach to join our program. Apply now.
Qualifications: Applicant must be willing to coach within the local system and should be certified in the following area: Health & Physical Education (P-12). The Teacher should have completed a Bachelor's degree or higher program in a Georgia Professional Standards Commission-approved program and possess a Georgia certificate as required by the Georgia Department of Education for the specified position. The Teacher should have demonstrated that he or she has the necessary ability, knowledge, and personal qualities to skillfully perform the duties and responsibilities of the position. He or she must have a philosophy compatible with Board of Education policy and possess a personal attitude congenial with other faculty and staff. Selection: The Teacher shall be employed by the Board of Education upon recommendation by the Superintendent Reports to: Principal or Building Administrator FLSA Status: Exempt Contract: 187-Day Certified Employee with Assistant Football Coaching Contract Salary: Based on Polk School District salary schedule for teachers Duties and Responsibilities: See attached Job Description Selected applicants will be chosen for interview. Please do not contact the local schools. Polk School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, disability, or sex in its employment practices, student programs and dealings with the public. It is the policy of the Board of Education to comply fully with the requirements of Title VI, Title IX, Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973, the Americans with Disabilities Act and all accompanying regulations. The E-Verify program has been used since July 2007 for employment eligibility verification by Polk School District. Federal ID: 47324

Lumpkin County High School is looking for a Softball coach to join our program. Apply now.
Lumpkin County Schools is now accepting applications for the position of Head Varsity Girls Softball Coach for the 2026-2027 school year.
Teaching position: To be determined
Description
Manage and supervise athletic activities, contests, and practice sessions, to promote individual growth in athletic skills, teamwork, discipline, respect, and good sportsmanship. Use a variety of instructional techniques and media to meet the needs and improve the abilities of student athletes in softball. Coordinate the development, promotion and recruitment for the girls softball program. Hold organizational meetings for team prospects and encourage potential athletes to participate in the girls softball program. Work with other members of school staff to plan and put in place instructional goals and objectives to ensure the overall educational development of student athletes. Ensure all girls softball coaches work together toward a common goal within the girls softball program and provide unity within the youth community programs. Establish performance criteria for competition and evaluate students' athletic abilities initially and on a regular basis. Develop a regular practice schedule and organize practice time to provide both individual and team development. Hold a preseason meeting with all student athletes and parents before the first official game. Assess players' skills and assign team positions. Determine game strategy based on the team's capabilities. Monitor and enforce student eligibility criteria for extracurricular participation. Apply and enforce student discipline during athletic contests, practice sessions, and while on trips off school property. Encourage sportsmanlike conduct in all phases of athletic participation at all times. Maintain records to ensure compliance with all pertinent GHSA regulations, including eligibility forms, and grade reports, and ensure that regulations are followed. Instruct and advise students on NCAA regulations with regard to academic requirements for scholarships and recruiting practices. Assist student athletes with college recruitment. Assist the Athletic Director with the scheduling of games and coordinate arrangements. Act as a team representative and promote the sport by communicating with the news media, booster clubs, service clubs, and other organizations. Participate in special activities to include senior night, banquets, award nights and pep assemblies. Conferences with parents/guardians, as necessary, regarding athletic performance of their student. Follow established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Represent Lumpkin County School System and the Girls Softball Program in a professional manner. Maintain nondiscriminatory practices in all activities. Attend staff development meetings, clinics, and other professional activities to improve expertise. Perform any other related duties as assigned by the Athletic Director or other appropriate administrator.

Effingham County Schools is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Cheer (Competition) Coach role at Effingham County Board of Education emphasizes collaboration, consistency, and long‑term program development. This position is ideal for a coach who enjoys working as part of a broader athletic staff and who values building connections across grade levels and teams. The goal is to help create a smooth pathway for student‑athletes as they progress, while maintaining a recognizable identity and standard of excellence for the program.
In practical terms, the Head Cheer (Competition) Coach coordinates closely with the athletic director and fellow coaches to align terminology, teaching priorities, and expectations. This might involve participating in coaching meetings, sharing practice plans, and adapting approaches so that athletes experience similar structures and messages at each level. At Effingham County Board of Education, the program is strongest when coaches communicate and support one another.
Day‑to‑day responsibilities include designing practices that teach core skills, reinforcing team concepts, and preparing athletes for competition. The coach works to balance repetition with variety, ensuring that key fundamentals are constantly reinforced while athletes also experience new challenges. The Head Cheer (Competition) Coach reviews performance with the team, helping athletes learn how to analyze both successes and mistakes in a constructive way.
The role extends into community and family engagement. Coaches represent Effingham County Board of Education at games, parent meetings, and potential youth outreach events. The Head Cheer (Competition) Coach may help host clinics or camps, encourage younger students to try the sport, and provide information about expectations to families who are new to the program. A welcoming, organized presence can greatly influence how the program is perceived.
Athlete support is ongoing. The Head Cheer (Competition) Coach monitors team morale, assists students in managing the demands of school and sport, and reinforces the importance of rest, nutrition, and healthy habits. The coach keeps the focus on sustainable growth rather than shortcuts, emphasizing that consistent effort over time leads to meaningful improvement.
Administrative tasks such as roster management, schedule communication, and equipment oversight are also part of the position. The Head Cheer (Competition) Coach must handle these responsibilities reliably, as they are essential to maintaining a well‑run program. Coaches who enjoy seeing the big picture, who are willing to share ideas, and who care about the experience of every athlete will thrive in this role at Effingham County Board of Education.

Homer High School is looking for a Baseball Co-Curricular/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Baseball Coach role at Homer High School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the Assistant Baseball Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Homer High School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The Assistant Baseball Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the Assistant Baseball Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. This open communication helps prevent misunderstandings and builds trust.
Safety and compliance cannot be overlooked. The Assistant Baseball Coach follows all relevant policies regarding equipment, supervision, travel, and emergency procedures. The coach maintains current certifications as required and keeps up with best practices around conditioning, concussion awareness, and hydration. At Homer High School, the well‑being of student‑athletes is non‑negotiable.
This role is a good match for coaches who appreciate structure, care deeply about students, and are committed to building a consistent, values‑driven program. By serving as the Assistant Baseball Coach at Homer High School, a coach has the opportunity to make a lasting impact on young people while contributing to a respected and sustainable athletic tradition.

Job.JobTitle: B Team Track and Field Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Kenai Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Co-Curricular/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The B Team Track and Field Coach at Kenai Middle School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the B Team Track and Field Coach is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Kenai Middle School, the B Team Track and Field Coach helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The B Team Track and Field Coach may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Kenai Middle School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The B Team Track and Field Coach follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobDescription:
Chapman School is looking for a Track & Field Co-Curricular/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The A Team Track and Field Coach role at Chapman School is focused on helping student‑athletes unlock their potential through intentional coaching, structured practice environments, and a strong emphasis on character. This description outlines a position in which the coach is expected to be a planner, a teacher, a mentor, and a positive representative of the school community. Successful candidates bring energy to practices, patience to instruction, and clarity to expectations.
Practices form the backbone of the program, and the A Team Track and Field Coach is responsible for designing sessions that are purposeful and efficient. Each practice should have defined goals, from teaching new concepts to reinforcing previously learned skills or preparing for upcoming competition. The coach uses a variety of drills and teaching methods to keep athletes engaged, and regularly evaluates whether the team is progressing in the desired areas.
Coaches at Chapman School are also trusted to guide student‑athletes through challenges. That can include addressing frustrations after a tough loss, helping a student handle the pressure of increased responsibility, or supporting someone who is balancing school, family, and activities. The A Team Track and Field Coach listens, provides perspective, and reinforces the idea that effort, attitude, and resilience are as important as any statistical outcome.
Communication is a key strength of this position. The coach shares information about schedules, team policies, and expectations with families and staff in a timely and professional way. Within the team, the A Team Track and Field Coach encourages athletes to ask questions, give feedback, and support one another. Job.JobTitle: B Team Track and Field Coach
Job.JobDescription:
The B Team Track and Field Coach position at Homer Middle School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The B Team Track and Field Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Homer Middle School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The B Team Track and Field Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The B Team Track and Field Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the B Team Track and Field Coach role at Homer Middle School is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.JobTitle: 2026-27 Head POM Coach - Shadow Mountain HS
Job.JobDescription:
Paradise Valley is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Position: Head POM Coach Location: Shadow Mountain High School Temporary FTE: 1.0 Salary: $3,970.00 Addendum per Semester Season Dates: 1st Semester TBD 2nd Semester TBD **ALL COACHES MUST BE A DISTRICT APPROVED VOLUNTEER. PLEASE ALSO APPLY FOR Chaperone /Community Volunteer/Volunteer Coach Purpose Statement The job of Head Coach is done for the purpose(s) of designing and implementing the athletic program for assigned sport(s) in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; providing supervision of other coaching personnel; providing supervision of students during all aspects of the program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program activities; serving as a positive role model to student athletes; and serving in a liaison capacity for the school and program with other schools both within and/or outside the district, to the community, and to various organizations. Individuals in this job classification are assigned to a position for assigned sport(s) and oversees all aspects of the respective athletic program(s). This job reports to Athletic Director Essential Functions Assesses student athletes for the purpose of providing feedback to students on their individual performance and/or determining team placement. Attends a variety of meetings, workshops, conferences, etc. for the purpose of receiving and conveying information related to their role. Conducts a variety of supporting activities (e.g. parent orientations, recognition events, banquets, etc.) for the purpose of providing information regarding the program and/or recognizing participants' accomplishments. Develops team and individual practice regimes for student athletes (e.g. strength, speed, agility, nutritional, injury prevention, etc.) for the purpose of enhancing and improving their performance. Develops game strategies/plans (e.g. analyzes opponent strengths and weaknesses, analyzing athlete abilities, creating plays/strategies, etc.) for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition. Guides other coaching personnel as may be appropriate in instructional techniques, organization of practices, supervision guidelines and responsibilities, etc. for the purpose of providing guidance and mentoring. Identifies program needs for the purpose of providing recommendations of expenditures for activities, equipment, supplies, etc. that will enhance the assigned athletic program. Implements practice schedules and related events (e.g. usage of facilities and equipment, staff assignments, etc.) for the purpose of ensuring efficient program operation. Instructs student athletes for the purpose of promoting individual growth in athletic skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. game/student athlete statistics, insurance, signed permission forms, emergency information, schedules, equipment inventories, etc.) for the purpose of complying with district, athletic conference, state requirements, and/or report scores and strategies to local news media. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing appropriate intervention (e.g. counseling, tutoring, etc.) to students identified as needing assistance to maintain their athletic eligibility. Oversees assistant coaches, student managers, volunteers, etc. for the purpose of providing direction and monitoring activities. Promotes athletic program through a variety of activities (e.g. conducts off-season clinics, information on skill-camps, attends community meetings, etc.) for the purpose of increasing interest and participation. Responds to inquiries of students, parents, other school personnel, media representatives, college representatives, etc. for the purpose of providing information, assistance and/or direction. Keeps their school athletic director informed when issues arise that impact the school's athletic program (e.g. liability concerns, parent concerns, etc.). Provides appropriate and prudent supervision for all athletes and students involved in the program. Explains and discusses the PVUSD Athletic Code of Conduct with all athletes in their program. Maintains high standards of personal conduct. Coaches should serve as role models for their athletes. Ensures that all coaches involved with their program, whether paid or volunteer are cleared through the PVUSD Human Resources Department. Other Functions Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the work unit. Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to adapt to changing work priorities; administering first aid; applying pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; coordinating activities with other schools, districts and/or agencies; organizing and communicating information and concepts; and overseeing financial transactions. KNOWLEDGE is required for community support organizations and pubic relations; equipment used in activity/program; injury prevention and appropriate treatment protocols; pertinent laws codes, policies, and/or regulations; participant eligibility, attendance and discipline guidelines; public relations protocols; relevant professional standards and practices; and safety practices and procedures. ABILITY is required to communicate with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; providing leadership, direction, and team building; traveling to off campus athletic events; traveling to off campus competitions; and working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends. Responsibility Responsibilities include: working under limited supervision following standardized practices and/or methods; directing other persons within a small work unit; utilization of some resources from other work units is often required to perform the job's functions. There is a continual opportunity to impact the organization's services. Work Environment The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: occasional lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, some climbing and balancing, some stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness. Experience: Job related experience is required. Education: High school diploma or equivalent. Equivalency: Required Testing Certificates and Licences Valid Arizona Teaching Certificate or Valid Arizona Substitute Certificate or NFHS Level 2 Certificate (National Federation of State High Schools) Continuing Educ. / Training Clearances Criminal Background Clearance IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card FLSA Status Non Exempt Paradise Valley Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex or disability, in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities. Any person having inquiries concerning the School’s compliance with the regulations implementing Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI), Section 504
Job.Experience: Job related experience is required.

Job.JobTitle: 2026-27 Assistant Varsity Football Quarterback Coach - Paradise Valley HS
Job.JobDescription:
Paradise Valley is looking for a Football Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Assistant: Varsity Football Coach Location: Paradise Valley High School FTE: 1.0 Salary: $2,431.00 Temporary Position Season Dates: 08/03/2026 - TBD **ALL COACHES MUST BE A DISTRICT APPROVED VOLUNTEER. PLEASE ALSO APPLY FOR Chaperone /Community Volunteer/Volunteer Coach**
Purpose Statement The job of Assistant: Varsity Football Coach was established for the purpose/s of assisting the Head Coach with organizing the Football program at the high school level; communicating information to staff, students, and parents; ensuring that all student athletes are academically eligible; and ensuring compliance with district, athletic conference, and state requirements.
Essential Functions Assists the Head Coach in evaluating conjuntively with the Athletic Director the Football program for the purpose of improvement and making changes for the following season. Assists Head Coach and Athletic Director in developing game schedules and practice schedules for the purpose of providing training and interscholastic competition. Assists in the supervision of athletes during all areas of participation for the purpose of ensuring activities are conducted in an environment with minimal risk of injury to the participants. Attends a variety of meetings (e.g. staff, parent, league, etc.) for the purpose of conveying and/or gathering information required to perform job functions. Communicates with Athletic Director, parents, and student athletes for the purpose of providing information and/or direction regarding the Football program. Develops with the Head Coach team and individual practice regimes for student athletes (e.g. strength, speed, agility, nutritional, injury prevention, etc.) for the purpose of enhancing and improving their performance. Implements with the Head Coach fundamental Football skills and proper Football game strategies for the purpose of developing the student athletes' knowledge and ability to play Football. Implements team activities (e.g. fund-raisers, banquets, etc.) for the purpose of recognizing student athletes' accomplishments and raising funds for the Football program. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. student athletic eligibility, medical, emergency contact information, game/practice statistics, equipment inventories, etc.) for the purpose of complying with district, athletic conference, and state requirements. Manages uniforms, equipment and school facilities for the purpose of maintaining items in a responsible and safe manner. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing appropriate intervention (e.g. counseling, tutoring, etc.) to students identified as needing assistance to maintain their athletic eligibility. Supervises student athletes during all areas of participation for the purpose of ensuring activities are conducted in an environment with minimal risk of injury to the participants. Teaches sports techniques, playing rules, strategies, and playing tactics for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition.
Other Functions Performs other related duties as assigned for the purpose of ensuring the efficient and effective functioning of the work unit.
Job Requirements: Minimum Qualifications Skills, Knowledge and Abilities SKILLS are required to perform multiple, technical tasks with a need to occasionally upgrade skills in order to meet changing job conditions. Specific skill based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: adapting to changing work priorities; administering first aid; applying pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; organizing and communicating information and concepts. KNOWLEDGE is required to perform basic math, including calculations using fractions, percents, and/or ratios; review and interpret highly technical information, write technical materials, and/or speak persuasively to implement desired actions; and analyze situations to define issues and draw conclusions. Specific knowledge based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: equipment used in activity/program; injury prevention and appropriate treatment protocols; pertinent laws, codes, policies, and/or regulations; participant eligibility, attendance and discipline guidelines; public relations protocols; relevant professional standards and practices; safety practices and procedures. ABILITY is required to schedule activities, meetings, and/or events; gather and/or collate data; and consider a wide variety of factors when using equipment. Flexibility is required to independently work with others in a wide variety of circumstances; work with data utilizing defined and similar processes; and operate equipment using defined methods. Ability is also required to work with a diversity of individuals and/or groups; work with data of varied types and/or purposes; and utilize a variety of types of job-related equipment. Problem solving is required to analyze issues and create action plans. Problem solving with data requires independent interpretation of guidelines; and problem solving with equipment is moderate to significant. Specific ability based competencies required to satisfactorily perform the functions of the job include: communicating with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; setting priorities; working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends.
Responsibility Responsibilities include: working under limited supervision following standardized practices and/or methods; directing other persons within a small work unit; utilization of some resources from other work units is often required to perform the job's functions. There is a continual opportunity to impact the organization's services.
Work Environment The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: occasional lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, some climbing and balancing, some stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness.
Experience: Job related experience is required. Education: High school diploma or equivalent. Equivalency: Required Testing Certificates and Licenses Continuing Educ./Training Clearances Criminal Background Clearance Valid Arizona DPS Level I IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card www.azdps.gov/services/public/fingerprint
FLSA Status - Non Exempt Paradise Valley Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex or disability, in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities. Any person having inquiries concerning the School's compliance with the regulations implementing Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI), Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (Section 504), Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), or Title II of the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act of 2008 (GINA) may contact the Assistant Superintendent for Human Resources at 602-449-2292.

Job.JobDescription:
Gila Ridge High School is looking for a Football Part-Time to join our program. Apply now.
JOB DESCRIPTION Yuma Union High School District #70 Assistant Coach - Out of District Purpose Statement: The position of Assistant Coach is done for the purpose/s of assisting the head coach in designing and implementing the athletic program for assigned sport(s) in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; assist with conducting practices, motivating students, and instructing student athletes in game strategies and techniques. Essential Functions: Assists the Head coach with instructing players in the rules, regulations, equipment, and techniques of the sport. Organizes and directs individual and small group practice activities/exercises as directed by the Head coach. Assesses player's skills, monitors players during competition and practice, and keeps the Head coach informed of the athletic performance of students. Assists with determining game strategy. Assists the Head coach with supervising athletes during practices and competition. Follows established procedures in the event of an athlete's injury. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards players, officials, and spectators. Distributes equipment, supplies, and uniforms to students as directed by the Head coach. Follows district and site procedures and policies; follows the district Athletic Handbook and Code; adheres to the Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA). Oversees any unsafe conditions of the facility or area in which the assigned sport is conducted at all times athletes are present. Teaches student-athletes to perform sound, safe fundamental techniques of the designated sport throughout the season. Fosters ethics, good conduct and sportsmanship for all participants and coaches. Responsible for the safety and security of the team before, during and after events and traveling to and from events, including riding the bus with the team. Complies with time constraints for practice and contest schedules, including holidays/Saturdays, as well as off-season schedules. Promotes your school with pride and dignity at all times. Other Functions: Performs any other related duties as assigned by the Head coach, Athletic Director, Principal or other appropriate administrator. Working Environment: The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: significant lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, frequent climbing and balancing, frequent stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness. It is normally performed in a school environment or outdoors. Duties may be occasionally performed on field trips away from school. The noise level in the work environment is usually moderate. Job Requirements: High School/Trade School degree preferred. Citizenship, residency or work visa required Experience: Job Related Experience Education: High school diploma or equivalent Certificates & Licenses: Drivers License & Evidence of Insurability NFHS - Fundamentals of Coaching Concussion in Sports Clearances: I.V.P. Job.JobDescription:
Tanque Verde High School is looking for a Tennis Head Varsity Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Tanque Verde High School is seeking a Head Boys Tennis Coach. Previous tennis coaching experience preferred. Promote sportsmanship and foster good character in student athletes.
Provide for the safety and welfare of all participants. Supervise students in practice areas, locker rooms, on the bus during trips and at all times during the season. Exercise patience, tolerance and diplomacy in relations with all players, parents, co-workers, contest officials and spectators. Interscholastic activities are an extension of the classroom and as such, your actions, behavior and interactions with our student athletes should be reflective of this.
All head varsity coaches shall have a valid Arizona Teaching Certificate, Arizona Coaching Certificate, or have completed each of the following criteria from the National Federation of State High School Associations online at www.nfhslearn.org. • Within forty-five (45) days from date of hire: • Fundamentals of coaching • Sport specific course • First Aid/CPR • Fingerprint Clearance Card
Job.Experience: Previous tennis coaching experience preferred

Job.JobTitle: JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School
Job.JobDescription:
The JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School at Youngker High School - Buckeye is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Youngker High School - Buckeye, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the JV Head Spiritline/Cheer Coach - Youngker High School at Youngker High School - Buckeye allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.CompanyDescription: Buckeye Union High School Dist. coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Coconino High School is looking for a Volleyball Other to join our program. Apply now.
Position Title: Head Coach - Varsity Beach Volleyball Department/Section: Coconino High School Supervisor: Athletic Director/ Principal Salary: Special Service Contract - Stipend: $4000.00 Start Date: 06/01/2026 Job Summary Coconino High School is seeking a dedicated and enthusiastic individual to lead our varsity volleyball program as the Head Coach. This role is pivotal in developing student-athletes both on and off the court, fostering a competitive and positive team environment. The ideal candidate will possess a strong knowledge of volleyball, excellent leadership skills, and a passion for mentoring young athletes. The Head Coach will work closely with players, parents, and school administration to promote the sport and ensure the success of the program. Responsibilities - Develop and implement a comprehensive training program that enhances the skills and performance of student-athletes. - Organize and conduct daily practices, ensuring a focus on skill development, teamwork, and sportsmanship. - Plan and manage the varsity volleyball schedule, including practices, games, and tournaments. - Recruit and evaluate potential team members, fostering an inclusive and supportive team culture. - Communicate effectively with players, parents, and school staff regarding team expectations, schedules, and progress. - Monitor the academic performance and personal development of student-athletes, promoting a balanced approach to athletics and education. - Collaborate with the athletic department to ensure compliance with school policies, league regulations, and safety protocols. - Analyze game performance and provide constructive feedback to players to enhance their skills and understanding of the game. - Foster relationships with the community and promote the volleyball program through outreach and engagement initiatives. - Attend coaching clinics and workshops to stay updated on best practices and trends in volleyball coaching. Requirements - NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching and Concussion in Sports Certifications - Previous coaching experience at the high school or collegiate level, particularly in volleyball. - Strong knowledge of volleyball techniques, strategies, and rules. - Excellent leadership, communication, and interpersonal skills. - Ability to motivate and inspire student-athletes to achieve their best. - Commitment to the academic and personal development of student-athletes. - IVP Fingerprint Clearance - Flexibility to work evenings and weekends for practices and games. - A strong understanding of the importance of sportsmanship, teamwork, and character development in athletics. Thank you for your interest in the Flagstaff Unified School District. Please submit a complete application. Education information must be included in the application. Complete work history needed for proper placement on the salary schedule. If this information is not complete, your application will be returned. Please be sure to include current certificate and IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card, if applicable to the position. All applicants (including current district employees) must submit a complete online application.
Job.Location: Coconino High School 2801 N Izabel St Flagstaff, AZ 86004

Girls Tennis Coach
Essential Functions:
The Girls Tennis Coach is responsible for various aspects of the Tennis Program, such as recruiting within the school, scheduling opponents (in conjunction with the Athletic Director), training, and coaching team members. The Girls Tennis Coach is required to abide the AIA/USGA rules and regulations as well as the rules and regulations set by the Kingman Unified School District and the athletic administration at Lee Williams.
Requirements:
If Applicable: All head varsity coaches shall have valid Arizona Teaching Certificate, Arizona Coaching Certificate, or have completed the National Federation of State High School Associations Level I Certification online at https://nfhslearn.com within forty-five (45) days from date of hire.
If Applicable: Head Coaches shall be approved in accordance with the personnel policies of the school district and in accordance with the Arizona Revised Statutes. This certification shall be on file or recorded in the office of the principal or his/her designee. Any and all salary or remuneration shall be from the school district by which the coach is employed and no salary or remuneration for school coaching shall be from any outside source.

Must be able to obtain and maintain a valid IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card from Arizona Department of Public Safety. (Assistance and instructions to obtaining one is provided is necessary or go to www.azdps.gov)

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Baseball Seasonal to join our program. Apply now.
The Baseball Coach at Saguaro Creek K-8 is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Baseball Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Saguaro Creek K-8 reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Baseball Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Baseball Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Baseball Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Saguaro Creek K-8. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.
Job.Location: Saguaro Creek K-8, 8150 S Rocking K Ranch Loop Tucson, AZ 85747

Job.JobTitle: 2025-26 Festival Sports Wrestling Head Coach - Shea MS
Job.JobDescription:
Paradise Valley is looking for a Wrestling Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job ID: 5518688 Application Deadline: Posted until filled Posted: Jan 30, 2026 7:00 AM (UTC) Starting Date: Apr 16, 2026
Job Details Purpose Statement The job of Head Coach is done for the purpose/s of designing and implementing the athletic program for assigned sport(s) in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; providing supervision of other coaching personnel; providing supervision of students during all aspects of the program; using sound instructional techniques in overseeing program activities; serving as a positive role model to student athletes; and serving in a liaison capacity for the school and program with other schools both within and/or outside the district, to the community, and to various organizations. Individuals in this job classification are assigned to a position for assigned sport(s) and oversees all aspects of the respective athletic program(s).
This job reports to Athletic Director
Essential Functions Assesses student athletes for the purpose of providing feedback to students on their individual performance and/or determining team placement. Attends a variety of meetings, workshops, conferences, etc. for the purpose of receiving and conveying information related to their role. Conducts a variety of supporting activities (e.g. parent orientations, recognition events, banquets, etc.) for the purpose of providing information regarding the program and/or recognizing participants' accomplishments. Develops team and individual practice regimes for student athletes (e.g. strength, speed, agility, nutritional, injury prevention, etc.) for the purpose of enhancing and improving their performance. Develops game strategies/plans (e.g. analyzes opponent strengths and weaknesses, analyzing athlete abilities, creating plays/strategies, etc.) for the purpose of preparing the team and individual student athletes for the competition. Guides other coaching personnel as may be appropriate in instructional techniques, organization of practices, supervision guidelines and responsibilities, etc. for the purpose of providing guidance and mentoring. Identifies program needs for the purpose of providing recommendations of expenditures for activities, equipment, supplies, etc. that will enhance the assigned athletic program. Implements practice schedules and related events (e.g. usage of facilities and equipment, staff assignments, etc.) for the purpose of ensuring efficient program operation. Instructs student athletes for the purpose of promoting individual growth in athletic skills, teamwork, and sportsmanship. Maintains a variety of records (e.g. game/student athlete statistics, insurance, signed permission forms, emergency information, schedules, equipment inventories, etc.) for the purpose of complying with district, athletic conference, state requirements, and/or report scores and strategies to local news media. Monitors student athletes' academic eligibility for the purpose of both complying with AIA requirements as well as providing academic intervention/support when/where necessary and appropriate. Oversees the an assigned athletic program (e.g. games, practices, team travel, equipment, etc.) for the purpose of ensuring that all aspects of the program are done so in accordance with district, state, and AIA regulations and guidelines. Responds to emergency situations for the purpose of resolving immediate safety concerns. Responds to inquiries from a variety of sources (e.g. students, parents, administrators, site athletic director, etc.) for the purpose of providing information regarding the program and/or directing to appropriate sources. Secures equipment, supplies, and facilities for the purpose of ensuring that they are available as needed.
Skills, Knowledge and Abilities ABILITY is required to communicate with persons of varied educational and cultural backgrounds; dealing with frequent interruptions; exhibiting strong leadership; maintaining confidentiality; meeting deadlines and schedules; modeling sportsmanship and enforcing sportsmanlike behavior among students; motivating participating student athletes; providing a firm, fair, and consistent discipline approach; providing leadership, direction, and team building; traveling to off campus athletic events; traveling to off campus competitions; and working extended hours that may include evenings and/or weekends.
Responsibility Responsibilities include: working under limited supervision following standardized practices and/or methods; directing other persons within a small work unit; utilization of some resources from other work units is often required to perform the job's functions. There is a continual opportunity to impact the organization's services.
Work Environment The usual and customary methods of performing the job's functions require the following physical demands: occasional lifting, carrying, pushing, and/or pulling, some climbing and balancing, some stooping, kneeling, crouching, and/or crawling and significant fine finger dexterity. Generally the job requires 20% sitting, 40% walking, and 40% standing. The job is performed under some temperature extremes and under conditions with exposure to risk of injury and/or illness.
Experience: Job related experience is required. Education: High school diploma or equivalent. Equivalency: Required Testing Teaching Certificate or Certificates and Licences Valid Arizona Substitute Certificate or NFHS Level 2 Certificate (National Federation of State High Schools) Continuing Educ. / Training Clearances Criminal Background Clearance IVP Fingerprint Clearance Card FLSA Status Non Exempt
Paradise Valley Unified School District does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex or disability, in admission or access to, or treatment or employment in its programs and activities. Any person having inquiries concerning the School's compliance with the regulations implementing Title VI of the Civil Rights Act of 1964 (Title VI), Section 504 of the Rehabilitation Act of 1973 (Section 504), Title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 (ADA), or Title II of the Genetic Information Nondiscrimination Act of 2008 (GINA) may contact the Assistant Superintendent of Human Resources.
Contact Information Name: Christine Rossini Email: crossini@pvschools.net Phone: (602) 449-3011 Fax: (602) 449-3018
Job.Experience: Job related experience is required.

Job.JobDescription: Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coaching Date Posted: 2/20/2026 Location: Newell Barney College Prep JOB GOAL: To develop and maintain an efficient and competitive athletic program. QUALIFICATIONS: 1. NFHS Concussion Course 2. NFHS Fundamentals of Coaching 3. NFHS Bullying, Hazing & Inappropriate Behaviors Course 4. Head Coaches must also have NFHS Sport-Specific Course and First Aid Course 5. Varsity coaching experience 6. Must pass a background clearance 7. Must obtain an Arizona IVP Level One Fingerprint Clearance Card 8. Must have substantial knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field. TERM OF EMPLOYMENT: Seasonal - 3:30 p.m.-6:30 p.m. COMPENSATION: Per QCUSD salary schedule for applicable position REPORTS TO: Athletic Director PERFORMANCE RESPONSIBILITIES: Responsibilities shall include, but not be limited to, the following: 1. Fundraising 2. Run camps and clinics 3. Compliance of all AIA Bylaws PHYSICAL DEMANDS: The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is regularly required to stand, sit and talk or hear. The employee is frequently required to use hands and arms, stand, bend or stoop. The employee may be required to lift and/or move up to 25 pounds and occasionally lift and/or move up to 50 pounds.

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Track & Field Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
Empire High School is seeking a Throws Track & Field Coach who is responsible for coaching, developing, and mentoring student-athletes in throwing events, including shot put, discus, hammer, and javelin (as applicable). The coach will design and implement training programs that emphasize proper technique, strength development, safety, sportsmanship, and competitive excellence while supporting the overall goals of the track and field program. Key Responsibilities: Coach and instruct athletes in throwing events (shot put, discus, hammer, and/or javelin). Develop and implement effective training programs to enhance player performance and team success. Provide leadership and mentorship to student-athletes, emphasizing sportsmanship, discipline, and teamwork. Organize and supervise team practices, conditioning programs, and game strategies. Promote academic achievement and ensure players adhere to eligibility requirements. Represent Empire High School with professionalism in the community and in interscholastic competitions. Ensure compliance with Arizona Interscholastic Association (AIA) rules and school policies. Qualifications: Required: Prior coaching experience at the high school, club, or collegiate level. Strong knowledge of the sport’s fundamentals, rules, and strategies. Excellent leadership, communication, and organizational skills. Ability to foster a positive and inclusive team environment. CPR/First Aid certification or willingness to obtain. Must pass a background check as required by the school district. Preferred: Previous experience as an assistant coach. Knowledge of strength and conditioning principles. Experience with student-athlete development programs. Benefits: Stipend-based compensation. Professional development opportunities. Access to school athletic facilities and resources.

Vail Unifiied is looking for a Baseball coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Baseball Coach position at Kate Robold is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Baseball Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Baseball Coach at Kate Robold sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Kate Robold, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Baseball Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Baseball Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Baseball Coach position at Kate Robold is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Weld County District Re-5j is looking for a Volunteer Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Terms: This position does not qualify for benefits and is a volunteer only position.
GENERAL STATEMENT OF RESPONSIBILITY The volunteer coach in each sport assigned is directly responsible to the head coach of that sport. He/she should have a working knowledge of the rules and regulations of the Re-5J Board of Education and the athletic standards of Roosevelt High School. He/she should also be familiar with the rules and regulations of the League and the Colorado High School Activities Association.
PROFESSIONAL QUALIFICATIONS 1. Teaching assignment at Weld County School District RE-5J. If not staff, must have successfully completed certified coaching class and be certified by CHSAA. 2. Successful experience in his/her chosen coaching area. 3. Successful background check. 4. Such alternatives to the above qualifications as may be found appropriate and acceptable.
PERSONAL QUALIFICATIONS 1. Encourage the student body and spectators to be courteous and polite to the visiting team. 2. Always display good sportsmanship, winning or losing. 3. Maintain poise and control at all times, especially at the contests. 4. Teach the team to play fairly and within the rules. 5. Be a good host to the visiting team and their coaches. 6. Recommend discipline to the head coach for players who disregard good sportsmanship either on the field/floor or on the bench. 7. Respect the officials’ judgement and interpretation of the rules. If an appeal is necessary, follow appropriate procedures. 8. Coach the team and not officiate the game. 9. Publicity attempt to shake hands with officials and the opposing coach prior to and after the game.
SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SEASONAL 1. Assist the head coach in carrying out his/her responsibilities. 2. Assist the head coach is the issuance of equipment, registration of the players, and providing accurate information needed to compile eligibility lists and other reports. 3. Assume responsibility for care of equipment and facilities. 4. Assume control over athletes and teams assigned to him/her and assist in helping supervise all athletes in the program. 5. Be in regular attendance at practice, contests, and meetings. 6. Discipline, when necessary, in firm and positive manner. 7. Provide the head coach with information needed in making reports and/or other releases. 8. Emphasize safety and be aware of the best training and injury.
END OF SEASON 1. Assist in the return and inventory of school equipment. 2. Recommend athletes for letter awards. 3. Recommend equipment to be purchased.

Job.JobDescription:
Valley High School is looking for a Volleyball Athletics/Activities/Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
Head Coach - Volleyball for the 2026-2027 season for Valley High School, Gilcrest, CO. Salary stipend is based on experience. Background check required. Go to the Weld RE-1 website to apply: http://www.wcsdre1.org
The Head Valley High School Volleyball Coach is responsible for leading and managing all aspects of the school's volleyball program, including developing student-athletes' skills, fostering teamwork and sportsmanship, and promoting a positive, competitive culture aligned with our Athletic Department values of integrity, accountability, commitment, and discipline. The head coach plans and conducts practices, oversees game preparation and strategy, supervises assistant coaches, and ensures compliance with school, district, and CHSAA policies/bylaws. Responsibilities include but are not limited to: supporting academic success, modeling strong character and leadership, promoting athlete safety and well-being, organizing offseason development opportunities, scheduling transportation, leading fundraising efforts, overseeing the program budget, maintaining a yearly inventory, and building positive relationships with students, families, staff, and the community. The Head Coach also assists with program promotion, player development at all levels, and the long-term growth and sustainability of the volleyball program. Previous high school head coaching experience is encouraged but not a requirement.

Job.JobTitle: High School Assistant Girls Wrestling Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Strasburg School District 31-J is looking for a Wrestling Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
Job Title: Assistant Wrestling Coach Reports to: Athletic Director Evaluated by: Head Coach and Athletic Director Job Goal: The Assistant Wrestling Coach is responsible to instruct in the fundamental skills, prepare and strategize on the needed training for competition, and foster individual growth as an individual athlete and as a teammate. Essential Requirements: To perform this job successfully an individual must be able to execute each requirement satisfactorily. The requirements list below are a representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability needed for the position. Basic Knowledge of wrestling and has participated in wrestling. Good communication skills. Ability to communicate effectively and resolve conflict with students, parents, and community groups. Ability to provide leadership and act with professionalism and integrity with school staff, students, parents, and community groups. Essential Duties & Responsibilities: The assistant coach is responsible for, and supervises all aspects of their level of the program with direction of the head coach. Schedules and attends practice sessions, develops and implements individual/team training plans by the day, week, month, year as appropriate for their level in conjunction with the overall program goals/philosophy. Complies with the rules and regulations as established by the Colorado High School Activities Association. Monitors athlete's attendance/behavioral/academic status. Understands the signs and symptoms of concussion and removes players that are exhibiting signs and symptoms of concussion. Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards students, officials, and spectators. Ability to drive students in a school vehicle to and from competitions. Performs other related duties as assigned or requested. Certificates, Licenses, Registrations, Bonding and/or Testing Required: Successfully pass the CHSAA coaches test, acquire CHSAA coaching registration and complete a concussion course per State Legislation and CHSAA By-Laws. CPR & AED Certifications required. Physical Demands/Work Environment: Between sitting, standing, and walking throughout the day lifting up to 50 pounds on an occasional basis. Days / Terms of Employment: Days and Terms to be determined by the Athletic Director. Questions: Please contact Rick Jacoby: rjacoby@strasburg31j.com Position Type: Part-Time Salary: $2,837 to $3,011 Stipend Job Requirements Citizenship, residency or work visa required

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Middle School Track and Field Coach 2025-2026
Job.JobDescription:
Weld Central Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Coach - Assistant to join our program. Apply now.
Position Type: Athletics/Activities/Coach - Assistant Date Posted: 1/15/2026 Location: Weld Central Middle School Date Available: Approx. March 2026 Closing Date: Open until filled Assistant Track and Field Coach at Weld Central Middle School for 2025-2026 Season typically runs March through May (dates subject to CHSAA approval). Location: Keenesburg, CO (travel required) Salary/Job Requirements Stipend of $2,325.00 based on level 4 of the Extra Duty Schedule, divided into semi-monthly installments or paid in lump sum at discretion of the district High School Diploma or GED required Previous coaching experience preferred and must have knowledge of the game Work closely with the Athletic Director and other coaching staff Must have the ability to work in inclement weather Physical ability to lift and move sports equipment Must be able to pass the CHSAA test and CHSAA Concussion test May require small vehicle training through the District (if applicable) Must be able to pass basic background check and have fingerprints completed upon hire As required by the Immigration Reform and Control Act (IRCA), the selected applicant must verify within 72 hours of employment, his/her legal right to be employed in the United States. Must submit an online application. Please see attached job description for more information
