Job.JobDescription:
Northridge High School is looking for a Soccer Athletics/Activities/High School Coaching to join our program. Apply now.
Classification: Non-Exempt Date Posted: January 30, 2026 Salary: Salary placement on our Co-Curricular Salary schedule is dependent on the sport, club, organization or program and the employees' in-district experience as outlined in Article XXIII of the Master Contract. Click here for more information. Contract Length/ Days: (2025-2026/ Varies) Benefits: We offer a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision and life insurance as well as other programs for benefit-eligible employees. The employee assistance program, voluntary life insurance through PERA, and 401(k), 403(b) & 457(b) plans are available to all employees regardless of hours worked and are available immediately (upon hire date). To learn more about our benefits, please see our Benefits Overview. Paid Leave: Depending on your position, the District offers a variety of leave options, including sick leave, discretionary leave, vacation and paid holidays. More information about leave options available to Classified employees can be found in the Board of Education Policy Manual. Reports to: School Athletic Director Deadline for application: Until filled EDUCATION/EXPERIENCE Required: High School Diploma/GED CPR certification CHSAA test and regulation Concussion Education Prior coaching experience in this sport Preferred: Bilingual skills Any other combination of related education, experience and training JOB DESCRIPTION Summary/Objective: This person will work closely with the head coach supporting their plan. They will also be in charge of running their own aspects of practice given the skill they are put in charge of. Another aspect of their duties will be to supervise students until they have been picked up from practice and/or meets. Essential Functions: Organizing and supervising game help. Assists with supervision at activities. Additional Qualifications: Applicant must complete concussion training. Other Duties: Knowledge, Skills, and Mental Ability Knowledge of Colorado and district content standards. Knowledge and skills sufficient to perform all duties of the position. Prefers leadership situations relating to meeting the social, emotional, psychological and academic needs of students. Prefers a work environment where flexibility, ability to deal with conflict, a high tolerance for stress, an ability to quickly internalize information and make informed decisions, and long hours are a part of the assignment. Please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. Duties, responsibilities and activities may change at any time with or without notice. Regular attendance is required for this position. Supervisory Responsibilities: None Work Environment/ Physical Demands: Ability to sit for long periods of time, numerous distractions, visual, auditory and ambulatory acuity important. Occasional lifting of up to 30 pounds. Travel: None Thank you for your interest in working for Greeley-Evans School District 6. Please feel free to contact our Human Resource Office at 970-348-6071 with any questions. EEO/AAP Statement Greeley-Evans School District 6 shall not discriminate in its employment or hiring practices on the basis of race, color, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression, gender identity, religion, national origin, ancestry, age, genetic information, veteran status, marital status or disability. The District shall identify, solicit, and consider applicants for employment from a broad spectrum of qualified individuals who will contribute to that effort. The Superintendent is committed to cultural diversity among district personnel as a means of enriching educational experience. The District shall identify, solicit, and consider applicants for employment from a broad spectrum of qualified individuals who will contribute to that effort.

Eagle Valley High School is looking for a Lacrosse coach to join our program. Apply now.
SUMMARY OF JOB DESCRIPTION: Instructs athletes in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student(s) shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
SUPERVISED BY: Head Coach and Athletic Director
SUPERVISES: Athletes
ESSENTIAL JOB ELEMENTS: Teach the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques as directed by the head coach. Arrange for the issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment. Responsible for meeting with parents and participants prior to the start of the sport season. Follow program expectations, procedures and policies developed by head coach. Complete delegated duties as assigned by head coach. Evaluate payers and give input on practice scheduling. Have knowledge of athletic policies approved by the Eagle County Schools and CHSSA. Have knowledge and be able to implement CHSAA and league regulations. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled practices/sporting events or and adheres to scheduled facility times. Assist in providing documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examination, parental consent, and eligibility, and proof of insurance. Monitor student athlete’s grades and conduct in regards to eligibility. Supervise athletic participants from the time they report to the practice site or game until they leave at the conclusion of the activity. Follow training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete participant. Direct and guide student managers. Monitor athletic areas. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports’ programs. Responsible for knowledge, understanding and enforcing all aspects of the Eagle County Schools athletic handbook. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, their care, and proper use. Secure all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving the building. Attend all practices, games, and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with parents, officials, volunteers and fans. Assist the athletic director in scheduling and requirements for tournaments and special sporting events. Follow the proper administrative chain of command and refer all requests/concerns accordingly. Provide proper safeguard for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and contest results. Perform other duties assigned. Utilize only team building exercises that do not include harassment or hazing. Within 24 hours of an incident, completes paperwork on all athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office.
CONTACTS: All students, staff, parents, and community representatives.
QUALIFICATIONS: Knowledge of the technical aspects of the sport and at the same time must continue to examine new theories and procedures pertinent to the field Successful coaching experience in assigned sport Demonstrated effective problem solving skills
Position Type: As Needed

Dolores School District Re-4a is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The HS Boys Head Baseball Coach position at Dolores School District Re-4a provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the HS Boys Head Baseball Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Dolores School District Re-4a, the HS Boys Head Baseball Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The HS Boys Head Baseball Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the HS Boys Head Baseball Coach at Dolores School District Re-4a is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.CompanyDescription: Dolores School District Re-4a coaching jobs

Job.JobTitle: Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning
Job.JobDescription:
Colorado College is looking for a Weightlifting Strength and Conditioning to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning at Colorado College is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At Colorado College, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Director and Coach, Strength and Conditioning at Colorado College allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.

Sabin Middle School is looking for a Soccer coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Middle School Soccer Coach at Sabin Middle School is a comprehensive coaching role that combines instruction, mentorship, and program management. This long‑form description reflects the multifaceted nature of school coaching, where the coach is both a technical expert and a caring adult presence in the lives of student‑athletes. The position is ideal for someone who values structure, communication, and collaboration while maintaining a clear focus on student development.
Day to day, the Middle School Soccer Coach plans and leads practices that prioritize safety, learning, and steady improvement. A typical session might include dynamic warm‑ups, small‑group drills, competitive games, and review conversations that help athletes understand why they are working on certain skills. The coach explains expectations clearly, uses age‑appropriate language, and adapts instruction so that athletes at different experience levels can participate and progress.
Another key element of the job is fostering positive habits. Coaches at Sabin Middle School reinforce punctuality, preparedness, and respectful communication. The Middle School Soccer Coach expects athletes to bring the proper equipment, to listen when others are speaking, and to take responsibility for their effort and behavior. When mistakes occur, the coach responds with guidance and accountability rather than embarrassment, reinforcing that learning is an ongoing process.
The role also emphasizes alignment with the school’s mission. Athletics is treated as part of the educational program, not as a separate or higher priority. That means the Middle School Soccer Coach supports academic expectations, respects teachers’ roles, and encourages athletes to manage their time effectively. By checking in on grades and reinforcing eligibility requirements, the coach helps students see the connection between their everyday decisions and their opportunities to compete.
Operationally, the position involves logistics such as coordinating with transportation, ensuring facilities are left in good condition, communicating schedules, and maintaining equipment inventories. The coach understands district policies, league rules, and safety procedures, and follows them consistently. Maintaining required certifications and participating in professional development keeps the coach informed about best practices in training and athlete welfare.
In addition, the Middle School Soccer Coach may contribute to long‑term program building through youth clinics, off‑season activities, and collaboration with other levels of the program. By investing in relationships with families and younger athletes, the coach helps sustain interest and continuity in the sport at Sabin Middle School. Candidates who enjoy balancing big‑picture planning with day‑to‑day teaching will find this position engaging and fulfilling.

Doherty High School is looking for a Cross Country coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Cross Country Coach at Doherty High School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Head Cross Country Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Doherty High School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Head Cross Country Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Head Cross Country Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Doherty High School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Doherty High School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Head Cross Country Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Head Cross Country Coach role at Doherty High School.

Mitchell High School is looking for a Baseball Volunteer to join our program. Apply now.
The Varsity Baseball Volunteer Pitching Coach position at Mitchell High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.

The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Mitchell High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Varsity Baseball Volunteer Pitching Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Varsity Baseball Volunteer Pitching Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Varsity Baseball Volunteer Pitching Coach position at Mitchell High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Russell Middle School is looking for a Football coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Football Coach at Russell Middle School is designed for a coach who believes that athletics is a powerful tool for teaching life skills. This role centers on developing student‑athletes as people first and competitors second, helping them learn responsibility, perseverance, and teamwork in a structured and supportive environment. The program places a strong emphasis on clear communication, mutual respect, and long‑term development rather than short‑term results alone.
On a daily basis, the Football Coach is responsible for planning and delivering practices that are active, safe, and inclusive. Training sessions should include a balance of fundamentals, decision‑making drills, and live or game‑like situations so that athletes are constantly learning. The coach provides direct instruction, demonstrates proper technique, and offers individualized feedback to help each athlete progress from their current skill level.
In addition to technical coaching, this position carries significant mentoring responsibilities. At Russell Middle School, coaches are expected to build positive relationships with student‑athletes and to support them through the challenges of school, competition, and adolescence. That means listening carefully, encouraging good choices, and reinforcing expectations related to attendance, academic effort, and personal conduct. The Football Coach helps create a team environment where athletes feel valued and where they understand that their actions affect the group.
The role also includes collaboration with the broader athletic department. The Football Coach works with the athletic director and other coaches to coordinate facility use, align program philosophies, and support multi‑sport participation when possible. Sharing information, attending department meetings, and contributing to school‑wide initiatives all help ensure that the program at Russell Middle School operates smoothly and consistently from season to season.
Game‑day responsibilities include preparing athletes physically and mentally, organizing lineups, communicating adjustments, and modeling composure in competitive situations. The coach is expected to represent Russell Middle School with professionalism toward officials, opponents, and fans. After competitions, the Football Coach reviews performance with the team, highlighting effort, improvement, and areas for growth.
Health and safety are a constant priority. The coach follows district and state guidelines for equipment use, conditioning progressions, and emergency response. Maintaining appropriate certifications and staying current with best practices helps ensure that every training and competition environment is as safe as possible for all participants. Candidates who care deeply about student development, enjoy teaching the details of the sport, and are committed to a positive, educational approach to athletics will be strong fits for the Football Coach role at Russell Middle School.

Mitchell High School is looking for a Track & Field coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head and Assistant Track Coach position at Mitchell High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the Head and Assistant Track Coach prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Mitchell High School, the Head and Assistant Track Coach works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The Head and Assistant Track Coach encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the Head and Assistant Track Coach at Mitchell High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.

Colorado Springs D11 is looking for a coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Hockey Coach role at D11 Team is focused on building a competitive, well‑organized program that still prioritizes the overall well‑being of student‑athletes. This position blends strategy, culture‑building, and teaching, providing the coach with an opportunity to influence the direction of the program for years to come. The successful candidate will bring a clear vision for how to teach the sport, how to manage a team, and how to represent the school in a positive and professional manner.
From a competitive standpoint, the Head Hockey Coach is responsible for developing seasonal plans that align practice content, conditioning work, and game preparation. This includes designing practice segments that progress from individual fundamentals to full‑team tactical execution. The coach should be comfortable breaking down film, identifying areas for improvement, and communicating game plans in a way that athletes can understand and apply under pressure.
Equally important is the culture created by the coach. At D11 Team, teams are expected to demonstrate sportsmanship, respect, and unity. The Head Hockey Coach sets standards for effort in practice, behavior in the classroom, and conduct in the community. That might involve establishing team guidelines, leading discussions about values, and recognizing athletes who demonstrate leadership or growth in those areas. The goal is to build a program where success is measured not just by wins and losses but by the quality of the experience for everyone involved.
This position also involves close coordination with the academic side of the school. The coach monitors eligibility, encourages students to keep up with assignments, and celebrates academic milestones. Communicating with teachers and counselors when necessary helps support student‑athletes as whole people, not just as performers in one activity. At D11 Team, the Head Hockey Coach is viewed as part of the educational team.
Program administration tasks include managing equipment and uniforms, tracking inventory, assisting with scheduling, and following all district and league regulations. The coach should be organized, punctual, and attentive to details such as transportation arrangements and roster paperwork. Safety protocols, including emergency action planning and injury‑reporting procedures, must be consistently followed.
Overall, the Head Hockey Coach at D11 Team is best suited for a coach who is excited about teaching, willing to collaborate with colleagues, and prepared to invest in the growth of student‑athletes over multiple seasons. It is an opportunity to provide structure, challenge, and support while representing the school with pride in the larger athletic community.

Job.JobTitle: MS Assistant Track and Field Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Hemphill Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Part-Time to join our program. Apply now.
Job Goal: The Assisstant Track & Field Coach is responsible to instruct in the fundamental skills, prepare and strategize on the needed training for competition, and foster individual growth as an individual athlete and as a teammate. Essential Requirements: To perform this job successfully an individual must be able to execute each requirement satisfactorily. The requirements list below are a representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability needed for the position. * Basic knowledge of track & field and has participated in track and field events. * Good communication skills. * Ability to communicate effectively and resolve conflict with students, parents, and community groups. * Ability to provide leadership and act with professionalism and integrity with school staff, students, parents, and community groups. Essential Duties & Responsibilities: * Models sports-like behavior and maintains appropriate conduct towards students, officials, and spectators. * The assistant coach is responsible for, and supervises all aspects of their level of the program with direction of the head coach. * Schedules and attends practice sessions, develops and implements individual/team training plans by the day, week, month, year as appropriate for their level in conjunction with the overall program goals/philosophy. * Complies with the rules and regulations as established by the Colorado High School Activities Association. * Monitors athlete's attendance/behavioral/academic status. * Participates in special activities to include parents' night, banquets, award nights, and prep assemblies. * Understands the signs and symptoms of concussion and removes players that are exhibiting signs and symptoms of concussion. * Ability to drive students in a school vehicle to and from competitions. * Performs other related duties as assigned or requested. Certificates, Licenses, Registrations, Bonding and/or Testing Required: * Successfully pass the CHSAA coaches test, acquire CHSAA coaching registration and complete a concussion course per State Legislation and CHSAA By-Laws. * CPR & AED Certifications required. Physical Demands/Work Environment: Between sitting, standing, and walking throughout the day lifting up to 50 pounds on an occasional basis and 10 pounds on a frequent basis, occasionally stooping, kneeling, crouching, overhead reaching and fine motor activity, frequently sitting, standing, walking, balancing, reaching at desk and floor level, firm and simple grasping. The work requires constant seeing, hearing, and talking. FLSA Status: Non-exempt Days / Terms of Employment: Days and Terms to be determined by the Athletic Director. Position Type: Part-Time Salary: $1,466 to $1,556 Per Year Job Requirements * Citizenship, residency or work visa required
Job.Experience: Basic knowledge of track & field and has participated in track and field events

Del Norte High School is looking for a Dance Team/Cheer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The HIGH SCHOOL CHEER COACH position at Del Norte High School is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The HIGH SCHOOL CHEER COACH is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Del Norte High School, coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The HIGH SCHOOL CHEER COACH encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.

By the District to occur during the school year related to this position/posting.

Reports consistently for work as scheduled Performs other duties as assigned Attains proficient or higher evaluations on established Performance Standards Actively seeks feedback from supervisor(s) and/or colleagues Communicates high expectations for athlete behavior and reinforces expectations Communicates regularly with the Athletic Director Consistently uses positive language and interactions for team growth and development Effectively creates a competitive environment in which all athletes have a chance to experience both individual and team success Effectively identifies and applies a variety of strategies and tactics to maximize opportunities for success in competitions for the individual and/or the team Equipment is consistently maintained, inventoried, stored, and re-ordered prior to the beginning of the season. The coach is expected to bring consistency and enthusiasm to each interaction with the team.
Practices led by the Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS are structured and purposeful. They begin with appropriate warm‑ups, transition into focused skill work, and conclude with competitive or situational activities that resemble live play. The coach introduces concepts in manageable steps and checks for understanding, encouraging athletes to ask questions and learn the reasoning behind drills and strategies. This approach helps athletes become smarter, more confident players.
Motivation is another key component of the job. Coaches at ThunderRidge High School use positive reinforcement, high yet realistic expectations, and clear feedback to help athletes push themselves. The Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS recognizes effort and improvement, not just outcomes, and supports athletes through setbacks. This creates an environment where students are willing to take risks and grow.
The role also includes a strong communication element. The coach keeps families informed about schedules, travel, and program expectations, and responds to questions in a respectful, timely manner. Within the school, the Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS collaborates with administrators and teachers to support students’ academic responsibilities, reinforcing that success in the classroom is essential.
Finally, the position requires adherence to safety and policy standards. The Assistant Coach - Boys Lacrosse - HS maintains appropriate supervision ratios, follows guidelines on equipment use, and ensures that emergency procedures are understood and practiced. The coach models appropriate conduct toward officials and opponents, reinforcing that competing with integrity is a core expectation at ThunderRidge High School. This comprehensive role is well suited to coaches who are organized, student‑focused, and eager to contribute to a strong athletic tradition.

Good rapport and demonstrated competency are invaluable for the coach. 2. Cooperation The district and school expect the highest level of cooperation by all coaches and athletic staff members in the execution of their duties. Coaches must work cooperatively with their school athletic director, administration, other staff, team and parents. 3. In this regard, the implementation of discipline and the attitude with which it is implemented is the coach's responsibility. Desire to do well, to win gracefully, and to lose with poise and a positive attitude should be emphasized. Staff, players and spectators should be motivated to achieve established goals as a team and as individual players. 5. Improvement All coaches must constantly take advantage of opportunities presented for self-improvement.

Job.JobTitle: 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt
Job.JobDescription:
Gateway High School is looking for a Baseball Non-contracted Jobs to join our program. Apply now.
The 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt at Gateway High School is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Gateway High School, the 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Gateway High School well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The 25-26 Gateway HS :AFS-Assistant Coach, Boys Baseball, Exempt follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.
Job.Location: Gateway High School, Aurora, CO, 80012, US

Job.JobTitle: 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt
Job.JobDescription:
Hinkley High School is looking for a Basketball Non-contracted jobs to join our program. Apply now.
The 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt position at Hinkley High School provides an opportunity to guide student‑athletes through an experience that combines challenge, support, and growth. This role is intended for coaches who see beyond wins and losses and who value the lasting impact that a positive athletic program can have on young people. Through careful planning, consistent expectations, and ongoing communication, the coach helps create a framework where athletes can thrive.
From an instructional perspective, the 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt prepares practices that sequence learning effectively. Early sessions may focus heavily on fundamentals and basic systems, while later practices refine execution and introduce more advanced concepts. The coach uses demonstrations, targeted drills, and guided scrimmages to help athletes apply what they learn. Regular assessment allows the coach to adjust plans based on the team’s progress.
The position also requires deliberate attention to team culture. At Hinkley High School, the 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt works to ensure that the environment is welcoming, fair, and focused on collective responsibility. The coach sets expectations around effort, communication, and respect, and follows through when those expectations are not met. Celebrating positive examples and giving athletes leadership opportunities strengthens the sense of ownership within the group.
Support for the broader student experience is built into the job. Coaches stay informed about academic calendars, test periods, and other school events so that practices and competitions are scheduled thoughtfully whenever possible. The 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt encourages athletes to communicate about conflicts in advance and to take initiative in managing their commitments. When students feel supported in both academics and athletics, they are more likely to remain engaged and successful.
Administrative responsibilities include coordination with the athletic department, assisting with transportation, submitting required paperwork, and maintaining accurate rosters. The coach must be attentive to detail and willing to complete these tasks reliably, as they directly affect the experience of athletes and families.
At its core, the 25-26 Hinkley HS ATH:AFS-Head Coach, Boys Basketball, Exempt at Hinkley High School is a chance to teach, to mentor, and to build something that extends beyond a single season. Candidates who are motivated by the opportunity to help students grow through sport will find this position especially meaningful.
Job.Location: Hinkley High School, Aurora, CO, 80011, US

Cañon City High School is looking for a Soccer Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS:
All Colorado High School Activities Association member school coaches of interscholastic activity programs who assume full responsibility for students under their direction at any level shall be either: A certified teacher or administrator holding a current certificate issued by the Colorado Department of Education; or Employed as a teacher, counselor and or administrator at least 50% of the school day in a private school; or A coach registered with the CHSAA. Coaches not holding a CDE-issued teacher or administrator certificate, shall be registered with the CHSAA in one of the following capacities: One-year Coaching Registration requested by a member school A one-time renewal of the Coaching Registration request Permanent Coaching Registration (applied for and held by individual coach) RESPONSIBILITIES AND EXPECTATIONS
COACHES' PROFESSIONAL AND PERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AND EXPECTATIONS:
1. Rapport A coach must be able to develop good rapport with numerous individuals and community groups; team personnel, the student body, the professional staff, (faculty, administration, maintenance, etc.), the community as a whole, spectators, officials, fellow coaches in the conference, media representatives and the parents of his/her players. Good rapport and demonstrated competency are invaluable for the coach in his/her position.
2. To his/her team A coach must demonstrate an interest in the players as individuals as well as athletes. He/she must treat them in a manner that reflects genuine human concern for their general well-being, while still motivating them to their highest degree of athletic achievement.
3. To the head coach It is essential that each assistant coach has a complete understanding of and belief in the head coach's philosophy and program. An assistant must carry out his/her responsibilities as assigned by the head coach in a manner that will reflect positively upon the head coach, the staff, and the program. Each assistant has an ethical obligation to be loyal to the head coach.
The head coach should expect all staff to contribute a full measure of time, effort, thought and energy to the program. Expectations should be communicated clearly in advance of the season. Assistant coaches should be valued and praised appropriately for their contributions to the success of the program.
Assistant coaches should have a strong sense of allegiance to the program. They must be willing and able to do things that they may not wish to do, or even like to do. They must fulfill their responsibility to the head coach, the athletes, and the sport itself. They must be willing to put in a full measure of time, effort, thought and energy to the program and the head coach.
5. To other coaches in the athletic program A coach must bear in mind that his/her sport is part of the total athletic educational program of Canon City Schools. Therefore, it is important to support, promote and cooperate with all the other coaches and activity sponsors for the well being of the total program.
Coaches should communicate regularly about athletes and off-season schedules to allow athletes to participate in a wide variety of activities.
A coach should support and cooperate with other coaches whenever possible. All remarks should reflect confidence in one's fellow coaches. A strong, harmonious, interpersonal relationship must exist among coaches and other faculty members.
6. To faculty members A coach is expected to cooperate with every faculty member. If the coach cooperates with the teachers of academic subjects by allowing an athlete to make up a test or homework on practice time, that respect for the athletic program, coaching staff and the values of athletics will be significantly enhanced.
7. To the physical plant Each coach is responsible for: Keeping the practice area and locker rooms in order Storing equipment and using equipment properly Keeping locker rooms and storage areas locked Filing proper maintenance requests on large items of need
Pride in the equipment and facilities is of primary importance to all athletes, coaches, and community.
TO APPLY:
A district application must be completed online at: https://jobs.redroverk12.com/org/canoncityschools
Anticipated Timeline for Hiring: Open until filled Screening of Applications: Thursday, February 12, 2026

Job.JobDescription:
Sterling High School is looking for a Golf Athletics/Activities/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Boys Golf Coach is responsible for supporting the team throughout the season. Leadership is to be provided by the coach in the following areas: Coordination of varsity and lower-level teams. Development of expectations and direction of the golf program. Tracking of eligibility and academic progress of students. Preparation of teams for competition. Teaching fundamental and advanced skills. Attend coaches' meetings, league coaches' meetings, and professional development. Communication with parents, players, staff, and media via RE-1 Valley School District-approved media. Following school district regulations and policies. ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS: Coaches individual participants in the skills necessary for effective performance in the activity, as individuals & team members. Plans and directs a regular program of practice in season. Maintains competency in rules, regulations, and coaching techniques for the sport/activity. Maintains discipline and sportsmanlike conduct of all participants and enforces the policies of the District uniformly when disciplining participants. Supervises student athletes when traveling. Oversees the safety conditions for students and facility, and the areas in which the sport is conducted during all times students are present. Demonstrates cooperative conduct. Shares in responsibility for equipment, distribution, collection and inventory. Only minimum duties are listed. Other functions may be required as given or assigned. QUALIFICATIONS: Required: Must be 21 years of age, or older High School Diploma or equivalent Complete and submit an RE-1 Valley Schools online application Willing and able to drive to away games, camps, scrimmages Knowledgeable in the techniques and strategies for the sport/activity. Ability to read & interpret documents such as safety rules, operating & maintenance instructions, and procedure manuals. Ability to follow both oral and written directions & instructions from the athletic director. Ability to write routine reports and correspondence. Ability to effectively communicate, orally and in writing, with students, parents, staff and the community. Ability to handle stressful situations. Ability to maintain confidentiality of employment and student matters. Ability to perform duties with an awareness of all district requirements and board policies. Ability to effectively manage time and responsibilities. Other qualifications as deemed necessary by the athletic director. Preferred: At least 3 years of high school and/or college coaching experience. Knowledge of all aspects of senior high school activity programming. Proven leadership qualities and successful experience in working with high school students. Background/experience in coaching high school golf, or equivalent Application and Selection Procedure: Candidates: Apply online at https://www.applitrack.com/re1valley/onlineapp/
Job.SalaryRange: $2342.00 to be paid at the end of the golf season
Job.CompanyDescription: Valley School District RE-1 coaching jobs

Job.JobDescription:
Eagle Valley High School is looking for a Lacrosse coach to join our program. Apply now.
Instructs athletes in the fundamental skills, strategies, and physical training necessary for them to realize a degree of individual and team success. At the same time, the student(s) shall receive instruction that will lead to the formulation of moral values, pride of accomplishment, acceptable social behavior, self-discipline and self-confidence.
SUPERVISED BY: Head Coach and Athletic Director
SUPERVISES: Athletes
ESSENTIAL JOB ELEMENTS:
Teach the fundamental philosophy, skills, and techniques as directed by the head coach. Arrange for the issuing, storing, and reconditioning of equipment. Responsible for meeting with parents and participants prior to the start of the sport season. Follow program expectations, procedures and policies developed by head coach. Complete delegated duties as assigned by head coach. Evaluate payers and give input on practice scheduling. Have knowledge of athletic policies approved by the Eagle County Schools and CHSSA. Have knowledge and be able to implement CHSAA and league regulations. Assist in the necessary preparation to hold scheduled practices/sporting events or and adheres to scheduled facility times. Assist in providing documentation to fulfill state and system requirements concerning physical examination, parental consent, and eligibility, and proof of insurance. Monitor student athlete's grades and conduct in regards to eligibility. Supervise athletic participants from the time they report to the practice site or game until they leave at the conclusion of the activity. Follow training rules and any other unique regulations of the sport to each athlete participant. Direct and guide student managers. Monitor athletic areas. Promote the sport within the school through recruiting athletes who are not in another sports' programs. Responsible for knowledge, understanding and enforcing all aspects of the Eagle County Schools athletic handbook. Instill in each player a respect for equipment and school property, their care, and proper use. Secure all doors, lights, windows and locks before leaving the building. Attend all practices, games, and while traveling, provides assistance, guidance, and safeguards for each participant. Responsible for maintaining good public relations with parents, officials, volunteers and fans. Assist the athletic director in scheduling and requirements for tournaments and special sporting events. Follow the proper administrative chain of command and refer all requests/concerns accordingly. Provide proper safeguard for maintenance and protection of assigned equipment sites. Present information to news media concerning schedules, tournaments and contest results. Perform other duties assigned. Utilize only team building exercises that do not include harassment or hazing. Within 24 hours of an incident, completes paperwork on all athletic injuries on proper forms and submits to athletic office.

Sand Creek High School is looking for a Football Athletics/Activities to join our program. Apply now.
ATHLETIC COACH PERSONNEL VACANCY REGULAR SUPPORT FOOTBALL COACH SAND CREEK HIGH SCHOOL
ABOUT THE POSITION School District 49 is actively seeking a regular support coach for Football at Sand Creek High School.
Reports to: Head Coach, Athletic Director and Building Administration Stipend Amount: Dependent upon the extra-curricular salary schedule. Payments are typically made in one lump sum payment of $3,036. Benefits: No benefits available
Interviews are site-based and not conducted by the Human Resources office. If you have questions regarding the status of an open or recently closed position, you may contact the site where the vacancy is located
ABOUT DISTRICT 49 School District 49, the fastest growing district in the Pikes Peak region, hosts 23 exceptional schools and programs within four distinct innovation zones, providing preK-12-aligned curriculum, online and alternative programming and homeschool enrichment. Home to more than 19,000 students and 1,500 staff members, District 49 aims to be the best choice to learn, work and lead. As one of Colorado's only two recognized Districts of Innovation, District 49 is a challenging and stimulating place to lead. As part of our commitment to innovate, we host a robust portfolio of conventional, charter, online, and blended schools, including homeschool support as well as career and technical education.
We are passionate about providing individualized educational experiences that launch every student toward success. Our teachers, administrators and staff members are committed to the creation of educational environments where all children can maximize their individual learning potential. Further, we are committed to meeting students’ needs in all domains: academic, social and physical. An emphasis in all activities will be toward empowering students to take an active role in their own learning.
See attached job description for details about this position.
EOE M/F/D/V
APPLICATION INSTRUCTIONS The entire application process is completed online via the Applitrack system. For your application to be deemed complete, you must complete the application in its entirety including all applicable disclosure questions.
NON-DISCRIMINATION DISCLOSURE District 49 does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex, disability, or age in its programs and activities and provides equal access to the Boy Scouts and other designated youth groups. For further information on notice of non-discrimination, visit the Office of Civil Rights for the address and phone number of the office that serves your area, or call 1-800-421-3481.

Coaches must work cooperatively with their school athletic director, administration, other staff, team and parents. 3. Leadership and Public Relations Diligence, enthusiasm, integrity, honesty and a love for the game are all part of the professional pride that should be exhibited by any coach. Personal appearance, dress- to include proper coaching attire and shoes, physical condition, etc. should be exemplary. Public demeanor and decorum should always reflect positively on the coach, team, athletic department, school and community. 4. Discipline Every athletic team requires a high level of discipline among coaches, players, and support staff. In this regard, the implementation of discipline and the attitude with which it is implemented is the coach's responsibility. Desire to do well, to win gracefully, and to lose with poise and a positive attitude should be emphasized. Season: Fall Season 2026 Location: Cañon City High School Salary: Stipend is based on the Supplemental Salary Schedule and years of experience. Starting supplemental of $1,871-$3,996 Other Information: All coaching assignments are one-year contracts.
QUALIFICATIONS AND SKILLS: All coaches of interscholastic activity programs who assume full responsibility for students under their direction at any level shall be either: A certified teacher or administrator holding a current certificate issued by the Colorado Department of Education; or Employed as a teacher, counselor or and administrator at least 50% of the school day in a private school; or A registered volunteer with Canon City Schools
RESPONSIBILITIES AND EXPECTATIONS:
COACHES' PROFESSIONAL AND PERSONAL RELATIONSHIPS AND EXPECTATIONS: 1. Rapport A coach must be able to develop good rapport with numerous individuals and community groups; team personnel, the student body, the professional staff, (faculty, administration, maintenance, etc.), the community as a whole, spectators, officials, fellow coaches in the conference, media representatives and the parents of his/her players. Good rapport and demonstrated competency are invaluable for the coach. 2. Cooperation The district and school expect the highest level of cooperation by all coaches and athletic staff members in the execution of their duties. Coaches must work cooperatively with their school athletic director, administration, other staff, team and parents. 3. Leadership and Public Relations Diligence, enthusiasm, integrity, honesty and a love for the game are all part of the professional pride that should be exhibited by any coach. Personal appearance, dress- to include proper coaching attire and shoes, physical condition, etc. should be exemplary. Public demeanor and decorum should always reflect positively on the coach, team, athletic department, school and community. 4. Discipline Every athletic team requires a high level of discipline among coaches, players, and support staff. In this regard, the implementation of discipline and the attitude with which it is implemented is the coach's responsibility. Individually, the coach becomes a model of all that the program represents—observation of school codes, rules of the game, ideals of good sportsmanship, behavior of participants throughout the season and especially where the student body is concerned. Desire to do well, to win gracefully, and to lose with poise and a positive attitude should be emphasized. Staff, players and spectators should be motivated to achieve established goals as a team and as individual players.

Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Baseball Head Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Head Baseball Coach position at Bridgeport School Dist. is an opportunity to shape a positive, inclusive, and hardworking team environment where students learn to love their sport and take pride in representing their school. This role emphasizes relationship‑building, clear expectations, and steady improvement over the course of a season and across multiple years of participation. The coach serves as a daily presence in the lives of student‑athletes, modeling the type of attitude and work ethic that leads to both athletic and personal growth.
At the core of the job is teaching. The Head Baseball Coach is responsible for breaking down complex skills into manageable steps, offering demonstrations, and creating drills that allow athletes to practice safely and effectively. Practices should be organized around clear objectives, with time built in for warm‑up, instruction, repetition, and review. The coach gives frequent feedback, adjusts activities based on what athletes need, and encourages questions so that students remain engaged in their learning.
The role also involves nurturing team chemistry and a sense of belonging. At Bridgeport School Dist., coaches work to make sure every athlete feels included and respected. That means setting guidelines that prevent negative behavior, addressing conflicts quickly and fairly, and celebrating contributions from both starters and role players. The Head Baseball Coach encourages athletes to support one another, communicate openly, and take pride in representing their school with class.
Beyond the field, court, track, or pool, the coach collaborates with school staff to support student‑athletes as whole people. That includes reinforcing academic expectations, respecting school policies, and helping students manage the demands of both athletics and coursework. Regular contact with families keeps everyone informed about schedules, expectations, and program goals, and offers a consistent channel for questions or concerns.
The Head Baseball Coach also participates in broader program responsibilities such as attending department meetings, assisting with youth outreach or clinics, and contributing to long‑term planning. The coach is expected to stay current with best practices in training, injury prevention, and sport‑specific strategy, and to maintain any certifications required by the district or state association.
For individuals who enjoy working with young people, who believe in the value of school‑based athletics, and who are prepared to invest time in building a healthy team culture, the Head Baseball Coach role at Bridgeport School Dist. is a meaningful and impactful opportunity.

Job.JobDescription:
Bridgeport School Dist. is looking for a Football Junior Varsity (JV) Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The JV FOOTBALL COACH at Bridgeport School Dist. is an integral member of the school's educational team, using athletics as a platform for teaching discipline, teamwork, and perseverance. This position calls for a coach who is organized, reflective, and committed to continuous improvement, both for themselves and for their athletes. The role blends hands‑on coaching with program administration and relationship‑building.
From a coaching standpoint, the JV FOOTBALL COACH is responsible for teaching the rules, techniques, and strategies of the sport in a way that is accessible to student‑athletes. Practices are planned to include progressive skill work, position‑specific instruction, and opportunities for athletes to apply learning in controlled scrimmage situations. The coach pays attention to pacing and variety so that sessions remain engaging while still challenging students to stretch their comfort zones.
Culture is a central focus of this job. At Bridgeport School Dist., the JV FOOTBALL COACH helps create a team environment where respect, honesty, and effort are expected. The coach sets clear standards regarding communication, attendance, and sportsmanship, and works with athletes to understand why these standards matter. When expectations are not met, the coach responds fairly, explaining consequences and encouraging athletes to learn from mistakes.
The position also requires responsiveness to student needs. Coaches remain aware of the emotional and academic pressures students face and adjust support accordingly. Checking in with athletes, acknowledging their efforts, and partnering with school staff all contribute to a positive experience. The JV FOOTBALL COACH may assist in connecting students with resources when additional support is needed.
Outside of direct coaching, the role involves tasks such as coordinating with officials and opponents, assisting with scheduling, and managing team communication channels. The coach must be punctual, prepared, and detail‑oriented, ensuring that events run smoothly and that the team represents Bridgeport School Dist. well at home and away.
Safety remains an ongoing responsibility. The JV FOOTBALL COACH follows district guidelines, maintains necessary certifications, and emphasizes proper warm‑up routines and technique to minimize risk of injury. This comprehensive approach to coaching allows student‑athletes to pursue their goals in a structured environment that prioritizes learning, well‑being, and long‑term success.

Job.JobTitle: Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach
Job.JobDescription:
Westhill High School is looking for a Softball Athletic Coaches/Coach to join our program. Apply now.
The Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach position at Westhill High School is a key leadership role within the school's athletic program and wider learning community. This long-form description is designed to give prospective applicants a clear view of the expectations, responsibilities, and culture that surround the role. Coaches in this position are trusted to model character, support academic success, and help student‑athletes grow through meaningful experiences in practice, competition, and daily school life.
In this role, the Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach works closely with the athletic director, school administrators, and other coaches to design and maintain a structured, developmentally appropriate program. That includes planning practices that blend fundamental skill instruction, competitive drills, and situational teaching so athletes understand both the technical and strategic sides of the game. The coach is responsible for teaching core skills, reinforcing proper technique, and helping athletes safely build strength, conditioning, and sport‑specific endurance.
The position also focuses heavily on culture and leadership. The Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach at Westhill High School sets the tone for effort, respect, and sportsmanship. This involves building clear expectations for behavior, guiding athletes through wins and losses with perspective, and maintaining standards that reflect the values of the school and community. Student‑athletes should come away from the program with improved confidence, stronger communication skills, and a better understanding of teamwork and accountability.
Another important part of the job is collaboration. The coach regularly communicates with teachers, counselors, and families to help student‑athletes balance athletics with academics and other responsibilities. At Westhill High School, athletic participation is viewed as an extension of the classroom rather than a replacement for it. The Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach encourages students to stay organized, meet eligibility requirements, and pursue long‑term goals in the classroom as well as on the field or court.
Program operations also fall under this role. The coach helps manage equipment, uniforms, and facilities, and assists with scheduling, transportation coordination, and adherence to league and district policies. The Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach is expected to understand and follow all relevant regulations, including eligibility rules, sportsmanship guidelines, and safety standards, while maintaining current certifications such as CPR/First Aid as required by the district.
Finally, the Westhill High School: Assistant Softball Coach position at Westhill High School is a visible role that represents the school within the larger community. The coach interacts with families, officials, visiting teams, and youth programs, modeling professionalism in every setting. Candidates who are passionate about teaching, committed to student growth, and prepared to invest time and energy into the long‑term health of the program will find this role both challenging and rewarding.

Fred Thomas Middle School is looking for a Track & Field Athletics/ Extracurricular/Assistant Coach to join our program. Apply now.
GENERAL INFORMATION: Fred Thomas Middle School Track Assistant Coach
General Description: The Assistant Coach is responsible for supporting the head coach to ensure that the head coach can lead the team, organize and implement game plans, and foster a positive and competitive environment. The ideal candidate will work collaboratively with the head coach and demonstrate an understanding of the sport.
Minimum Qualification A minimum of a high school diploma or GED is required. Advanced education above high school is preferred.
Essential Responsibilities 1. Team Leadership and Development: Develop and implement training programs to enhance player performance. Provide mentorship and support to players, fostering their physical, mental, and emotional development. Evaluate player performance and provide constructive feedback. 2. Game Strategy and Planning: Analyze opponents and develop game plans and strategies to maximize team performance. Make real-time decisions during games to adapt strategies as needed. Lead meetings/practices when asked by the head coach to review performance and plan future games. Assist with the selection of players Ensure all players adhere to team rules, policies, and standards. 4. Communication and Collaboration: Maintain open lines of communication with players, coaching staff, and families. Foster a positive and inclusive team culture. 5. Performance Monitoring: Track and analyze team and player performance metrics. Implement changes and improvements based on performance statistics. 6. Administrative Duties: Assist in managing the team's schedule, including practices, games, and travel. Ensure compliance with DIAA rules and regulations.
Professional Responsibilities Able to establish and maintain collaborative working relationships. Willingness to accept guidance and suggestions. Able to communicate effectively both orally and in writing. Able to work independently and exercise initiative. Able to maintain confidentiality. Continue professional growth and development through opportunities provided by CHSD and DIAA. Able to work with other cultures represented in the community.
Competencies: Success in this position requires the following knowledge, skills, and abilities 1. Knowledge Must have knowledge of DIAA rules and regulations. Must complete training regarding Title IX and Athletics. Must complete Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) and Concussion Training every two years. Must be familiar with Cape Henlopen School District’s Policy 118 (Prohibited Actions of Employees) & Policy 551 (Electronic Communication & Social Media Policy). Equity initiatives & resources that promote a culture of diversity and inclusion embracing differences as a strategic opportunity towards leading the District’s efforts to build a culture of equality and embodiment for all students, families, employees and the community. Must be aware of relevant policies and procedures mandated by the State of DE for coaches and employees such as Criminal Background Checks, Child Protection Registry Forms & TB Tests. Must have experience and a detailed knowledge of online systems such as Recruiting and Hiring and Google Docs. Must possess online, digital skills to maintain records and to complete evaluations of coaching staff. 2. Skills Active listening –Giving full attention to what others are saying and taking the time to understand what is being said. Social perceptiveness – Being aware of others’ reactions and understanding why they react as they do. Coordination & Flexibility – Adjusting actions in relation to others’ actions, and an openness to accepting new assignments and duties. Communicating – Providing information to supervisors, co-workers, and subordinates by telephone, in written form, e-mail, or in person, and communicating with the public. Planning and organization – Coordinating schedules in a timely and efficient manner. 3. Abilities Interpersonal Relationships – Developing constructive and cooperative working relationships with students and staff, and maintaining them over time. Oral comprehension – Able to listen and understand information and ideas presented through spoken words and sentences. The foregoing statements are intended to describe the general purpose and responsibilities assigned to this job and are not an exhaustive list of all duties, responsibilities and competencies that may be required.
Must have completed and submitted online application prior to closing date. Your application is not submitted until you click on the submit button and get verification.

Job.JobDescription:
GULF BREEZE HIGH SCHOOL is looking for a Football Full-Time to join our program. Apply now.
The Assistant Varsity Football Coach (Spring) at GULF BREEZE HIGH SCHOOL is a comprehensive coaching assignment where teaching, organization, and care for students come together in a single role. The coach is expected to be prepared, professional, and consistent, creating a framework in which athletes understand what is expected of them and feel supported in meeting those expectations. The program thrives when the coach brings a balanced focus on performance, safety, and personal development.
During practices, the Assistant Varsity Football Coach (Spring) manages time efficiently, moving athletes through warm‑ups, skill stations, team concepts, and competitive play. The coach provides instruction and feedback that is specific and actionable, helping athletes understand not just what to do, but why it matters. Attention is given to fundamentals, conditioning appropriate to the sport and season, and mental preparation for competition.
The position carries a strong responsibility for modeling behavior. At GULF BREEZE HIGH SCHOOL, coaches are role models whether they are on the sideline, traveling with the team, or speaking with families. The Assistant Varsity Football Coach (Spring) demonstrates composure, fairness, and respect, even in emotionally charged situations. Athletes observe and imitate these behaviors, so the coach’s example has a direct impact on team culture.
The role also involves building strong connections with the broader school community. The coach collaborates with staff members, responds to communication from parents and guardians, and participates in department or school events as appropriate. Being visible and approachable helps families feel confident that their children are in a safe, supportive program.
Safety and compliance requirements form an important foundation. The Assistant Varsity Football Coach (Spring) follows all district and league regulations, maintains necessary certifications, and ensures that facilities and equipment are used appropriately. Emergency procedures are understood and practiced so that the team is prepared if an unexpected situation occurs.
This position is well suited to coaches who are detail‑oriented, who enjoy planning and facilitating engaging practices, and who care about the long‑term lessons students take away from their time in athletics. Serving as the Assistant Varsity Football Coach (Spring) at GULF BREEZE HIGH SCHOOL allows a coach to influence not only competitive outcomes, but also the habits and character of the next generation.
Job.Location: GULF BREEZE HIGH SCHOOL - Gulf Breeze, Florida
Job.CompanyDescription: Santa Rosa County School District coaching jobs
